Verdict accuracy — PR bench only

Measures how often gardener's PR verdict matches the actual maintainer decision. Only scored on PRs with resolved outcomes (merged or maintainer-rejected). Issues are excluded — they lack a clear correct/wrong signal.

How accuracy is calculated

Gardener saidMerged cleanlyMerged after revisionMaintainer rejected
ALIGNEDCorrectPartialWrong
NEEDS_REVIEWWrongCorrectCorrect
CONFLICTWrongCorrectCorrect

Excluded: Author-withdrawn PRs (gardener can't predict humans leaving) and pending PRs (no outcome yet).

Formula: (correct + 0.5 × partial) / scorable × 100

How engagement is detected

A reply is classified as engaging with gardener if it matches one or more signals:

  • mentions-gardener — mentions "gardener", "repo-gardener", "context tree", etc.
  • references-verdict — references verdict terms (ALIGNED, NEEDS_REVIEW, severity, etc.)
  • quotes-gardener — markdown blockquote containing gardener text
  • addresses-review — phrases like "addressed findings", "response to gardener", "update pushed"

All gardener comments (335)

PR #3850 fix(hermes): spawn Hermes with --yolo by default
NEW_TERRITORY low open PENDING by @vishnukool · 2026-04-17T01:11:28Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: 2f4faf6

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Hermes-local adapter | Wraps hermesExecute in registry.ts to always prepend --yolo to extraArgs; idempotent | adapters/NODE.md lists sub-domains (claude-local, codex-local, cursor-local, gemini-local, openclaw-gateway, opencode-local, pi-local) but not hermes-local — tree is entirely silent on this adapter | 🆕 New |
| Agent-side dangerous-command bypass | --yolo is Hermes's own interactive safety prompt; skipping it when running as non-interactive subprocess | product/governance/NODE.md: "Governance rules are control-plane invariants enforced at the server, not at the adapter or agent level." Hermes's --yolo is agent-internal, not a Paperclip approval gate — server-enforced board/issue approvals are untouched | ✅ Aligned |
| Opinionated adapter wiring | Paperclip owns the --yolo policy rather than delegating to config | product/NODE.md decision #5: "Unopinionated about agent implementation" but also "the adapter defines the agent's inner workings" — policy lives in the adapter module, consistent with decision #5 | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — adapter sub-domain list (hermes-local absent)
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — governance rules enforced server-side, not at adapter layer
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Decision #5 (unopinionated about agent implementation)

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The behavior itself is fine — Hermes runs as a non-interactive subprocess so approval prompts would always time out. --yolo bypasses Hermes's internal safety prompt, not Paperclip's server-enforced approvals. But the tree doesn't currently document hermes-local as a sub-domain at all (sibling issue paperclipai/paperclip#3833 noticed the same gap), so there's no place to record this policy decision or future hermes-specific conventions.

Suggested path forward: Land the fix — it's a blocker for Hermes being usable at all and aligns with how sibling adapters handle their own interactive-prompt equivalents. Separately, add a node at adapters/hermes-local/NODE.md on the tree that captures: (1) Paperclip integrates with Hermes via the upstream hermes-paperclip-adapter package, (2) non-interactive execution means Paperclip prepends --yolo to every run, (3) any other hermes-specific conventions (session management capability, skill sync support). This gives future changes a documented home and closes the gap #3833 flagged.

---

<sub>Reviewed commit: <code>2f4faf6</code> · Tree snapshot: <code>ddfe6b6</code> · Commands: <code>@gardener re-review</code> · <code>@gardener pause</code> · <code>@gardener ignore</code></sub>

<sub>🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.</sub>

Issue #3849 Title: DELETE /api/agents/:id and DELETE /api/companies/:id fail with FK violation on cost_events
NEW_TERRITORY low open by @kr8vkaos · 2026-04-17T00:49:31Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-----------|---------------|-----|
| Hard-DELETE path for agent/company | DELETE /api/agents/:id / DELETE /api/companies/:id 500s on FK from cost_events.heartbeat_run_id → heartbeat_runs | Tree documents terminal statuses — agents: terminated (irreversible, board-only); companies: archived (read-only historical) — as the product-level lifecycle endpoints. Tree is silent on whether/when hard-delete endpoints should exist or on FK cleanup ordering across heartbeat_runs/cost_events. | 🆕 New |
| Reporter's workaround (PATCH status=terminated + POST /archive) | Exactly the documented lifecycle ("terminate agents", "archived companies are read-only historical records") | Matches product/agent-model and product/company-model decisions | ✅ Aligned |
| Preserving cost_events on entity removal | FK prevents losing financial records when their parent run/agent is removed | product/governance — Cost Tracking + "Auditable Everything" (every mutation logged; cost_events/finance_events are financial tracking) | ❓ Partial — preserving audit/cost history is a documented invariant; hard-delete cleanup order must be reconciled with it |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Agent statuses; terminated is irreversible (board only)
- [product/company-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/company-model/NODE.md) — Company lifecycle: active / paused / archived (read-only historical)
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Cost tracking at every level; "Auditable Everything"; cost_events/finance_events are part of the audit contract
- [engineering/database/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/database/NODE.md) — cost_events / finance_events under Operational / financial tracking; heartbeat_runs under Execution Infrastructure
- [engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — Event-sourced run history (heartbeat_run_events immutable per state transition)

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The FK-ordering fix itself (delete cost_events rows, or null the FK, before removing heartbeat_runs) is pure execution detail and not a tree concern. What is tree-adjacent: the tree documents terminated + archived as the product-level terminal states and treats cost_events/activity_log as load-bearing audit/finance records. A hard-DELETE path that cascades and removes cost_events would erase auditable financial history — which contradicts "Auditable Everything" and the "financial tracking" framing. The reporter's workaround (PATCH to terminated, POST /archive) is exactly the documented product lifecycle.

Suggested path forward:
- Fix the 500 in execution (correct delete order) so the endpoint stops failing ungracefully.
- Decide the product question before adding cascade deletes that would remove cost_events / heartbeat_runs: should DELETE /api/agents/:id and DELETE /api/companies/:id exist as hard-delete endpoints at all, or should they be aliases for terminate/archive? Given the documented irreversibility of terminated and the read-only historical contract of archived, the safest default is: hard-delete only via an explicit, audited "purge" path (board-only, separate endpoint), and have the generic DELETE route resolve to terminate/archive.
- If maintainers add a purge path, a short note in product/governance/ or product/company-model/ covering which tables may be physically removed vs. retained (audit records, cost_events, finance_events, activity_log) would lock the invariant in the tree.

---

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Per-agent heartbeat option to skip timer wakes when no actionable assignments | New skipIfNoActionableAssignments flag on heartbeat policy; on source === "timer", count assignee's issues in todo/backlog; if 0, log heartbeat.skipIfNoActionableAssignments, bump lastHeartbeatAt, return null — event-triggered wakes unaffected | Agent Model NODE — Heartbeat Protocol: "Paperclip controls when to fire (schedule/frequency), how to fire, and what context to include." This PR adds a per-agent skip predicate that sits in the "when" layer without changing the protocol shape. | ✅ Aligned |
| Budget / token-burn intent | Hint text: "Prevents token burn on blocked or stalled work." Only applies to timer wakes; mentions, assignments, on-demand wakes all bypass it. | Governance NODE — Budget Hard-Stops: "hidden token burn is not [allowed]." Skipping idle timer wakes when there's nothing to do aligns with the safe-autonomy principle. | ✅ Aligned |
| UI surface | New ToggleField on AgentConfigForm with descriptive hint covering which statuses count and which wake types are unaffected | Frontend NODE — agent config form lives in AgentConfigForm; React Query + centralized API patterns unchanged | ✅ Aligned |
| Single-assignee scoping | Count query filters by eq(issues.assigneeAgentId, agentId) + in ["todo", "backlog"] | Task-system NODE — Single-Assignee Model; Issue Status Workflow fixed enum | ✅ Aligned |

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Heartbeat Protocol; Context Delivery Spectrum.
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Budget Hard-Stops; "hidden token burn is not allowed."
- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Issue Status Workflow (backlog, todo, in_progress, in_review, done, blocked, cancelled); Single-Assignee Model.
- [engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — Run lifecycle state machine; scheduling vs execution boundary.

</details>

No concerns. This is a narrow, per-agent skip predicate on timer-triggered heartbeats that fits the "Paperclip controls when to fire" clause of the Heartbeat Protocol and the "no hidden token burn" side of Budget Hard-Stops. Event-driven wakes (assignment, on-demand, mention) remain unaffected, so the agent still responds immediately to new work — the skip only collapses idle polling.

PR #3842 feat: cost-monitoring reconciliation L1+L2+L3 (subscription billing fix)
NEW_TERRITORY medium open PENDING by @lathonez · 2026-04-17T00:01:04Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: 068eaef3

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Fix spentMonthlyCents = 0 for Claude subscription users (L1) | normalizeBilledCostCents for subscription_included billing type now computes equivalent API cost from token counts × pricing table instead of returning 0; cacheCreationInputTokens propagated through UsageSummary, cost_events, parse pipeline, and the claude-local adapter | Governance NODE — Cost Tracking at Every Level: "Fully-instrumented agents report token/API usage. Costs are tracked per Agent, per task, per project, and per Company." Budget Hard-Stops rely on real cost signals; returning 0 silently broke this for subscription users. | ✅ Aligned |
| Per-agent real-time rollup (L2) | Uses existing costs.createEvent to update agents.spentMonthlyCents on each run; no new cron | Governance NODE — Cost rollups are read-time aggregate queries for V1. Per-agent materialization via the cost-event path fits the monthly-window model. | ✅ Aligned |
| Anthropic Admin API daily reconciliation (L3) | New billing_reconciliation table (migration 0057); billing-reconciliation service queries the Anthropic usage report, aggregates by company, writes paperclip_cents / anthropic_cents / drift_pct; warns on drift > 10%; scheduled 03:05 UTC daily with in-process guard; safe no-op without ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_API_KEY | Governance NODE: Auditable Everything, "nothing happens silently". A drift-reporting layer is new but directly in service of the cost-tracking + auditability decisions. | 🆕 New |
| Company-level (not per-agent) reconciliation — migration 0058 flips agent_id nullable and moves uniqueness to (date, company_id) | Documented in-code: "per-agent reconciliation against the Anthropic usage report is deferred until metadata.user_id tagging is feasible in the adapter layer" | Adapter claude-local NODE: no current guidance on whether the CLI-driven adapter can inject metadata.user_id. The deferred-per-agent note is a reasonable constraint but worth capturing. | 🆕 New |
| Anthropic model pricing table + Opus fallback | ANTHROPIC_MODEL_PRICING in packages/shared/pricing.ts with exact + prefix match; unknown models fall back to Opus rates (conservative against under-counting); cache-read = 10% input, cache-write = 125% input | Tree has no pricing-table decision today; the fallback policy (conservative) and multipliers are product decisions that will affect budget hard-stops. | 🆕 New |
| --exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections in claude-local execute | Non-resume runs now pass this flag to stabilize the system prompt across heartbeats, so Anthropic caches it and input-token cost drops on repeat wakes | Adapter claude-local NODE — Stream-json output format over plain text; Billing: reports usage and cost from Claude's stream-json output. Cache-optimizing the system prompt is an execution-layer refinement consistent with "adapters own execution mechanics" (heartbeat-run-orchestration NODE). | ✅ Aligned |
| Startup scheduling (03:05 UTC, 5-min check interval, per-company loop) | New setInterval in server/src/index.ts with in-flight + date guards | Tree does not yet describe the scheduling model for governance-support cron jobs. Prior jobs live in heartbeat / backup paths. | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Cost Tracking at Every Level; Budget Hard-Stops; Auditable Everything; "nothing happens silently."
- [product/governance/server-enforced-approvals-and-budget-stops.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/server-enforced-approvals-and-budget-stops.md) — Server-side enforcement rationale.
- [adapters/claude-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/claude-local/NODE.md) — Stream-json, billing extraction from adapter output.
- [adapters/claude-local/stream-json-and-headless-permissions.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/claude-local/stream-json-and-headless-permissions.md) — Structured stream output + headless permissions.
- [engineering/database/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/database/NODE.md) — Schema / migration discipline.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The root-cause fix (subscription-billed runs silently reporting costCents = 0, which meant spentMonthlyCents stayed at 0 and Budget Hard-Stops never engaged) is a direct bug against Cost Tracking at Every Level. Fixing it is squarely aligned — but the PR also lands a chunk of new product surface (pricing tables, Opus-fallback policy, org-level drift reconciliation against Anthropic's Admin API, a new daily cron) whose policies (not code) the tree doesn't yet describe. Future reviewers will want to know, e.g., why per-agent reconciliation is deferred, whether drift > 10% is an alert threshold or an automation trigger, and where the model-pricing table lives in the change-control flow.

Suggested path forward:
1. Add a decision record under [product/governance/](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) (e.g. cost-monitoring-reconciliation.md) covering: three tiers (per-run capture, per-agent real-time rollup, org-level daily reconciliation), the known gap (per-agent Anthropic attribution deferred until metadata.user_id tagging lands), the drift-alert policy (warn at > 10%), and how this relates to Budget Hard-Stops (today: informational only; reconciliation does not feed back into budget enforcement).
2. Add a short note in [adapters/claude-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/claude-local/NODE.md) on the subscription-billing code path — specifically that normalizeBilledCostCents derives a synthetic cost from token counts × pricing table for subscription_included billing, and that the pricing table is versioned in packages/shared/pricing.ts with an Opus-rate fallback for unknown models.
3. Cross-reference from [adapters/claude-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/claude-local/NODE.md) that --exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections is used on non-resume runs to enable system-prompt caching and reduce input-token cost; this is a Claude-CLI-specific knob future adapter work should be aware of.
4. Consider documenting the scheduling approach (in-process setInterval + UTC-hour check) as a pattern under [engineering/backend/](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md), since this is the second non-heartbeat cron job in the server (after DB backups) and future jobs will want a shared pattern.
5. Once (1)–(3) land, this moves to ALIGNED.

Severity medium: correctness-critical fix (subscription-billed agents had silent cost tracking, which undermines Budget Hard-Stops), combined with new policy surface (pricing table, drift threshold, org-level reconciliation, deferred per-agent attribution) that should be written down before the next cost/billing PR cites this one as precedent.

---

PR #3840 feat: template gallery
NEW_TERRITORY medium open PENDING by @bdervishi · 2026-04-16T23:59:49Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: 43f35e07

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Template registry + one-click install of pre-configured companies | docs/templates/registry.json (four curated companies pointing at paperclipai/companies); Zod-validated shape in packages/shared/template-types.ts; server templateRegistry with in-memory cache; GET /api/templates/companies, POST /api/templates/companies/install, POST /api/templates/refresh; TemplatesPage + CompanyCard + Playwright smoke | Company Model NODE — Company Portability: "Exportable company configs enable sharing templates ('pre-built marketing agency'), version-controlling org structure, and duplicating/forking companies." Also: import supports creating new companies, collision strategies, dry-run preview. | ❓ Partial |
| Install path reuses the portability bundle contract | templatesRouter builds a { source: { type: "github", url }, target: { mode: "new", newCompanyName }, include: [...], collision: "skip" } payload and delegates to portability.importBundle(...) | Company Model NODE anticipates import via the portable package contract (markdown-first, .paperclip.yaml sidecar, collision strategies). Reusing the portability adapter preserves that contract. | ✅ Aligned |
| Authorization model for the templates routes | requireBoard on list + install; requireAdmin (instance admin) on refresh | Governance NODE — Board Powers (Always Available): board has full project management access; Unified API, Different Authorization: one API surface with actor-scoped checks. Gating templates install behind board fits the board-power model. | ✅ Aligned |
| Registry source-of-truth and trust boundary | Registry JSON lives in-repo, published with the server; install fetches template bundles from arbitrary GitHub URLs via the portability service | Tree is silent on where curated templates live, who can propose or refresh them, and the trust boundary when pulling arbitrary GitHub content into a Paperclip instance. | 🆕 New |
| Cache + refresh lifecycle | In-memory cache; invalidate() exposed via admin-only refresh endpoint; no server-startup refresh policy documented | Tree has no decision on template-registry staleness or refresh cadence. | 🆕 New |
| UI shell surface | New /templates route; nav link added to Sidebar Company section | Frontend NODE enumerates pages but does not yet include a Templates page; this is a visible product surface. | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/company-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/company-model/NODE.md) — Company Portability, import/export, template export vs snapshot export.
- [product/company-model/company-is-the-top-level-boundary.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/company-model/company-is-the-top-level-boundary.md) — Company as the primary boundary.
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board Powers; Unified API, Different Authorization.
- [engineering/frontend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — Page catalog; no Templates page yet.
- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — Route groups; no /templates grouping yet.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The intent is squarely aligned with the tree: Company Portability explicitly calls out "sharing templates" and import/export as a V1 goal, and the install path reuses the portability bundle contract rather than inventing a parallel one. Where the tree is silent:

1. Registry governance — where does the curated list live, who adds to it, what's the trust boundary for pulling bundles from arbitrary GitHub URLs (today: https://github.com/paperclipai/companies/tree/main/...)?
2. Refresh lifecycle — in-memory cache with a board-admin refresh endpoint is a reasonable default, but future PRs will want to know whether a startup warmup, a periodic refresh, or an external CDN-backed registry is on the roadmap.
3. Page catalog — /templates is a new top-level route and nav entry; the frontend page list should mention it so other contributors don't miss it.

Suggested path forward:
1. Add a decision record under [product/company-model/](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/company-model/NODE.md) (e.g. template-gallery.md) describing: the registry shape and source of truth (docs/templates/registry.json pointing at paperclipai/companies), authorization (board to list/install, instance admin to refresh), install semantics (reuses the portability importBundle with collision: "skip" and target.mode: "new"), and the known limitation around metadata.user_id tagging that the cost-monitoring work in #3842 also references.
2. Cross-reference from [engineering/frontend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) Page Structure and [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) Route groups to mention /templates.
3. Consider the trust boundary explicitly: today registry.json is co-maintained with the server binary, but tpl.url is a runtime-fetched GitHub URL. Decide whether the portability service does (or should) pin commits, verify signatures, or otherwise bound what the server can fetch.
4. Once these are captured, this moves to ALIGNED.

Severity medium because this adds a visible product surface (install flow, new route, new admin endpoint) with a new trust/source-of-truth boundary that other contributors will want documented before extending.

| Zen reading mode on IssueDetail | New zenMode state on issue detail page (via sidebar collapse) that hides the chat panel and widens the main column | No tree node currently describes IssueDetail layout modes or focused-reading UX | 🆕 New |
| Plugin-slot gating when collapsed | PluginSlotOutlet slots (sidebar, sidebarPanel) are hidden when sidebar is in icon-only mode | Plugins NODE: plugin UI integration via slots; collapse-mode interaction with plugin slots is a new constraint | 🆕 New |
| State persistence | SidebarContext manages collapsed state; it is not documented whether this persists across reloads, per-user, per-device, or per-company | Frontend NODE: No global state library. <details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [engineering/frontend/api-layer-and-react-query-over-global-state.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/api-layer-and-react-query-over-global-state.md) — "React context for UI state" side of the decision.
- [plugins/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Plugin slots; their interaction with layout modes is not yet covered.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree is silent on sidebar layout modes today — only the sidebar's structural composition (Sidebar, InstanceSidebar, SidebarAgents, SidebarProjects, SidebarNavItem, SidebarSection) is mentioned. This PR introduces a user-visible behavior (icon-only mode, zen reading) and a cross-cutting SidebarContext that downstream components (including plugin slots) will need to be aware of. That's worth capturing so the next contributor touching a sidebar entry knows to handle both modes and tooltip-wrap their icon.

Suggested path forward:
1. Add a short node under [engineering/frontend/](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) (e.g. sidebar-collapse-and-zen-mode/NODE.md, or a decision record on the frontend NODE) describing: (a) the two sidebar modes (w-60 / w-12), (b) what components must handle the collapsed case (icon + tooltip), (c) how plugin slots and IssueDetail's zen mode interact with sidebar state, (d) whether collapsed state persists (and how — session, localStorage, per-user).
2. Decide whether collapsed-state persistence should move to a server-backed user preference eventually (so the mode survives across devices) — today it lives in React context only.
3. Once captured, future sidebar additions (e.g. from plugin authors) can follow a documented contract instead of reading this PR as precedent.

Severity low because this is UI-layer UX with no governance, data-isolation, or adapter-contract implications; the tree gap is documentation-only.

| Scoping | Hook is scoped by companyId; resume targets status === "paused"; pause excludes terminated, already-paused, pending_approval | Company Model NODE — Company-scoping is enforced at every entity fetch and mutation; Agent Model Agent Statuses (terminated is irreversible) | ✅ Aligned |

Issue #3845 bug: GET /skills returns 500 due to duplicate key race in upsertImportedSkills
NEW_TERRITORY low open by @wfrank2509 · 2026-04-16T23:54:28Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-----------|---------------|-----|
| Duplicate-key race in upsertImportedSkills during GET /skills | Manual check-then-insert TOCTOU across workers; fix switches INSERT to INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE on (company_id, key) | Tree captures company-scoped isolation, Drizzle-in-services, pluggable company_skills inventory — no recorded decision on upsert idempotency for bundled skills | 🆕 New |
| Read-only endpoint doing writes | GET /skills triggers ensureSkillInventoryCurrent → ensureBundledSkills on every request | Tree silent on lazy-refresh-on-read vs scheduled refresh for bundled skills | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [engineering/database/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/database/NODE.md) — Drizzle queries live in services; no ORM abstraction layer
- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — Route/service split; no DI framework, explicit deps
- [engineering/database/company-scoped-isolation.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/database/company-scoped-isolation.md) — Company-scoped isolation boundary

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This is an execution-level correctness fix. ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE targeted on (company_id, key) is the canonical Postgres remedy for the described TOCTOU and is consistent with the tree's company-scoped isolation boundary (the conflict target preserves the isolation invariant).

Suggested path forward: Land the upsert fix. Two secondary notes worth considering but not required for this issue:
- The in-process skillInventoryRefreshPromises only helps within one Node.js process — after this fix, multi-worker concurrency is safe, but the "read endpoint does writes" pattern still couples request latency to sync work. A future tree/product decision could address whether bundled-skill refresh should be scheduled/lazy-once-per-process rather than per-request.
- If the service does similar check-then-insert patterns elsewhere (e.g. plugin inventory, routine seeds), they likely share the same TOCTOU shape.

No tree update required for the fix itself.

---

Issue #3841 bug: UTF-8 encoding error when listing issues with multi-byte characters near description truncation boundary
NEW_TERRITORY low open by @wfrank2509 · 2026-04-16T23:54:24Z → gardener comment
(2 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Update — reporter confirms duplicate of #3829 + second affected row

@wfrank2509 has flagged this as a duplicate of #3829 ("heartbeat-runs endpoint 500s when result_json->>'result' has multibyte UTF-8 at byte-500 boundary (SQL_ASCII cluster + left(text, 500))") and reported that a second issue row hit the same failure — another issues.description contained an em dash (—, bytes e2 80 94) starting at byte 1200, producing the same invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8": 0xe2 error. The row was patched via replace(description, '—', ' - '), confirming the bug isn't a single-row fluke: any description whose growth lands a multi-byte UTF-8 character exactly on the 1200-byte truncation boundary will trip the same 500.

This is the same root cause as #3829 — byte-indexed truncation on a SQL_ASCII cluster splitting a multi-byte UTF-8 codepoint — applied to a different column (issues.description via substring(description FROM 1 FOR 1200)). The proposed server-side fix (left(convert_from(convert_to(description, 'SQL_ASCII'), 'UTF8'), 1200)) is the structural close.

### Context fit (unchanged)

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-----------|---------------|-----|
| UTF-8 truncation of issue descriptions in GET /issues | Byte-indexed substring(description FROM 1 FOR 1200) splits multi-byte UTF-8 mid-sequence; fix uses left(convert_from(convert_to(...,'SQL_ASCII'),'UTF8'), N) to count code points | Tree covers Drizzle-in-services, query-level multi-company isolation, route/service split — no guidance on SQL truncation of text columns | 🆕 New |
| Duplicate of #3829 | Same root cause family (byte-vs-codepoint slicing on SQL_ASCII cluster), different column | Tree is silent on shared truncation helpers across services | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — Route/service split; Drizzle queries live in services
- [engineering/database/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/database/NODE.md) — Drizzle + Postgres; schema-as-code; no raw SQL layer

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This remains a correctness bug in the issues service SQL — execution-level detail the tree intentionally does not cover. No product/architecture decision is at stake; truncating on code-point boundaries is straightforwardly correct. With a second affected row confirmed and #3829 already triaged as the same root cause on heartbeat_runs.result, the bug class is real and recurring but still not a tree-level concern.

Suggested path forward:
- Close as duplicate of #3829, or keep open and link #3829 as the canonical fix tracker — maintainer's call; either works because the fix shape is identical.
- Audit the service layer for other list-select columns that use byte-based substring or left(text, N); if there's more than one, a small shared "truncate to N codepoints" helper in the service layer prevents this reappearing when a future column is added. No tree update required.

---

NEW_TERRITORY low open by @UltimYT4445 · 2026-04-16T23:54:08Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-----------|---------------|-----|
| Polymarket trading bot / ULT-16 task | Build bot core + paper trading harness + dashboard for 5-min crypto prediction markets | Paperclip is infrastructure for orchestrating AI companies; it is not an agent runtime or a delivery infrastructure manager | 🆕 New |
| Target runtime | Dedicated bot on port 8081, SQLite, Polymarket CLOB client | Tree: "Paperclip never runs agent code directly. Agents run wherever they run (local processes, cloud services…) and report into the control plane via adapters" | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Control plane, not execution plane; anti-requirements explicitly exclude agent runtime and delivery infrastructure
- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Tasks are the communication channel; all work traces back to a company goal

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This issue appears to be a downstream product task (a Polymarket bot) being tracked inside a Paperclip-managed company, not a request to modify Paperclip itself. The tree is silent on Polymarket, trading, or financial-market integrations because Paperclip is the orchestration layer — the agent workloads it schedules are out of scope.

Suggested path forward: Keep the bot implementation in its own repo/workspace (the body already references /home/ubuntu/polymarket-bot/). Use Paperclip's task hierarchy (Initiative → Project → Milestones → Issues → Sub-issues) to track the ULT-16/17/18 chain, and let an agent adapter execute the actual bot logic. If during the build you identify something missing from the Paperclip control plane (e.g., a needed capability for scheduled market scans, budget policy around paper-trade volume, or an adapter need), open a separate issue scoped to that Paperclip gap.

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Skill import inventory | Fixes the root-directory case in readLocalSkillImports() so importing a path already at the skill root preserves scripts + support files instead of collapsing to markdown_only; adds regression test covering SKILL.md, README.md, references/, scripts/ | Skills are package-like assets (SKILL.md + scripts + references + assets) that adapter skill-injection relies on; losing files silently would degrade the injected capability surface | ✅ Aligned |
| Test pattern | Embedded-Postgres service regression test at server/src/__tests__/company-skills-service.test.ts | infrastructure/testing/NODE.md: Vitest + embedded-Postgres is the unit-test pattern | ✅ Aligned |

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

</details>

No concerns. Narrow bug fix that restores the skill-as-package semantics every skill-aware adapter (claude-local, codex-local, cursor-local, gemini-local, opencode-local, pi-local) depends on. The prefix fix is correct: path.posix.dirname("SKILL.md") returns ".", and walkLocalFiles() emits unprefixed relative paths, so the old ./ prefix filter excluded everything.

PR #3834 fix: coalesce stale queued execution locks
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open PENDING by @mystickcal · 2026-04-16T19:48:25Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: 480d75df

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Stale queued execution-run lock cancellation during checkout / ownership assertion | cancelStaleQueuedExecutionRun auto-cancels a queued heartbeat run older than 15 min (never started, never finished) when the original assignee presents a newer actorRunId, then clears the issue's execution lock fields so the same assignee can proceed | Task-system NODE: "No Automatic Recovery... Stale tasks are surfaced through dashboards. Recovery is manual/explicit." Heartbeat-run-orchestration NODE: failure hardening covers duplicate runs, adapter timeout outliving, race conditions — but not stale-queued-run cancellation thresholds. | ❓ Partial |
| Single-assignee invariant preservation | Repair path fires only when current.assigneeAgentId === actorAgentId; no cross-agent reassignment | Task-system NODE: Single-Assignee Model + Atomic Task Checkout | ✅ Aligned |
| 15-minute staleness threshold (STALE_QUEUED_EXECUTION_RUN_MS) | Fresh constant in the issues service | Tree does not document the threshold for a queued heartbeat run being safe to auto-cancel | 🆕 New |
| assertCheckoutOwner accepts checkoutRunId == null | After stale-lock clearing, an assignee with a valid actorRunId asserts ownership even though checkoutRunId is null | Task-system: 409-conflict semantics target other agent holding the lock; allowing a null-lock ownership assertion by the assignee is a refinement | ❓ Partial |
| New: reap runs whose tracked child process has already exited (reapOrphanedRuns) | Previously skipped any run id in runningProcesses; now detects exitCode !== null or dead PID and deletes the stale tracking map entry so reaper logic can proceed | Heartbeat-run-orchestration NODE: explicit failure hardening against "adapter processes that outlive their timeout window" — this is the symmetric case (tracking outlives the process) | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Atomic Task Checkout, Single-Assignee Model, No Automatic Recovery.
- [engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — Run lifecycle, failure hardening separation between adapter failures and agent failures.
- [product/task-system/auto-checkout/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/auto-checkout/NODE.md) — Scoped-wake auto-checkout; same 409 lock semantics.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The original concern remains: the tree documents the intent (no auto-reassignment of tasks) but not the boundary (auto-clearing of orphaned heartbeat-run locks when the assignee tries to advance their own work is allowed and bounded). The new third commit — detecting when a tracked child process has already exited and removing it from runningProcesses so reapOrphanedRuns can then process it — directly strengthens the "failure hardening" section of the heartbeat-run-orchestration node. It's a sound extension of the documented pattern: the server is explicitly permitted to reconcile its runtime-process map against reality, and that's what this commit does. The stale-queued-run cancellation (original scope) is still the piece that should be written down because it introduces a new numeric threshold (15 min) and a new trigger condition (assignee presents a fresher actorRunId).

Suggested path forward:
1. Add a decision to [engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) describing stale queued-run coalescing: run is queued, never-started, unchanged for ≥15 min; trigger is the original assignee presenting a newer actorRunId; outcome is status=cancelled, errorCode=stale_queued_lock with an audit-trail error field citing the superseding run.
2. While there, add a short note on orphaned-tracking recovery: when runningProcesses.get(runId).child.exitCode !== null (or PID is dead), the map entry is removed so the standard reaper path can re-drive it. This is the non-controversial mirror of "process outlives its tracking" and fits the existing failure-hardening language.
3. Cross-reference from [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md)'s "No Automatic Recovery" clause: the invariant is about task assignment, not heartbeat-run lock bookkeeping.
4. Consider whether the 15-minute threshold belongs alongside (or should be derived from) the heartbeat timeout — today it's an independent constant that could drift.
5. Once (1)–(3) are captured, this moves to ALIGNED.

Severity stays medium: the repaired paths are user-visible (an assignee who previously saw a stuck issue now silently advances), the thresholds are not documented, and the new reaper-map commit introduces another spot where the server silently reconciles state.

---

PR #3825 fix(routines): recover from stale routine execution lock conflicts
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open PENDING by @DeadlySilent · 2026-04-16T19:48:07Z → gardener comment 1 engaging 1 other
(2 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: b131b0ff

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Stale routine execution-lock repair (original scope) | On 23505 / issues_open_routine_execution_uq during routine dispatch, clear executionRunId when the referenced heartbeat run is terminal and retry issueSvc.create | Task-system NODE: "No Automatic Recovery... Stale tasks are surfaced through dashboards. Recovery is manual/explicit." Tree does not yet distinguish lock bookkeeping from task reassignment. | ❓ Partial |
| New partial unique index issues_open_routine_origin_uq (migration 0057) | Adds a second conflict surface: (company_id, origin_kind, origin_id) for open routine-execution issues where hidden_at IS NULL. Complements the existing issues_open_routine_execution_uq. | Database NODE / Routines NODE: duplicate-open-routine-issue prevention. Decision to prevent duplicates via DB uniqueness is consistent with existing patterns. | ✅ Aligned |
| New assertCanCreateIssues route guard + legacy canCreateIssues/canCreateAgents permission read | Blocks non-CEO, non-board agents from creating issues unless agent.permissions.canCreateIssues \|\| canCreateAgents is set. Board actors pass through unchanged. | Governance NODE: "Unified API, Different Authorization... agent API keys have scoped access." Shared API + actor-scoped authorization decision record. The new gate aligns with this model. | ✅ Aligned |
| Permission-shape — Record<string, unknown> field named permissions on agents | Inline permission key enumeration (canCreateIssues, canCreateAgents) lives only in a route-local helper. Tree does not yet document the agent-permissions schema or how it interacts with role-based access (role === "ceo" bypass). | Agent-model / Governance: role-based agent permissions are not described in the tree beyond CEO/role primitives. | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Atomic Task Checkout, No Automatic Recovery, Single-Assignee Model.
- [product/routines/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/routines/NODE.md) — Routine concurrency policies; coalesce/skip/enqueue semantics.
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Unified API, Different Authorization; Governance Enforced at Server Layer.
- [engineering/backend/shared-api-and-actor-scoped-authorization.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/shared-api-and-actor-scoped-authorization.md) — Route-level actor checks over separate namespaces.
- [engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — Failure hardening: duplicate runs, race conditions, adapter timeout outliving.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The prior review's core concern — the tree doesn't distinguish "clear a stale executionRunId lock when its run is terminal" (lock bookkeeping) from "auto-reassign a crashed task to a new agent" (task recovery) — is still open. Since the last pass, two new changes landed: (1) a second partial unique index that further constrains open routine-execution issues, which is sound and aligned with the existing uniqueness pattern; and (2) a route-level permission gate that blocks non-CEO agents from creating issues unless their permissions.canCreateIssues or permissions.canCreateAgents is set. The gate itself is consistent with the Unified API + Actor-Scoped Authorization decision, but the shape of the agent.permissions record (which keys exist, how they compose with role) is not documented in the tree — and reviewers touching other agent-write routes will need that shape to apply the same pattern consistently.

Suggested path forward:
1. Still outstanding from the first review: add a note to [product/routines/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/routines/NODE.md) and [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) clarifying that auto-clearing a terminal-run executionRunId lock is lock bookkeeping, not task reassignment.
2. New this pass: add a short decision to [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) (or a new sibling node) enumerating the agent.permissions keys Paperclip reads at the route layer (today: canCreateIssues, canCreateAgents; plus the implicit role === "ceo" bypass). Otherwise future PRs will either duplicate the enumeration ad hoc or miss gates.
3. Consider whether the two uniqueness indices (issues_open_routine_execution_uq on executionRunId vs the new issues_open_routine_origin_uq on (companyId, originKind, originId)) should be consolidated or documented as complementary guards in the database NODE.
4. Once (1) and (2) are captured in the tree, this moves to ALIGNED.

Severity stays medium because the lock-bookkeeping vs task-recovery distinction is still unwritten, and the new agent-permissions key set is also unwritten — both will be cited as precedent by future PRs.

---

Engaging with gardener (1)

DeadlySilent · 2026-04-16T21:39:13Z references-verdict

Follow-up push after live incident audit (duplicate routine issue storm across companies):

### What was missing in the previous fix
The prior stale-lock recovery reduced failures, but open routine uniqueness was still gated by execution_run_id IS NOT NULL (issues_open_routine_execution_uq).
When execution_run_id had already been cleared/null, duplicate open issues could still be created for the same routine origin.

### Additional changes now pushed to this PR
- server/src/services/routines.ts
- findLiveExecutionIssue now has a final fallback to any open issue with matching (company_id, origin_kind='routine_execution', origin_id) even when not bound to a live heartbeat run.
- This ensures conflict handling coalesces to existing open routine-origin issues instead of re-attempting create paths.
- packages/db/src/schema/issues.ts
- Added issues_open_routine_origin_uq partial unique index definition.
- packages/db/src/migrations/0057_open_routine_origin_uniqueness.sql
- Adds DB guardrail unique index for open routine-origin uniqueness independent of execution_run_id.
- packages/db/src/migrations/meta/_journal.json
- Added migration entry for 0057_open_routine_origin_uniqueness.

### Validation
- pnpm --filter @paperclipai/db run check:migrations
- pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p server/tsconfig.json
- pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p packages/db/tsconfig.json

Commit: 5a2412e3f51807524004f8d56d4be96c20dbbd37

1 other reply (not engaging with gardener)
DeadlySilent · 2026-04-16T21:56:02Z

Follow-up noise-control patch pushed to the PR branch:

- Commit: b131b0ff
- File: server/src/routes/issues.ts
- Change: add assertCanCreateIssues(...) on POST /companies/:companyId/issues.
- Behavior: agent-authenticated issue creation now requires legacy creation privilege (role=ceo or permissions.canCreateAgents/canCreateIssues). Non-privileged agents now get 403 Missing permission: issues:create.

This prevents routine/agent self-spawn issue noise from standard worker agents while preserving existing assignment permission semantics (tasks:assign) and workspace-command mutation guards.

ALIGNED medium open PENDING by @Helmi · 2026-04-16T19:48:00Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · severity: medium · commit: ba78087d

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| deriveAuthTrustedOrigins includes config.port when hostname lacks an explicit port; skips :80/:443; handles IPv6 bracket/bare forms | Authenticated docker & local-trusted dev decision: auth, API access, and UI flows must always respect deployment mode, and compose entrypoints fail fast on missing auth config. Trusted origins are a direct consequence of authenticated mode. | ✅ Aligned |
| Explicit-port hostnames ("myhost:8080", "[::1]:8080") preserved as-is | Deployment NODE documents PORT=3100 as the production default, which means authenticated mode routinely runs on a non-standard port and the previous https://${hostname} origin was incomplete | ✅ Aligned |
| deploymentMode === "local_trusted" skipped | Decision record explicitly says auth and origin handling differ between local_trusted and authenticated; the PR preserves that distinction | ✅ Aligned |

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [infrastructure/deployment/authenticated-docker-and-local-trusted-dev.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/deployment/authenticated-docker-and-local-trusted-dev.md) — Two load-bearing modes: authenticated (Docker/hosted) vs local_trusted (dev). Auth flows must respect deployment mode.
- [infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md) — PORT=3100 production default; PAPERCLIP_DEPLOYMENT_MODE=authenticated + Better Auth requiring BETTER_AUTH_SECRET.
- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — Better Auth + three deployment modes; origin validation is part of the auth surface.

</details>

Medium severity (not low) because this is a behavioural fix in the auth trusted-origin path — wrong trusted origins break login in the primary authenticated deployment shape, but it does not contradict any tree decision. The implementation also matches the documented escape hatch: the inline comment correctly points operators at authBaseUrlMode="explicit" + authPublicBaseUrl for reverse-proxy cases, which aligns with the tree's "respect deployment mode" framing.

No concerns from a tree-fit perspective.

Issue #3820 UI shows "Waiting for output" on `blocked` issues — state confusion
NEW_TERRITORY low open by @hansmon · 2026-04-16T19:47:50Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-----------|---------------|-----|
| Live Runs card on blocked issues | "Waiting for run output" shown even when issue is blocked and no run is active — mixed signals for the operator | product/task-system/NODE.md: blocked is a first-class status meaning work is paused until a condition is met. issue-blockers/NODE.md: blocker state is the primary driver of agent behavior. Tree does not define UI semantics for the Live Runs card. | 🆕 New |
| Blocked-with-active-run edge case | Agent may still run on a blocked issue (comment wake that doesn't unblock) — UI conflates "blocked waiting on Board" with "work in flight" | product/task-system/comment-wake/NODE.md explicitly allows comment-driven wakes independent of status; tree doesn't say the wake should be hidden from the UI, only that it should be scoped. | ❓ Partial |
| "Surface problems, don't hide them" | UI currently hides the blocker primacy under an ambiguous "waiting" label | product/NODE.md principle: progressive disclosure; surfacing over hiding. Ambiguous phrasing is the opposite of surfacing. | ⚠️ Conflict (soft / principle-level) |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — fixed issue status set (backlog, todo, in_progress, in_review, done, blocked, cancelled).
- [product/task-system/issue-blockers/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/issue-blockers/NODE.md) — blocker links as a first-class concept with behavioral semantics.
- [product/task-system/issue-thread-ux/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/issue-thread-ux/NODE.md) — thread rendering conventions (scope for related UI decisions).
- [product/task-system/comment-wake/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/comment-wake/NODE.md) — comment-driven wakes can fire on a blocked issue.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree treats blocked as the primary state indicator but doesn't articulate how UI surfaces (Live Runs card, issue header, activity feed) should reflect that primacy. The reporter's proposed framing — blocked is primary, run activity is secondary context — is a decision worth codifying so it applies to the whole UI, not just this one card.

Suggested path forward:
1. Code fix (per reporter): when issue.status === 'blocked':
- No active run → hide the Live Runs card, or replace with "Blocked — no active work. See blocker comment above."
- Active run → render with explicit "Agent responding — does not unblock. Still waiting on X" framing.
2. Tree update (recommended): add a short decision record under product/task-system/issue-blockers/ (or a new product/task-system/issue-state-display/) naming the invariant: "When an issue is blocked, the blocker is the primary state indicator in every surface; run activity is secondary context." This way the next UI surface (inbox badges, sidebar, notifications) doesn't re-introduce the same ambiguity.
3. Related: would also remove any language suggesting agents auto-unblock via runs — they don't, per issue-blockers/NODE.md's "only all-blockers-done fires a dependency wakeup" rule.

Low severity, but the tree gap is load-bearing for consistent UI decisions across the board.

---

PR #3812 docs: productize local Hermes Paperclip workflow
NEW_TERRITORY medium open PENDING by @breakneo · 2026-04-16T19:47:44Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: 45aee522

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Hermes adapter as a first-class local runtime | Adds two long workflow docs, pnpm hermes:verify / pnpm hermes:demo scripts, and JSON templates for a hermes_local adapter + demo issue | Adapters NODE lists sub-domains: claude-local, codex-local, cursor-local, gemini-local, openclaw-gateway, opencode-local, pi-local. No hermes-local node exists. | 🆕 New |
| Workflow documentation surface | doc/PAPERCLIP_HERMES_LOCAL_WORKFLOW.md (378 lines, zh-CN), doc/PAPERCLIP_HERMES_CHECKLIST.md (56 lines, zh-CN), both embedding a single operator's absolute paths (/Users/neo/projects/paperclip) | Tree does not document doc/ as the canonical home for operator workflows; deployment/CI docs live as tree decision records, not per-adapter checklists in the source repo | 🆕 New |
| Plugin-only integration path | Docs state "当前分支的 Hermes 接入以 plugin-only 模式为准" and reference ~/.paperclip/adapter-plugins.json file: entries | Plugins NODE (not read inline here) owns plugin-SDK decisions; whether a new runtime ships as an adapter package vs. a plugin is a decision the tree does not capture for Hermes | 🆕 New |
| Root package.json script surface | Adds hermes:verify + hermes:demo scripts pointing at cli/node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs | Engineering/backend dev-runner decisions are tree-documented; one-off adapter demo scripts at the monorepo root don't have tree coverage | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Lists the seven adapter sub-domains; Hermes is not among them, and the "one package per adapter" decision plus declarative config schema model are the canonical path for new runtimes.
- [adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md) — All runtimes plug into one server adapter contract rather than living alongside it as docs + scripts.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: Introducing a new adapter runtime — even "plugin-only" — is a tree-level decision: it changes the set of supported agents, the set of adapter sub-domains, and the integration mode (adapter package vs. plugin). 1,177 lines of documentation + demo scripts is non-trivial surface area, and it bakes in a specific machine's paths (/Users/neo/projects/paperclip), a specific port (3100), and Chinese-only narrative — none of which line up with how the existing adapter and deployment nodes are written.

Suggested path forward:
1. Draft an adapters/hermes-local/NODE.md (or plugins/<something>/NODE.md if Hermes is deliberately plugin-only) that states: what Hermes is, why it's integrated as a plugin vs. a first-class packages/adapters/ entry, which config keys are canonical, and how it relates to the shared adapter contract.
2. Decide whether the workflow checklist belongs in the source repo at all, or on the tree as a decision record linking to a minimal scripts/hermes-local-* reference implementation. If it stays in-repo, strip machine-specific paths, English-translate the core reference flow, and keep the zh-CN copy as a translation rather than the canonical text.
3. Confirm with an adapters/plugins owner before merging — the hermes_local adapter type key shows up in the JSON template and the scripts assume it exists in /api/adapters, so there's an implicit dependency on code that is not in this PR.

Scoped as medium because this only lands docs + demo scripts (no runtime code), but it sets expectations about a runtime the tree hasn't adopted.

---

PR #3796 fix(heartbeat): emit documented plugin run lifecycle events
ALIGNED medium open PENDING by @LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-16T19:47:36Z → gardener comment
(2 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · severity: medium · commit: 3166559c

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Plugin event bus contract | Wires heartbeat run transitions to emit declared but previously unemitted agent.run.started/finished/failed/cancelled events through the plugin event bus (fixes #3789) | plugins/runtime/NODE.md: PluginEventBus routes domain events to subscribed plugins; manifest-declared subscriptions must be honored by the host | ✅ Aligned |
| Event contract hygiene | Maps timed_out → failed at the public event boundary so plugins don't see undocumented statuses | plugins/NODE.md: capability-gated, manifest-driven contract — matching the declared enum values is correct behavior | ✅ Aligned |
| Test coverage | Adds 287-line Vitest suite with embedded-Postgres verifying each lifecycle transition emits the right event | infrastructure/testing/NODE.md: Vitest + embedded Postgres is the documented unit-test pattern | ✅ Aligned |
| Overlap with parallel PR | Same topic as #3750, which also emits these lifecycle events (plus cost_event.created, agent.delegation.created, configurable reap threshold) | Two PRs touching the same heartbeat transitions need coordination — merge order and deduplication matter | ❓ Partial (coordination) |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [plugins/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Capability-gated plugins; manifest-driven event contract.
- [plugins/runtime/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/runtime/NODE.md) — PluginEventBus is the documented domain-event extension point.
- [infrastructure/testing/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/testing/NODE.md) — Vitest + embedded-Postgres unit testing.

</details>

Clean, focused fix that delivers on the plugin event contract the tree describes. The bug is real: events were declared in @paperclipai/shared and documented in the SDK README but never emitted, which meant plugins subscribing to them got no traffic. Wiring them through emitPluginEvent from activity-log.ts matches the plugin event-bus architecture.

### Recommendation

The tree fit is good; the only product-context concern is coordination with #3750. That PR targets the same lifecycle transitions (plus a wider scope: cost events, delegation events, configurable reap threshold). Pick one:
1. Land this first (focused, bug-fix-shaped, with tests), then rebase #3750 on top so it only adds the incremental events (cost_event.created, agent.delegation.created) and the reap-threshold knob.
2. Land #3750 first and close this as "already fixed", keeping the Vitest suite here as follow-up test coverage.

Either merge order is fine; just avoid both landing independently and producing duplicate agent.run.* emissions.

---

PR #3809 fix(ui): stop recreating markdown mention observers
NEW_TERRITORY low open PENDING by @LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-16T19:47:36Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: e233b918

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Markdown mention decoration observer | Stops re-creating the MutationObserver on every controlled-value change; dedupes decoration via requestAnimationFrame and drops value from the effect deps | Frontend NODE lists Lexical/MDX Editor as the rich text stack but does not document mention decoration observer lifecycle at this level of detail | 🆕 New |
| Shared markdown composer stability | Applies across issue and workflow comment surfaces that reuse MarkdownEditor | Issue Thread UX node describes thread polish (reference/mention rendering) as a frontend rendering concern decoupled from routing — aligned in spirit | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [engineering/frontend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — Stack: Lexical + MDX Editor; no decision-level mention of observer lifecycle.
- [engineering/frontend/issue-thread-ux/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/issue-thread-ux/NODE.md) — Markdown polish for thread display is a frontend rendering concern.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This is an implementation-level fix — don't re-attach the observer on every keystroke-driven re-render — that sits beneath the tree's granularity. No documented decision is touched; the behavioral goal (stable mention rendering during typing) aligns with the Issue Thread UX node's framing of markdown polish as a rendering concern.

Suggested path forward: Land as a routine bug fix; no tree update required. If the team wants to capture a new invariant ("shared composer must not rebuild decoration observers per keystroke") as a regression-prevention note, a short decision record under engineering/frontend/ could go in alongside this change, but that's optional.

PR #3808 fix: proactive session rotation + role-based max turns
CONFLICT high open PENDING by @mv50000 · 2026-04-16T19:47:28Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ⚠️ CONFLICT · severity: high · commit: 92e722fd

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Session compaction for native-context adapters | Switches ADAPTER_MANAGED_SESSION_POLICY to proactively rotate at 8 runs / 400k raw input tokens / 24h for adapters with native context management (Claude, Codex local) | Adapters NODE explicitly lists nativeContextManagement: "confirmed" as "Paperclip does not apply threshold-based session compaction". Claude-local NODE repeats: "Claude Code manages its own context window, so Paperclip skips threshold-based session compaction." | ⚠️ Conflict |
| maxTurnsPerRun default | Bumps claude-local execute/test to fall back to 1000 when unset; lowers UI default for new configs from 1000 → 200 | Tree is silent on the specific number, but the Claude-local node documents max-turns detection as the signal for "agent hit turn limit" — changing defaults affects run completion semantics | ❓ Partial |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Session management section: nativeContextManagement: "confirmed" → Paperclip does not apply threshold-based compaction; unknown → defaults kick in (max 200 runs, 2M input tokens, 72h).
- [adapters/claude-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/claude-local/NODE.md) — Capabilities: "Native context management: Confirmed. Claude Code manages its own context window, so Paperclip skips threshold-based session compaction."

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree records a deliberate boundary — native-context adapters own their own context window, and the adapter-managed policy is explicitly the opt-out from Paperclip-side thresholds. Flipping those thresholds from 0/0/0 to 8 / 400k / 24h is not a tuning change, it reverses the documented decision. The stated rationale ("avoiding expensive Haiku compaction calls on long contexts") is a real cost trade-off, but the tree is where that trade-off should be re-decided — not in the defaults file.

Suggested path forward:
1. Open a tree PR on adapters/NODE.md (and adapters/claude-local/NODE.md) that re-states the decision: Paperclip now rotates native-context adapter sessions proactively to pre-empt runtime compaction. Include the cost/latency rationale and the chosen thresholds.
2. If the decision should only apply to some native-context adapters (or only when a feature flag is set), encode that distinction in the tree so future adapters default correctly.
3. Land this code change after the tree change lands, or gate the new thresholds behind an opt-in config until the boundary is re-drawn.

The maxTurnsPerRun fallback (|| 1000) and the UI default drop from 1000 → 200 also deserve a short note somewhere — they move behaviour for every existing agent that left the field at 0.

---

Issue #3819 Heartbeat release fails to clear `checkoutRunId` — causes silent agent stalls
NEEDS_REVIEW high open by @hansmon · 2026-04-16T19:47:08Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: high · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-----------|---------------|-----|
| Run lifecycle state machine | Auto-release on run completion clears executionRunId / executionAgentNameKey / executionLockedAt but not checkoutRunId; dispatcher then sees the issue as still locked to a completed run and the agent exits ~7s with no work | engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md: "State transitions are atomic — a run cannot regress to a prior state." "Run orchestration includes guards against edge cases: duplicate concurrent runs for the same agent, adapter processes that outlive their timeout window..." Leaving a stale checkoutRunId behind breaks the atomicity spirit of the transition. | ⚠️ Conflict (state-machine invariant) |
| Atomic task checkout | Stale checkoutRunId pointing at a succeeded run effectively keeps the issue "checked out" forever | product/task-system/NODE.md: "Claiming a task is an atomic database operation. If another agent already claimed it, the request fails with a 409." Auto-release is the counterpart of the checkout; omitting a field from it defeats the 409 semantics from the other side. | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Explicit vs auto-release divergence | Reporter notes: POST /api/issues/:id/release does clear checkoutRunId; only the auto-release path omits it | Tree doesn't dictate that the two release paths must be equivalent, but the "failure hardening" and "auditable everything" principles make silent divergence a trap. | ❓ Partial |
| Silent failure mode | Agent appears alive, heartbeats fire with exit 0, but produces no work — operator cannot tell without reading stdout | Product principle: "Surface problems, don't hide them" (product/NODE.md). A succeeded run that did zero work should not look indistinguishable from a real one. | ⚠️ Conflict (principle) |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — run lifecycle state machine, failure-hardening invariants.
- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — atomic task checkout, 409 semantics, "no automatic recovery" philosophy.
- [product/task-system/auto-checkout/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/auto-checkout/NODE.md) — harness-level checkout on scoped wake; stale checkoutRunId undermines this.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The fix is obvious and small (add checkoutRunId: null to the set object in releaseIssueExecutionAndPromote), but the shape of the bug points to a missing tree invariant: auto-release and explicit release must clear the same set of lock fields, or one or both of those fields doesn't belong on the lock model.

Suggested path forward:
1. Code fix: apply the one-line addition the reporter proposes. Add a regression test that runs a full wake-execute-release-wake cycle and asserts issues.checkout_run_id is NULL after step 3.
2. Shared helper: extract the fields that comprise "issue execution lock" into one constant / helper function used by both releaseIssueExecutionAndPromote (auto) and POST /api/issues/:id/release (explicit). Prevents the next field being added in one path and not the other.
3. Tree update (recommended): add a short decision record under engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/ (or alongside the shared-api-and-actor-scoped-authorization.md decisions) naming the full set of "issue execution lock" fields and the invariant: every release path clears every field, atomically. This is the kind of cross-cutting constraint the tree exists for.
4. Also: surface the "succeeded but did zero productive work" case as an observable signal, not a silent success. Principle is in product/NODE.md but hasn't been operationalized here.

---
