Issue #3833 hermes_local adapter: AGENTS.md instructions not injected into agent context
NEW_TERRITORY medium open by @kainos46 · 2026-04-16T19:47:00Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| hermes_local adapter | AGENTS.md / HEARTBEAT.md / SOUL.md / TOOLS.md not injected into hermes prompt context | Tree does not document hermes_local — adapter sub-domains listed are claude-local, codex-local, cursor-local, gemini-local, openclaw-gateway, opencode-local, pi-local | 🆕 New |
| Instruction file injection is adapter-owned | Issue argues hermes should match claude_local behavior for AGENTS.md injection | product/agent-model/NODE.md "Adapter-Defined Identity": SOUL.md / HEARTBEAT.md / CLAUDE.md are not part of the Paperclip protocol — they are adapter-specific configurations | ❓ Partial — the expectation of adapter-owned injection is aligned, but hermes's specific wiring isn't covered |
| Related issues pattern | Issue references #2316 (hermes missing listSkills/syncSkills/resolveDesiredSkillNames) and #2915 (hermes missing PAPERCLIP_ env vars) — same class of "capabilities exist in hermes-paperclip-adapter but aren't wired into the server registry" | adapters/NODE.md ServerAdapterModule interface enumerates the capabilities (listSkills, syncSkills, sessionCodec, etc.) that a fully-integrated adapter must provide | ✅ Aligned in shape — the fix is registry wiring to match the shared contract |
| Silent ignore failure mode | Custom instructions configured in the UI are silently dropped | product/governance/NODE.md: "Nothing happens silently" | ⚠️ Conflict with the silent-drop symptom, aligned with the proposed fix direction |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Shared ServerAdapterModule contract; listed adapters do not include hermes_local
- [adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md) — "Adding a new runtime should usually mean adding one adapter package plus any parser or UI wiring it needs, not rewriting core orchestration"
- [product/agent-model/adapter-defined-agent-internals.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/adapter-defined-agent-internals.md) — Agent-internal identity files are adapter-owned, not protocol-owned
- [adapters/claude-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/claude-local/NODE.md) — Reference implementation for skill + instruction injection (symlinks into .claude/skills/, passed via --add-dir)

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The hermes_local adapter is new territory in the tree — it isn't listed among the documented adapter sub-domains, and neither is the pattern of "capabilities exist in the adapter package but aren't wired into the Paperclip server registry" (which this issue plus #2316 and #2915 all describe). That's three hermes-specific issues converging on the same class of bug, which suggests the tree should have a node capturing the hermes integration state and its gaps rather than leaving the decision implicit.

Suggested path forward:
1. Add a tree node adapters/hermes-local/NODE.md that records: what hermes_local is, what subset of ServerAdapterModule is wired, and what's missing (skill capabilities, env var injection, instruction injection). This becomes the source of truth for integration scope.
2. Ship the instruction-injection wiring parallel to how claude_local does it — the symlinked .claude/skills/ + instruction directory pattern is already the reference.
3. Cross-link #2316 and #2915 as siblings of this issue in the new hermes node so future agents can see the full shape of the integration gap at once.

Fixing the bug without the tree node leaves the next hermes integration question (e.g., quota, skill sync, session resume) to be rediscovered from scratch.

---

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| jq missing from base image | skills/paperclip/SKILL.md:123 mandates jq --arg for multi-line comment encoding, but node:lts-trixie-slim doesn't ship it — agents silently "smoosh" multi-line comments | Infra tree names node:lts-trixie-slim as the base and lists bundled runtime deps (ripgrep, git, wget, etc.); jq is absent and no decision justifies the omission | 🆕 New |
| Option A (bundle jq in image) | One-token Dockerfile apt-get addition | Deployment tree: "The production image installs Claude Code, Codex, and opencode-ai globally — these are the agent runtimes the server orchestrates." Runtime deps for shipped skills fit the same pattern. | ✅ Aligned |
| Option B (remove jq requirement, use node -e) | Rewrite the skill's helper script to use Node for JSON encoding | Tree doesn't have a node-vs-native-tool dependency policy; Option A is the simpler fit and preserves the existing skill contract | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md) — node:lts-trixie-slim base image; agent runtimes installed globally.
- [infrastructure/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/NODE.md) — Two deployment modes; Docker production image is the shipped default.
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Principle #6: "Surface problems, don't hide them" — silent comment mangling is a visibility regression.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This is a small, well-scoped infra fix. Option A (add jq to the Dockerfile apt-get install line) matches the existing pattern for runtime tooling (ripgrep/git/wget are already bundled for similar reasons). Option B would decouple the skill from a system binary but changes the skill contract without benefit — the skill already requires other binaries via adapters.

Suggested path forward:
- Land Option A. It's the contract-preserving change and matches how other skill-required binaries are handled.
- As a follow-up, consider a lightweight tree decision in infrastructure/deployment/ listing what binaries the base image guarantees (ripgrep, git, wget, python3, jq, bubblewrap). Today this only exists in the Dockerfile; having it documented makes future "what must the base image ship?" questions answerable without spelunking.
- Optionally audit other shipped skills for implicit binary dependencies not declared anywhere (would prevent this class of bug from recurring).

---

NEW_TERRITORY low open PENDING by @tmartin2113 · 2026-04-16T19:46:35Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: 09a71e4a

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| External health aggregator | Proxies /api/infrastructure/health from server to a "Vibe agent" service, gated on PAPERCLIP_HEALTH_URL env var, sidebar entry feature-flagged via health payload | Tree has no node describing Vibe-Stack integration, external health aggregators, or first-party infrastructure-health UX | 🆕 New |
| Frontend API layer | Adds ui/src/api/infrastructure-health.ts and a new InfrastructureHealth page under Company > Infrastructure | engineering/frontend/NODE.md: API calls centralized in /ui/src/api/, React Query for server state, shadcn/ui components — followed here | ✅ Aligned |
| Feature-flag pattern | Routes only mounted when env var set, sidebar entry only shows when features.infrastructureHealthEnabled is true | Consistent with existing companyDeletionEnabled feature-flag pattern on the health endpoint | ✅ Aligned |
| Plugin alternative | Implemented as first-party server routes + UI page | plugins/NODE.md: plugins are the declared extension surface for third-party integrations; external health aggregators may fit better there | ❓ Partial |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [infrastructure/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/NODE.md) — Deployment, monitoring surface area; does not mention external health aggregators.
- [engineering/frontend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — API layer + React Query pattern; page list does not currently include an Infrastructure page.
- [plugins/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Plugins as extension surface for integrations like observability.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree is silent on Vibe-Stack and on first-party infrastructure-health dashboards for external service aggregators. The PR's shape (env-var-gated proxy, React Query + shadcn card grid, sidebar entry behind a feature flag) is consistent with existing frontend patterns and doesn't contradict anything documented. The open question is whether third-party infrastructure integrations should live as first-party routes/pages or as plugins (plugins/NODE.md names observability as an example use case for plugins, and PR #3752 is landing an OpenTelemetry plugin that covers a similar concern).

Suggested path forward:
1. Confirm with maintainers whether Vibe-Stack integration should be first-party (as here) or a plugin (like the observability plugin in #3752); that decision is worth recording on the tree once made.
2. If kept first-party, consider adding an infrastructure/vibe-stack-integration node describing the env var, endpoint contract, and when this UI surfaces, so future agents know when to extend it.
3. The implementation itself is clean and scoped; no code-level product-fit blockers.

Issue #3802 Codex workspace-write sandbox blocks loopback — agents silently can't reach PAP API; no UI toggle
NEEDS_REVIEW high open by @inbusco · 2026-04-16T19:46:24Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: high · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Sandboxed Codex agent can't reach PAP API (loopback blocked) | Heartbeat exits 0, UI shows success, no API call happened — silent failure | Product principle #6 "Surface problems, don't hide them" and heartbeat orchestration "guards against edge cases…system degrades gracefully rather than producing inconsistent state" | ⚠️ Conflict (silent success is a principle violation) |
| Proposed UI toggle ("Allow sandboxed agents to reach the PAP API", default on) | Company-level setting surfaced in PAP UI, writes TOML at provision time | Adapters tree: declarative getConfigSchema + adapter-defined config blob; codex-home resolution is already adapter-owned | ✅ Aligned |
| Bypass via dangerouslyBypassApprovalsAndSandbox=true masks the issue in smoke tests | CEO agents configured that way work; sandboxed executors fail silently | Agent-model and governance say agents cannot bypass server-enforced governance; but here the opposite asymmetry exists — sandboxed ICs are more broken than bypass-mode CEOs | ❓ Partial (inverse of the expected safety direction) |
| Volume persistence (docker compose down -v wipes workaround) | Named volume for codex-home/config.toml loses the manual TOML fix | Infra tree owns Docker volume/UID remapping decisions; no decision on persisting per-adapter runtime config across volume resets | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Principle #6: "Surface problems, don't hide them. No silent auto-recovery."
- [engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — Adapter-level failures should be surfaced separately from agent-level failures; graceful degradation, not inconsistent state.
- [adapters/codex-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/codex-local/NODE.md) — Codex home directory resolution (codex-home.ts) is already adapter-owned.
- [infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md) — Docker / named-volume patterns; authenticated mode's network topology.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The biggest issue here isn't the missing TOML section — it's that the heartbeat returns status=succeeded, exit_code=0 while no API call happened. That directly contradicts Paperclip's core principle of surfacing problems and heartbeat-orchestration's "graceful degradation rather than inconsistent state." The UI toggle is a good ergonomic fix, but two tree-level decisions are needed:

1. Signal layer (high priority). Define the contract for "adapter succeeded but couldn't reach the API" — e.g., a new terminal state or a first-class heartbeat_run_event that the orchestration layer can count as a degraded run, not a success. This belongs in engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/. Without it, any future adapter can reproduce the same silent-fail pattern.
2. Network-access policy (medium priority). Record the decision that, by default, sandboxed Codex agents must have loopback access so they can reach the PAP API. This probably lives as a codex-local leaf (e.g., adapters/codex-local/sandbox-network-policy.md). The "air-gapped" opt-out is a governance-shaped feature (board decision), so also verify with product/governance/ owners.

Suggested path forward:
- Short-term: apply the TOML section unconditionally at company import / adapter provisioning (Option "Alternative (lower effort)") so the immediate breakage is fixed.
- Concurrent: add an end-of-heartbeat check that detects "zero outbound API calls + presence of 'cannot reach API' in transcript" and flips the run to a non-success state; this closes the silent-failure loophole regardless of the TOML fix.
- Medium-term: ship the UI toggle + persist the TOML override outside the wipeable named volume (mount or derived at startup from PAP settings).
- Tree PR first for the signal-layer decision, then code PR.

---

Issue #3832 [Bug] Server always binds to 0.0.0.0 on IPv6-only deployments — ALL_INTERFACES_BIND_HOST hardcoded in @paperclipai/shared
ALIGNED medium open by @Plectar · 2026-04-16T19:46:01Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · severity: medium · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Deployment mode coverage | On an IPv6-only LXC host, bind: lan always resolves to 0.0.0.0, leaving the server unreachable; was working on v2026.403.0 (regression) | infrastructure/NODE.md: "Two deployment modes" — authenticated/public with bind: lan is a first-class deployment shape; product/NODE.md: "Local-first, cloud-ready — the mental model should not change between local and shared deployments" | ✅ Aligned |
| Shared constants as source of truth | Fix is in @paperclipai/shared/dist/network-bind.js ALL_INTERFACES_BIND_HOST | engineering/shared/NODE.md: "single source of truth for domain vocabulary shared across backend, frontend, CLI, and adapter packages" — bind presets correctly live here; the fix belongs in this package | ✅ Aligned |
| Config override was silently ignored | config.json "host": "::" and HOST=:: env are overwritten by resolveRuntimeBind() | product/NODE.md: "Surface problems, don't hide them" — silently overriding user config is the wrong shape | ✅ Aligned |
| Scope | Change constant to "::" (dual-stack); trivial and reversible | No tree decision gates this — pure default hardening | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [engineering/shared/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/shared/NODE.md) — Shared constants and config schemas live in one package with zero runtime deps; the right surface for bind presets
- [infrastructure/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/NODE.md) — authenticated deployment mode is first-class; LXC/Proxmox-style hosts are expected targets
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Local-first, cloud-ready" — the mental model shouldn't change between dual-stack and IPv6-only hosts

</details>

No concerns. Flipping ALL_INTERFACES_BIND_HOST to "::" is the minimum-diff correct default — on dual-stack Linux (the common case) the kernel maps IPv4 onto IPv6 sockets, so behavior is preserved; on pure-IPv6 hosts the server actually becomes reachable. The regression from v2026.403.0 → v2026.416.0 makes this a shipping blocker for IPv6-only users, hence medium rather than low. Worth double-checking whether any deployment doc still documents 0.0.0.0 as the literal bind expected in ss -tlnp output (the constant change will flip that to :::3100).

---

Issue #3799 Skill import drops non-markdown files when source is the skill root directory
NEW_TERRITORY medium open by @aidyfeng · 2026-04-16T19:45:40Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Skill import inventory bug | When source is the skill-root dir (skillDir === "."), entry.startsWith("./") never matches so the inventory collapses to only SKILL.md and trustLevel is forced to markdown_only | Tree documents skill-injection mechanics at the adapter level but does not specify skill-import inventory semantics or trustLevel derivation on the server | 🆕 New |
| Silent data loss | Agents assigned the skill lose visibility into scripts/references/assets; workaround is passing the parent dir | Tree principle: "Surface problems, don't hide them" / "No silent auto-recovery" — this bug silently degrades skill capability without signaling it | ❓ Partial (violates principle in spirit, though not a documented server decision) |
| Fix options A vs B | A = fix the prefix check; B = stop overriding the correct inventory returned by collectLocalSkillInventory("full") | Tree does not record a skill-import design doc, so either fix is compatible with existing decisions | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — Service-oriented backend; services own skill-import business logic.
- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Skill injection mechanics happen downstream of company-skill records; trust level affects what agents can execute.
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them" (Key Product Decision #6).

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This is a legitimate bug — a path-prefix mismatch that silently drops the inventory and downgrades trustLevel. No tree decision is at stake; the question is only which fix to land.

Suggested path forward:
- Prefer Option B (drop lines 1016–1017 and trust the inventory from readLocalSkillImportFromDirectory → collectLocalSkillInventory("full")). It deletes the brittle filter path entirely for the single-skill case.
- Keep the filter only for the multi-skill discovery branch (when skillPaths.length > 1), and in that branch apply Option A's fix (empty-prefix handling) plus explicit sibling-exclusion.
- Add a regression test that imports a single-skill root directory with scripts/, references/, and assets/ and asserts trustLevel === "scripts_executables" and the file kinds are populated. The existing parent-dir workaround path should keep working unchanged.

No tree update required beyond optionally recording the skill-import inventory contract if it is touched again.

---

Issue #3816 Execute vault migration scripts from security-hardening branch
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open by @grabault · 2026-04-16T19:45:35Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-----------|---------------|-----|
| Move .env secrets into Paperclip vault | Execute migrate-env-to-vault.sh + configure-agent-secrets.sh from claude/security-hardening-scripts-2026-04-16 | Tree has a secrets-provider abstraction (engineering/backend/NODE.md lists "secrets — credential vault (supports multiple secret providers)") and a security drift node that mandates "no hardcoded secret fallbacks" and secret redaction. Direction is aligned. | ✅ Aligned (direction) |
| Branch provenance | Issue references claude/security-hardening-scripts-2026-04-16 on /home/geoff/paperclip | Branch is not present on paperclipai/paperclip (checked via GitHub API, 404). This is a local branch in the reporter's workspace. | ❓ Partial |
| "Dry-run by default, --apply to mutate" + "verify heartbeats" | Operational playbook embedded in the issue | Tree does not document a scripts/ runbook convention for operational migrations | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — names the secrets credential-vault domain and multi-provider abstraction.
- [drift/paperclip-e392f6b1/infrastructure/security/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/drift/paperclip-e392f6b1/infrastructure/security/NODE.md) — "No hardcoded secret fallbacks" + "secrets are redacted in logs and API responses".
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — every mutation writes to activity_log; auditable-everything principle applies to secret-binding operations.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The outcome (secrets in vault, bound to agent adapter configs) is aligned with tree direction, but the artifact being executed doesn't live in this repo yet — the referenced branch is absent on paperclipai/paperclip. Running migration scripts against production-ish state without the scripts landing as reviewed code on main is a governance-trail gap ("auditable everything" cuts both ways).

Suggested path forward:
1. Open a PR that adds scripts/migrate-env-to-vault.sh and scripts/configure-agent-secrets.sh (+ tests / dry-run output fixtures) to paperclipai/paperclip. Execute from the merged version, not from a local workspace branch.
2. Make sure both scripts write to activity_log for every secret that moves (satisfies governance audit invariant).
3. After merge, consider promoting the security drift node (drift/paperclip-e392f6b1/infrastructure/security/) to a permanent infrastructure/security/ node and add a short runbook entry naming the vault migration path as the supported way to seed secrets. This closes the scripts-runbook gap the tree currently has.

ALIGNED low open by @bscottdavis · 2026-04-16T19:45:25Z → gardener comment

Issue #3797 Research digest 2026-04-16: OpenClaw instability + plan-mode surge, Paperclip governance hardening
NEW_TERRITORY low open by @drumnation · 2026-04-16T19:44:59Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------------|---------------|-----|
| Strategic research digest | Daily digest summarizing OpenClaw instability cluster and Paperclip governance-hardening PRs; recommends operational watch + assimilation targets | Tree documents Paperclip's product identity and governance model but has no node that tracks external-ecosystem digests, assimilation queues, or Dynasty-direction decisions | 🆕 New |
| OpenClaw assimilation targets (plan-mode stack, mutation gates, cross-session plan store, skill plan templates) | Candidates flagged for potential absorption into Paperclip | Tree treats OpenClaw only as an adapter target (adapters/openclaw-gateway/); no roadmap node for "what from OpenClaw should move into Paperclip core" | 🆕 New |
| Control-plane direction reaffirmation | "Continue treating Paperclip as a governance/control-plane candidate over OpenClaw" | Tree already states this explicitly in product/NODE.md identity and in governance decisions — the digest is consistent with the tree, not in tension | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Control plane, not execution plane"; Paperclip as the governance/control-plane substrate.
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Approval gates, cost tracking, audit trail — the "governance hardening" the digest references.
- [adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md) — OpenClaw integration is as an adapter today; no tree posture on assimilating OpenClaw capabilities upstream.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This is a meta/strategic artifact, not a feature request. The digest itself is useful context, but there is no tree node that tracks external-ecosystem monitoring, assimilation backlogs, or which OpenClaw capabilities are in-scope to pull upstream. The key tension worth flagging: the digest lists plan-mode, mutation gates, approval state machines, and cross-session plan storage as assimilation candidates. The tree's V1 scope explicitly defers things like plugin framework/SDK and self-healing orchestration — so whether these belong in core (vs. plugins) is a real decision, not an obvious one.

Suggested path forward:
1. If the digest is to be recurring, consider a tree node (e.g., product/external-signals.md or a routines sub-node) that captures the assimilation-evaluation rubric — at minimum: "core vs. plugin", "V1 vs. post-V1", "who owns evaluation".
2. The specific assimilation candidates (mutation gates, approval state machine, plan storage) overlap with product/governance/ and product/task-system/ — any action beyond "watch" should produce a decision record in those domains before code.
3. No code action needed for this issue itself; the tree update (if any) is the right follow-up.

Issue #3813 codex_local adapter flagged as nativeContextManagement='confirmed' — sessions never rotate, prompt grows unbounded
CONFLICT high open by @btilus · 2026-04-16T19:44:50Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · ⚠️ CONFLICT · severity: high · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-----------|---------------|-----|
| codex_local native context management | Empirical data: rawInputTokens grows monotonically to 483M in a single run; sessionReused=true, sessionRotated=false, rotation never driven by thresholds. Codex CLI is not compacting reused sessions in practice. | adapters/codex-local/NODE.md asserts: "Native context management: Confirmed. Codex manages its own context, so Paperclip skips compaction thresholds." The code flag nativeContextManagement: "confirmed" and ADAPTER_MANAGED_SESSION_POLICY (thresholds=0) enact this claim. | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Governance visibility / budget hard-stops | 9M → 4.73B tokens/day growth; hit ChatGPT subscription cap | Governance tier 3: "Hard ceiling -- at 100%, auto-pause the Agent" — but the ceiling is on the budget, not the underlying prompt-size pathology. The tree does not anticipate a single heartbeat emitting 0.5B input tokens. | ❓ Partial |
| Default compaction policy | Reporter suggests default policy (maxRawInputTokens=2M, maxSessionRuns=200, maxSessionAgeHours=72) | Tree does not document the default policy numbers; they live in adapter-utils | 🆕 New (tree silent on numbers) |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/codex-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/codex-local/NODE.md) — explicitly lists Native context management: Confirmed as a codex-local capability. This is the decision the evidence contradicts.
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — cost-tracking / budget hard-stop decisions; unbounded prompt growth is an upstream precondition that the governance layer relies on not happening.
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — "Context Delivery Spectrum" (fat vs. thin payload) assumes the adapter/agent bounds its own state; unbounded reuse sits outside both poles.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The issue's evidence directly contradicts a tree-level capability claim ("Confirmed" native context management for codex_local). Keeping the claim while the implementation leaks megatokens will keep producing this exact incident and will mislead anyone reading the tree to decide whether to add codex-local-style "confirmed" flags for future adapters.

Suggested path forward:
1. Code fix (implementation, per reporter):
- Either drop codex_local from the nativeContextManagement: "confirmed" set, or pair it with the default threshold policy.
- Add a regression test: after N heartbeats in a reused session, a rotation must fire when rawInputTokens crosses threshold.
2. Tree fix (decision record): update adapters/codex-local/NODE.md to either
- downgrade the capability ("Native context management: Observed unreliable — Paperclip applies default threshold policy as a safety net"), or
- if the team believes codex-cli will compact and this is a codex-cli bug, record that decision along with a bounded safety net so the "Confirmed" claim is not taken at face value.
3. Optional: add a product-level invariant that no single run may emit input > N tokens without an approval — closes the governance gap the issue exposes. Would go under product/governance/ or adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md.

This is one of the clearest tree-vs-reality conflicts I've seen on this repo recently; resolving it in the tree (not just the code) will prevent the same decision being re-confirmed for future adapters.

---

Issue #3830 GET /issues/:id/comments?after=<uuid> returns 500: TypeError when Date is passed as postgres.js bind param
ALIGNED low open by @bscottdavis · 2026-04-16T19:44:48Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit (refresh)

Re-review triggered by a cross-reference only — the issue body is unchanged since the previous review. Verdict stays ALIGNED: this is a postgres.js bind-param type error (Date not coerced before the driver sees it) in the after cursor path of /issues/:id/comments. Execution-level fix; no tree decision at stake. Recommendation still stands: coerce the cursor to .toISOString() (or validate with a zod datetime) before binding.

Issue #3794 Context budget telemetry per heartbeat run
NEEDS_REVIEW low open by @ambigos1 · 2026-04-16T19:44:23Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Per-heartbeat context/cost telemetry | Instrument heartbeat runs to capture startup context bytes/tokens, skills loaded-vs-activated, tokens in/out, dollar cost | Governance tree mandates cost tracking at every level (tokens + $), visibility dashboards, and "nothing happens silently" auditing | ❓ Partial |
| Skill-size budgets | Issue raises "enforcing skill size budgets" as a downstream goal | Governance tree defines budget hard-stops for token/dollar spend (visibility → soft alerts → hard ceiling), but not context-size budgets; adapters tree has no context-size policy | ❓ Partial |
| Heartbeat run event stream | Surface data in run detail view and as aggregate metrics per agent | Heartbeat-run-orchestration defines event-sourced heartbeat_run_events and state transitions as first-class | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — "Cost Tracking at Every Level", visibility dashboards as a core governance mechanism.
- [engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — Event-sourced run lifecycle; ideal carrier for startup-context telemetry events.
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — "Fully instrumented" is the top integration level and the expected shape for cost/usage reporting.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The data being proposed (tokens in/out, dollar cost per run) is already a tree decision — governance demands cost tracking per run, agent, task. What's new is bytes-of-context and skills-loaded-vs-activated, which extend the telemetry model from "what the agent spent" to "what the harness cost". That extension is useful (it's a prerequisite for #3791/#3793) but should be nailed down once rather than discovered per-adapter.

Suggested path forward:
1. Confirm with governance owners that context-size and skill-activation counts belong in heartbeat_run_events alongside token/cost data (tree's current framing is token/dollar-centric).
2. Capture the decision in engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/ (what fields, emitted by server vs. adapter) before adding columns — this is the right place because per-run lifecycle events are already owned there.
3. Treat #3791/#3793 as downstream of this issue: land telemetry first so optimization decisions are driven by data rather than estimated savings.

Also consider whether "skill activation" detection is adapter-feasible without instrumenting the agent's tool-call transcript — that is not a trivial capability for every runtime (Gemini/Cursor especially), and the tree should note which adapters can honestly report this.

---

Issue #3793 Task-aware skill injection for adapters
NEW_TERRITORY medium open by @ambigos1 · 2026-04-16T19:43:35Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Task-conditional skill injection | Propose gating SKILL.md injection on task labels / titleMatch / type (e.g., review skill only on review tasks) | Adapters tree describes pre-run skill injection (ensureCodexSkillsInjected) and declarative adapter config, but no decision on content-gating injection on task metadata | 🆕 New |
| Cross-domain coupling | Adapter would need to read task metadata (labels/title) at heartbeat time to decide which skills to symlink | Tree currently treats adapters as runtime-agnostic and says task-acceptance logic lives in SKILL.md; coupling adapters to task labels is a new cross-cutting design | 🆕 New |
| Context-budget impact (~116KB savings) | Largest Tier 2 optimization identified | Tree is silent on context budgeting targets (also see sibling issue #3794 asking for telemetry first) | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Skill injection via symlinks; declarative config schema.
- [adapters/codex-local/rpc-quota-and-fresh-skill-injection.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/codex-local/rpc-quota-and-fresh-skill-injection.md) — Skills refreshed on the execution path for each run.
- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Task hierarchy and communication channel; tasks as the work/context surface.
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Context-Delivery Spectrum: fat payload vs. thin ping is a first-class config axis.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This is a strong fit for the "Context Delivery Spectrum" decision (fat payload vs. thin ping) — skill injection is effectively part of the fat-payload envelope. But implementing task-aware injection requires adapters to read task-level metadata, which crosses a boundary the tree currently keeps clean (adapters don't own task scheduling or task-data access). Before coding, a decision should name the contract: what task metadata is passed to the adapter at heartbeat time, and who computes the label-match / titleMatch rules — adapter, adapter-utils, or server.

Suggested path forward:
1. Sequence behind telemetry (#3794): without per-run context size / skill-activation data, the optimization target is a guess.
2. Capture a decision under adapters/ or adapters/skill-injection-contract.md covering:
- Where the predicate runs (server-side selection vs. adapter-side decision).
- Whether the when: { labels, titleMatch } schema is part of the Paperclip protocol or each adapter's config blob (the tree prefers the latter, per "Adapter-Defined Identity").
- Interaction with always-required skills like paperclip (the protocol skill) vs. role/task skills.
3. Coordinate with #3791 (override bundled required: true) — these two design the same skill-scoping axis from different directions; decide them together to avoid two overlapping mechanisms.

---

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |

| Duplicate report | Same content / same author / same date as #3804 (identical body, minor whitespace) | N/A — duplicate detection is not a tree decision | ❓ Partial |
| Reaper / stuck agent detection | Automated health-check flags 1 agent stuck in running >10min; reaper didn't catch it | Tree lists running as an agent status and flags "Health monitoring -- distinguishing 'stuck' from 'working on a long task'" as an open question. heartbeat-run-orchestration mentions "adapter processes that outlive their timeout window" as a hardening concern but does not spec a reaper. | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — agent statuses and the open question about stuck-vs-long-task discrimination.
- [engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — failure-hardening responsibilities of the orchestration layer.

### Recommendation

Why: This issue is a duplicate of #3804 — identical automated health-check report. Consolidating the thread prevents double-triage and duplicate fix tracking.

Suggested path forward:
1. Close as duplicate of #3804 and continue investigation/decision there.
2. If the automation at amplifywhy-ai is firing twice per health check, add a de-dup key on the issue filer to avoid future noise.
3. After the root-cause fix, document reaper policy under engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/ and remove the "stuck vs. long task" open question from agent-model.

---

Issue #3804 [INFRA] Agent stuck in running state >10min — reaper issue
NEW_TERRITORY low open by @AmplifyWhy · 2026-04-16T19:43:06Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-----------|---------------|-----|
| Reaper / stuck agent detection | Automated health-check flags 1 agent in running state >10min; reaper didn't catch it (second occurrence in 2 days) | Tree lists running in agent statuses and flags "Health monitoring -- distinguishing 'stuck' from 'working on a long task'" as an open question. heartbeat-run-orchestration documents "adapter processes that outlive their timeout window" as a hardening concern but does not specify a reaper mechanism. | 🆕 New |
| Failure surfacing | Silent degraded state — operator must read logs to know | Tree principle: "Surface problems, don't hide them" (product/NODE.md); activity_log is audit-trail foundation | ❓ Partial — the product principle exists, concrete surfacing mechanism doesn't |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — declares agent statuses (active, paused, idle, running, error, terminated) and names stuck-vs-long-task discrimination as an open question.
- [engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md) — names "adapter processes that outlive their timeout window" as a failure-hardening concern; does not specify reaper semantics.
- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — "No Automatic Recovery": stale tasks are surfaced through dashboards, recovery is manual/explicit.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree explicitly flags "distinguishing stuck from long-running" as unresolved. The repeating pattern (2 incidents in 24h) plus the "no automatic recovery" principle means a recurring gap between adapter process timeouts and the running status field — worth closing in the tree so future agents/contributors know what the reaper is supposed to guarantee.

Suggested path forward:
1. Investigate and fix the specific missed termination (diagnostics in the issue are the right next step).
2. After fixing, update engineering/backend/heartbeat-run-orchestration/NODE.md with a short decision record that (a) names the reaper's responsibility, (b) defines the timeout policy, and (c) clarifies what "stuck in running" means relative to the agent-model open question. This removes the question from the agent-model open-questions list.

Issue #3822 Parent-issue assignees not auto-woken on child status changes (`blocked`/`done`/`cancelled`)
NEW_TERRITORY medium open by @hansmon · 2026-04-16T19:43:05Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Parent-assignee wakeup on child status change | Proposes auto-wakeup for the parent issue's assignee when a child transitions to blocked/done/cancelled | Tree is silent on parent/child wake propagation specifically | 🆕 New |
| Existing "automatic wake" precedents | Issue argues for platform-level propagation over agent-prose compliance | product/task-system/issue-blockers: "all-blockers-done" already fires an automatic dependency wakeup using the same wake-event infrastructure; product/task-system/comment-wake: comment-driven wakes are intentionally scoped with minimal payload | ❓ Partial — proposal extends an existing pattern, but the analogy isn't yet decided |
| Tension with "No Automatic Recovery" | Proposal favors platform behavior over the emergent manager-agent pattern | product/task-system/NODE.md: "A project manager agent whose job is to monitor for stale work is the emergent pattern, not a built-in behavior" — but issue-blockers shows this is not an absolute rule | ❓ Partial |
| Opt-out config knob | Proposes runtime_config.heartbeat.wakeOnChildStatus per agent | Aligns with existing "Context Delivery Spectrum" (fat payload vs thin ping) being per-agent configurable | ✅ Aligned in shape |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Single-assignee model; "No Automatic Recovery" principle
- [product/task-system/issue-blockers/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/issue-blockers/NODE.md) — Dependency wakeups: automatic platform-level wake when all blockers clear
- [product/task-system/comment-wake/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/comment-wake/NODE.md) — Comment-driven wake trigger; minimal scoped payload
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Heartbeat protocol; per-agent context delivery spectrum

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree has a clear precedent for automatic, platform-level wakes on dependency state transitions (issue-blockers), but the parent/child status axis is a separate decision that isn't recorded. There's also a counter-principle ("No Automatic Recovery" → manager-agent as emergent pattern) that needs an explicit reconciliation before shipping. The proposal's shape — event-scoped wake with a typed reason, per-agent opt-out — is consistent with the existing wake infrastructure, which is what makes it a viable extension rather than a conflict.

Suggested path forward:
1. Add a tree node (e.g., product/task-system/parent-child-wake/) recording the decision: when do parent assignees auto-wake on child transitions, and which transitions qualify (blocked/cancelled are the strongest candidates; done is more debatable because it could storm a coordinator on high-fan-out trees).
2. Reconcile with "No Automatic Recovery" — the principle allows automation that surfaces state changes (like dependency wakeups) versus automation that hides them (auto-reassign). Platform-level parent wakes fit the former.
3. Spec the opt-out knob shape alongside the existing heartbeat config surface so it doesn't become a one-off.

Ship the tree decision first, then the server change.

---

Issue #3792 Per-agent GEMINI_CLI_HOME isolation for extensions and skills
NEW_TERRITORY medium open by @ambigos1 · 2026-04-16T19:42:58Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Per-agent Gemini home isolation | Proposes geminiCliHome adapter config that sets GEMINI_CLI_HOME per agent, so ~/.gemini/extensions/ and ~/.gemini/skills/ don't leak across sessions | Tree documents Gemini adapter's skill sync (listGeminiSkills/syncGeminiSkills) and auth-expiry detection, but not per-agent home directory isolation | 🆕 New |
| Extension-induced context bloat | Human-installed Gemini extensions (e.g. caveman) get inlined into every heartbeat system instruction (14–18KB) | Adapter node doesn't name an extension-pollution constraint; governance/agent-model don't address per-agent host isolation | 🆕 New |
| Config schema extension | Adds a new adapterConfig.geminiCliHome field | Tree says adapter settings are adapter-defined and rendered via declarative getConfigSchema — supported mechanism, but this specific field has no tree decision | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/gemini-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/gemini-local/NODE.md) — Gemini adapter spawns gemini as a local child process; persistent skill sync only.
- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Declarative config schema; skill injection via symlinks into a temporary .claude/skills/ directory (but this pattern isn't yet generalized to Gemini extensions).
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Adapter-defined agent internals; Paperclip doesn't reach into agent config space.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The fix is sound and matches the tree's "adapter-defined internals" stance — Paperclip sets an env var and the adapter handles the rest. But this is the second issue in as many days (see #3791) in the same "heartbeat context-budget" theme without a tree decision anchoring it. Before landing a geminiCliHome field, the skill/extension isolation strategy should exist as a decision the adapters team can reference consistently (Codex has an analogous home via codex-home.ts that also hosts shared config).

Suggested path forward:
1. Capture an adapters-level decision (e.g., adapters/per-agent-runtime-home.md or within gemini-local/) stating that adapter homes are per-agent by default for runtimes whose home directory inlines extensions/skills into system instructions.
2. Align the Codex analogue (codex-home.ts) with the same principle so #3802's named-volume config issue and this one share a model.
3. Then add geminiCliHome (and document default resolution) to the adapter config schema.

This keeps the fix small while making sure the next adapter (e.g., Cursor) inherits the same posture rather than rediscovering it.

---

Issue #3791 Allow desiredSkills to exclude bundled skills (required: true override)
NEW_TERRITORY medium open by @ambigos1 · 2026-04-16T19:42:18Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Bundled skill required: true override | Issue asks for per-agent exclusion of bundled Paperclip skills (to reduce heartbeat context size for ICs) | Adapters tree describes skill injection via symlinks + ensureCodexSkillsInjected / ensureGeminiSkillsInjected, but no decision on per-role skill scoping or a required → optional override policy | 🆕 New |
| Config surface (desiredSkills / excludeSkills) | Proposes extending desiredSkills semantics or adding excludeSkills to adapter config | Tree says adapter config is declarative via getConfigSchema, but does not document a skill-scoping contract | 🆕 New |
| Context-budget rationale | ~12KB/heartbeat wasted for Codex, ~1KB for Gemini; follow-up to shipped caveman-extension removal (1a) | Tree is silent on context-budget targets or cross-adapter skill policy | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Skill injection via symlinks into temp .claude/skills/; declarative config schema over custom UI.
- [adapters/codex-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/codex-local/NODE.md) — Pre-run ensureCodexSkillsInjected for skill freshness.
- [adapters/gemini-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/gemini-local/NODE.md) — Persistent skill sync via listGeminiSkills / syncGeminiSkills.
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Adapter-defined agent internals (agent config blobs are adapter-specific, not part of Paperclip protocol).

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree treats adapter-internal skill/config as adapter-defined (see agent-model's "Adapter-Defined Identity"), and describes skill injection mechanics but never decides whether paperclip-* skills bundled by adapter-utils are per-role or universal. That decision is load-bearing for the "Context Budget" optimization thread (#3794 telemetry, #3793 task-aware injection, this issue) and determines whether desiredSkills is additive or authoritative.

Suggested path forward: Before implementing a config option, capture the decision in the tree — most naturally a new leaf under adapters/ (or a shared adapters/skill-injection-contract.md) answering:
1. Is desiredSkills additive or authoritative?
2. When is a bundled skill required: true justified (e.g., the paperclip protocol skill itself) vs. role-scoped?
3. What is the precedence order between bundled-defaults, agent-config, and company-level overrides?

Option 3 in the issue (make bundles required: false by default and let desiredSkills own the set) is the cleanest fit with "Adapter-Defined Identity" — the adapter ships defaults, but the agent config is the source of truth. Worth confirming with adapters owners before coding, since this also interacts with #3793's task-aware injection proposal.

---

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Board approval discoverability | No sidebar/badge/dashboard surfaces pending approvals; users must hit the API to find PEN-XXXX | product/NODE.md: the default experience must answer "what needs my approval"; product/governance: approve/reject/cancel is a core board power that must be accessible | ✅ Aligned |
| Approvals page exists but isn't surfaced | Issue proposes sidebar entry + badge, dashboard card, clickable PEN-XXXX links | engineering/frontend/NODE.md already lists an Approvals page as a top-level route; making it discoverable is an unambiguous gap-fill | ✅ Aligned |
| Agent compliance vs platform behavior | Today agents compensate by appending paperclipai approval list to every blocker comment | product/NODE.md: "Surface problems, don't hide them" — governance visibility belongs in the platform, not in prompt compliance | ✅ Aligned |

<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/governance/issue-approvals/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/issue-approvals/NODE.md) — Approvals reuse shared board infrastructure; discovery must be uniform

</details>

No concerns. This is a clean product-UX gap against documented tree decisions — the Board is supposed to know at a glance "what needs my approval," and right now they don't. Proposed fix (1) — sidebar entry with pending-count badge — is the minimum-viable surface that closes this gap and matches the existing Approvals page infrastructure. Fix (3) — clickable PEN-XXXX — is a natural follow-on that removes the discovery burden from agent prose.

PR #3790 fix(routes): return 409 for routine checkout conflicts
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-16T04:57:46Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: cf8c4b42

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Checkout error semantics | Maps the issues_open_routine_execution_uq unique-constraint violation on POST /issues/:id/checkout from a 500 to a 409 Conflict | Task System decides that claiming a task is an atomic operation and a failed claim "fails with a 409 conflict" — agents build retry/handoff around 409, not 500 | ✅ Aligned |
| Routine concurrency | Keeps only one active routine execution per routine by surfacing the DB guard as a 409, letting agents fall back to the normal conflict path | Routines domain requires a concurrency policy (skip/queue/cancel-previous); agents need a recoverable signal to implement it, which is the 409 | ✅ Aligned |
| Error-handling scope | Narrow try/catch around a single svc.checkout() call; re-throws anything that is not the target constraint | Tree has no decision forbidding route-level error translation, and the change preserves existing failure behavior for unrelated errors | ✅ Aligned |

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — "Atomic Task Checkout": failed checkout must return 409 identifying the conflict
- [product/task-system/auto-checkout/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/auto-checkout/NODE.md) — Auto-checkout on wake relies on the same 409-conflict semantics
- [product/routines/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/routines/NODE.md) — Concurrency policies govern overlapping routine runs; a recoverable 409 is what makes them enforceable at the agent layer

</details>

No concerns. The fix tightens the server so routine-execution contention surfaces as the 409 the task system and routines domains already promise, instead of a generic 500 that crashes agents. Resolves #3660.

---

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| OpenClaw session key strategy | When sessionKeyStrategy: "issue" is selected but the wake carries no issueId (e.g. a heartbeat-discovered task), the adapter now falls back to a run-scoped key paperclip:run:{runId} instead of the shared fixed fallback. | adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md documents the three session key strategies (issue/fixed/run) and states that issue ties sessions to Paperclip issues. Silently coalescing heartbeat work into the shared fixed session contradicts the isolation an operator expects from the issue setting. | ✅ Aligned |
| Regression coverage | New unit test in execute.test.ts covers the no-issueId heartbeat path of resolveSessionKey. Follow-up commit f787c91b adds packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/vitest.config.ts and registers the package in the root vitest projects list so the new test is actually discoverable by the repo test runner. | The tree's [shared-server-adapter-contract.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md) treats session resume as part of the adapter contract. Wiring the adapter's vitest project into the monorepo's test runner matches the monorepo pnpm-workspace convention in the root CLAUDE.md. | ✅ Aligned |

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md) — Session resume is part of the shared adapter contract.

</details>

No concerns. The new commit (f787c91b) is a test-runner plumbing change — adds a local vitest.config.ts for the adapter and includes the package in the root workspace projects — so the regression test for the issue-strategy fallback is picked up by CI. The fix itself is unchanged from the prior review: preserves the intent of issue strategy (isolation) in the missing-issueId edge case by degrading to run-level isolation instead of the global shared session. Narrow (4 files, +25/-2), with regression coverage now wired into the test runner.

PR #3785 fix(server): suppress blocked issue status wakeups

1 other reply (not engaging with gardener)
xkonjin · 2026-04-16T04:06:11Z

Root cause — Passing a JS Date object directly into a Drizzle/sql template literal can cause driver-specific serialization issues (e.g., inconsistent timezone handling). Serializing to an ISO string and explicitly casting with ::timestamptz is a clean, safe fix.

Suggestion — The local anchorCreatedAt string is constructed right before the sql block, which is readable. Consider extracting this cursor-pagination pattern into a small helper if it is repeated elsewhere in the issue service, so the timestamp serialization is always consistent.

No blockers. LGTM.

(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: a362f6bf

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Multi-user / invite flows | Real operator identities, company memberships, invite + profile surfaces, and company-scoped access controls for multiple humans | product/company-model/NODE.md: "Single-Tenant Deployment, Multi-Company Data Model. Paperclip is not a SaaS. One deployment = one human operator's companies." | ⚠️ Conflict (requires tree amendment) |
| Runtime modes | Extends authenticated mode to cover shared, multi-operator use with invite auth clarity + pagination + dedupe | product/NODE.md #8: two modes local_trusted (no login) and authenticated (session-based); still framed as one operator's deployment | ❓ Partial |
| Governance boundary | Invite links gated from members, invite history paginated, inbox approvals fixed for new users | product/governance/NODE.md: Board as the human control surface; doesn't anticipate multi-operator RBAC within one deployment | ❓ Partial |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Key Product Decisions #2 (company = unit of org) and #8 (two runtime modes).
- [product/company-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/company-model/NODE.md) — "Single-Tenant Deployment, Multi-Company Data Model" decision is unchanged in the current tree.
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board as human control surface.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: Same structural concern as the previous review still stands on the re-review commit (a362f6bf, "fix(pr): restore upstream dev and skill behavior"). Recent commits on this branch (Prevent members from consuming invite links, feat: paginate company invite history, Improve invite auth clarity, Deduplicate human join requests, Fix inbox approvals for new users) are excellent invite-flow hardening, but they operate inside the new multi-operator shape that the tree does not yet describe. The single-tenant decision in product/company-model/NODE.md is unchanged on the tree, so merging this would land a direction change as implementation detail.

Suggested path forward: Before merge, open a tree PR that either (a) amends product/company-model/NODE.md and product/NODE.md #8 to describe the move from single-tenant to collaborative multi-operator (with explicit scope: still "one org's operators", not generic SaaS multi-tenancy), or (b) keeps the feature behind a deployment-mode flag so local_trusted and the existing single-operator mental model stay unchanged. Either path, get product-owner sign-off recorded on the tree so future agents can answer "is shared-operator deployment in-scope?" without re-deriving it from this PR.

---

Engaging with gardener (1)

greptile-apps[bot] · 2026-04-16T03:15:33Z addresses-review

<h3>Greptile Summary</h3>

This is a large branch-consolidation PR implementing multi-user access for Paperclip: authenticated board sessions, invite flows, company membership management, profile settings, join-request deduplication, and tightened authorization across plugins, adapters, and workspace routes. All critical issues from previous review rounds appear addressed — the profile-image silent-clear bug, non-atomic role+grants update, last-owner race condition, data: URI validation bypass, DB-level join-request uniqueness, and plugin lifecycle authorization are all correctly fixed.

Remaining findings are P2: the owner-guard transaction logic is duplicated across four places (two route handlers and two service functions that are never called from routes), the avatar upload in ProfileSettings silently requires a selected company context, and a minor React useMemo deps gap in Auth.tsx. Per CONTRIBUTING.md, this PR has UI changes across invite landing, company access, profile settings, sidebar, and account menu — please provide before/after screenshots before merging.

<h3>Confidence Score: 5/5</h3>

Safe to merge once screenshots are provided; all previous P0/P1 issues are resolved and only P2 style/maintenance findings remain.

Every critical concern from prior review threads (atomic member updates, last-owner protection, data: URI validation, DB-level join-request uniqueness, plugin admin authz) is correctly addressed. The three new findings are P2: code duplication that does not introduce a current bug, a UX limitation in profile avatar upload, and a minor hooks lint issue. No data-loss or security regressions were found.

server/src/services/access.ts and server/src/routes/access.ts — the new service functions updateMember/updateMemberAndPermissions should replace the duplicated inline transaction logic in the two PATCH route handlers.

<h3>Important Files Changed</h3>


| Filename | Overview |
|----------|----------|
| server/src/routes/authz.ts | Adds hasBoardOrgAccess/assertBoardOrgAccess and extends assertCompanyAccess with viewer/status enforcement; instance-admin bypass on the outer membership check is intentionally removed, consistent with the multi-user design. |
| server/src/services/access.ts | Adds updateMember and updateMemberAndPermissions with correct FOR UPDATE lock and last-owner guard; both functions are exported and tested but not called from any route handler — route handlers re-implement the same inline transaction logic, creating four copies of the guard. |
| server/src/routes/access.ts | Large addition of invite listing, company-member CRUD, user-directory, and admin-user endpoints; PATCH /members/:memberId and PATCH /…/role-and-grants both duplicate the owner-guard transaction inline rather than calling the service-layer functions. |
| server/src/middleware/auth.ts | Extends board actor with userName, userEmail, and full memberships array (role + status) for all auth paths; well-structured and consistent across session, API-key, and local-implicit sources. |
| packages/shared/src/validators/access.ts | Adds profile-image validation that correctly restricts to http:/https: and asset paths (fixes prior data: URI issue); updateCurrentUserProfileSchema correctly uses optional transform to preserve image on name-only updates. |
| packages/db/src/migrations/0057_tidy_join_requests.sql | Cleans up duplicate pending-human join requests via ranked CTEs and adds partial unique indexes on (company_id, requesting_user_id) and (company_id, lower(request_email_snapshot)), providing DB-level uniqueness enforcement. |
| server/src/routes/auth.ts | Profile PATCH now uses conditional spread to avoid clearing image on name-only updates; GET-session and GET/PATCH profile are clean. |
| server/src/routes/plugins.ts | Destructive lifecycle mutations (delete, enable, disable, config) now require assertInstanceAdmin; read/data endpoints use assertBoardOrgAccess — addresses prior concern about under-restricted plugin management. |
| server/src/lib/join-request-dedupe.ts | In-memory dedup helpers are now backed by DB-level partial unique indexes from the migration; correctly handles user-ID and email identity matching. |
| ui/src/pages/CompanyAccess.tsx | New company-member management page using single updateMemberAccess call to atomic /role-and-grants endpoint; correctly handles pending join requests and propagates the access capability flags. |
| ui/src/pages/InviteLanding.tsx | Adds human sign-in/sign-up flow on the invite landing page with branded company header, error-code mapping for common auth failures, and invite-memory persistence for post-login redirect. |
| ui/src/pages/ProfileSettings.tsx | New profile settings page; avatar upload correctly ties to company file storage but silently requires a selected company context, which may surprise users without an active membership. |
| tests/e2e/multi-user-authenticated.spec.ts | New E2E suite covering multi-user authenticated flows; paired with separate playwright config for the authenticated mode. |
| packages/db/src/schema/join_requests.ts | Adds Drizzle-ORM partial unique indexes matching the SQL migration; correctly uses sql template for lower() expression index. |

</details>

<!-- greptile_other_comments_section -->

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix All With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: ui/src/pages/ProfileSettings.tsx
Line: 91-100

Comment:
Avatar upload silently requires a selected company

uploadAvatarMutation gates on !!selectedCompanyId, throwing "Select a company before uploading a profile avatar." if none is selected. A user who has just signed up via an invite but whose invite has not yet been approved — or who is an instance admin without any active company membership — sees no company in the picker and therefore cannot upload an avatar at all. The error only surfaces after they choose a file, which is a confusing failure point.

Consider either rendering the upload button as disabled (with a tooltip) when there is no company context, or allowing avatar upload without a company by using a user-scoped storage path.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.

---

This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: server/src/services/access.ts
Line: 97-190

Comment:
Service functions not used by route handlers — duplicated owner-guard logic

updateMember and updateMemberAndPermissions were added to accessService with the correct FOR UPDATE lock and last-owner check, but neither is called by the route handlers. Both PATCH /companies/:companyId/members/:memberId (lines 3619–3711 of access.ts) and PATCH /…/role-and-grants (lines 3713–3835) implement the identical transaction inline a third and fourth time. That means the same guard logic now lives in four places: any future fix or change must be made consistently across all of them.

The route handlers could delegate to these service functions and remove the duplicated inline transactions entirely.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.

---

This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: ui/src/pages/Auth.tsx
Line: 21-24

Comment:
getRememberedInvitePath() missing from useMemo dependency array

getRememberedInvitePath reads from localStorage but is not listed in the useMemo dependencies. In practice this is fine because localStorage changes do not trigger re-renders, so the value is effectively read once on mount. However, the lint rule react-hooks/exhaustive-deps will flag this, and a future refactor that wraps the read in a reactive state could silently break the fallback order.

Or extract the value to a const outside the memo so the dep is explicit.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````

</details>

xkonjin · 2026-04-16T04:06:39Z

This is a large feature PR with a lot of surface area. Here are a few focused notes on bugs, security, and coverage gaps:

1. Invite landing auto-accept race condition (InviteLanding.tsx)
The useEffect that triggers acceptMutation.mutate() depends on acceptMutation itself (an unstable object). This can cause the effect to re-fire more often than intended. Consider tracking a stable autoAcceptStarted ref or adding a stricter guard so the mutation fires exactly once per token.

2. Missing input validation on invite form
In CompanyInvites.tsx, the role radio buttons are client-side only. Make sure the backend createCompanyInvite endpoint validates humanRole against the allowed set and rejects unknown roles, otherwise a crafted request could create an invite with an invalid/missing role.

3. useMemo dependency on companyMembers?.users in IssueDetail.tsx
buildCompanyUserProfileMap and buildMarkdownMentionOptions are called inside useMemo with companyMembers?.users as the only dependency. If those utility functions are ever changed to be impure or to default something globally, this could leak. Not a bug now, but worth a quick audit.

4. Test coverage gaps
- JoinRequestQueue.tsx and InstanceAccess.tsx do not appear to have corresponding test files in this diff. Given they handle approve/reject mutations and admin promotion/demotion, unit tests for the error states (403, empty lists, mutation failures) would add good hardening.
- CompanyAccess.tsx is similarly untested in this changeset.

5. Clipboard fallback
In CompanyInvites.tsx, copyInviteUrl falls back to a toast when navigator.clipboard is unavailable. That is good UX, but verify that the invite URL is also visually selectable in the UI so the user can still copy it manually.

Overall this is a well-structured changeset with good fixture/test coverage for the landing flows. The main follow-ups are backend validation and tests for the queue/admin pages.

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |

| Approval workflow | Extends requester wakeup to reject and request-revision handlers, completing the feedback loop for all approval decisions | Governance: "approval workflow is synchronous — agents block until approval is granted or denied"; Board can approve/reject/cancel approvals | ✅ Aligned |
