PR #3771 Skip timer heartbeat for reactive agents with empty inbox
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @michaelkd01 · 2026-04-16T02:13:29Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: f8567c0

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Heartbeat optimization | Adds heartbeatMode (reactive/proactive) config; skips adapter invocation for reactive agents with empty inbox on timer wakes | "Heartbeat is a protocol, not a runtime. Paperclip controls when to fire" (agent-model/NODE.md). Server-side inbox pre-check is within orchestration scope. | ✅ Aligned |
| Cost control | Eliminates wasted token spend on empty-inbox timer wakes (hundreds of thousands of tokens/day saved) | "Budget Hard-Stops" and cost tracking at every level (governance/NODE.md) — reducing waste aligns with cost-conscious design | ✅ Aligned |
| Agent mode config | New per-agent mode field in heartbeat config (reactive default, proactive for CEO/coordinator) | Configurable per agent between fat/thin payload poles (agent-model/NODE.md: Context Delivery Spectrum) | ✅ Aligned |
| Zombie process cleanup | Adds launchd plist and kill script for orphaned Claude processes | Infrastructure/operational concern (infrastructure/NODE.md) | ✅ Aligned |

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Heartbeat Protocol, Context Delivery Spectrum
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Budget Hard-Stops, Cost Tracking
- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Adapter execution context

</details>

No concerns. The server-side inbox pre-check is a smart optimization that stays within the heartbeat protocol's orchestration scope — Paperclip controls "when to fire" and deciding not to fire when the inbox is empty is a natural extension. The 3k+ line count is mostly agent instruction config files, not new core logic.

---

NEEDS_REVIEW medium closed AUTHOR WITHDRAWN by @sparkeros · 2026-04-16T02:10:03Z → gardener comment 1 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: eb3647e

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Agent self-assignment | Adds proactiveAssignment toggle + proactiveAssignmentScope selector so agents scan for and claim unassigned work during heartbeat | "Single-Assignee Model: each task has at most one assignee. Atomic Task Checkout prevents conflicts at design level." (task-system/NODE.md) — the checkout model supports this, but proactive claiming changes the agent autonomy model | ❓ Partial |
| Agent model autonomy | Agents can now autonomously decide to pick up work without explicit delegation | "Cross-Team Coordination via Tasks" (agent-model/NODE.md) describes task acceptance rules but assumes tasks are explicitly assigned. Self-assignment is a new pattern. | 🆕 New |
| Heartbeat protocol | Extends heartbeat policy with new config fields passed to runtime | Heartbeat protocol is well-documented (agent-model/NODE.md) and extensible by design | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Single-Assignee Model, Atomic Task Checkout
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Cross-Team Coordination, Heartbeat Protocol
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Decision #5: Unopinionated about agent implementation

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree documents explicit delegation as the task assignment model ("Delegation = creating a task and assigning it"). Proactive self-assignment introduces a new pattern where agents autonomously claim work from backlog/todo queues. While the atomic checkout mechanism supports safe claiming, this changes the coordination model — an agent scanning and grabbing work is fundamentally different from being assigned work by a manager or human. The opt-in toggle (default: off) mitigates risk, but the pattern itself is undocumented.

Suggested path forward: This is a reasonable feature that the opt-in default handles well. Consider adding a brief section under product/agent-model/NODE.md documenting proactive self-assignment as a new capability pattern — when it fires, what scope it considers, and how it interacts with the delegation model. This helps future contributors understand the two task acquisition paths (explicit delegation vs. proactive claiming).

---

PR #3774 feat(issue-relations): auto-transition blocked dependents to todo on blocker done
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open PENDING by @sparkeros · 2026-04-16T02:08:44Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: f52df35

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Automatic state transitions | When a blocker issue reaches done, automatically transitions blocked dependents from blocked to todo | "No Automatic Recovery" (task-system/NODE.md): "Paperclip does not auto-reassign or auto-release the task... Recovery is manual/explicit" | ❓ Partial |
| Problem surfacing | Logs issue.auto_unblocked activity for each transition; wakeups are still sent to assignees | "Auditable everything" (governance/NODE.md) | ✅ Aligned |
| Issue relation types | Extends relation service with autoTransitionBlockedDependents() building on the new relation types | Open Question: dependency tracking (task-system/NODE.md) | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — "No Automatic Recovery" decision and "Blocking/dependency tracking" open question
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Decision #6: "Surface problems, don't hide them"
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Auditable everything

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The "No Automatic Recovery" decision in the tree is scoped to agent crashes (stale in_progress tasks), not to dependency resolution. Auto-transitioning a blocked issue to todo when its sole blocker completes is arguably a different pattern — it is deterministic (all blockers resolved = no longer blocked) rather than speculative (agent crashed = maybe retry?). However, the tree does not explicitly distinguish these cases, so the fit is ambiguous. The activity logging (issue.auto_unblocked) and wakeup behavior are well-aligned.

Suggested path forward: This is a reasonable extension that the tree does not clearly prohibit or endorse. If the maintainers agree this is the right behavior, update product/task-system/NODE.md to clarify that "No Automatic Recovery" applies to agent crash recovery (status reset), not to deterministic dependency resolution (blocked-to-todo when blockers complete). This would resolve the ambiguity for future contributors. Alternatively, make the auto-transition opt-in per company/routine if the team wants conservative defaults.

---

(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: 6845dbd

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Issue relation types | Extends ISSUE_RELATION_TYPES from ["blocks"] to ["blocks", "blocked_by", "related", "duplicate"] with full REST CRUD and bidirectional inverse edges | Tree lists "Blocking/dependency tracking between issues for agent coordination" as an Open Question (task-system/NODE.md). No decision exists yet for richer relation types. | 🆕 New |
| Company-scoped isolation | Relations enforce same-company constraint, use companyId scoping, activity-logged | All entities company-scoped (product/NODE.md decision #2) | ✅ Aligned |
| Audit trail | All relation create/delete operations logged via logActivity | "Auditable everything" (governance/NODE.md) | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Open Question: "Blocking/dependency tracking between issues for agent coordination"
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Decision #2: Company-scoped isolation
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Auditable everything principle

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree explicitly lists issue dependency/blocking tracking as an open question that has not been decided yet. This PR answers that question by extending the relation model to four types (blocks, blocked_by, related, duplicate) with full REST endpoints and bidirectional edge maintenance. The implementation respects existing constraints (company scoping, audit logging), but since no tree decision covers the relation type taxonomy or the bidirectional-edge invariant, this is new territory.

Suggested path forward: Consider adding a decision record under product/task-system/ documenting the relation type taxonomy (blocks/blocked_by/related/duplicate), the bidirectional-inverse-edge pattern, and the rationale for these specific types. This would resolve the listed open question and give future contributors a reference. The PR itself follows existing patterns well.

PR #3769 feat: notify human via Discord/Telegram when agent is blocked or needs help
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open PENDING by @sparkeros · 2026-04-16T02:07:50Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: f4e20af

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Core vs plugin boundary | Adds Discord and Telegram notification dispatch directly into activity-log.ts in the server core | "Knowledge bases, external revenue tracking, rich chat, and special surfaces go into plugins — not the core control plane." (product/NODE.md decision #9: Thin core, rich edges) | ❓ Partial |
| Problem surfacing | Notifies human when agent is blocked or comments with help keywords | "Surface problems, don't hide them" (product/NODE.md decision #6) — aligns with the spirit of making problems visible | ✅ Aligned |
| Agent model | Integrates into activity log fire-and-forget; no new agent or governance concepts introduced | Agent model and governance unchanged | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Decision #9: "Thin core, rich edges" and Decision #6: "Surface problems, don't hide them"
- [plugins/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Plugin system architecture: event subscriptions, webhook endpoints, capability-gated access
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board notification patterns

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The "thin core, rich edges" principle (decision #9) explicitly lists "special surfaces" as plugin territory, and external messaging integrations (Discord, Telegram) are a textbook example. The plugin system already supports event subscriptions and webhook endpoints — a notification plugin could subscribe to issue.updated and issue.comment_added events and dispatch to any channel. Baking specific third-party integrations into the core server couples the control plane to external services it should not know about.

Suggested path forward: Implement this as a plugin using the plugin SDK's event subscription system. The plugin would listen for issue.updated (status=blocked) and issue.comment_added events, apply the keyword matching logic, and dispatch to Discord/Telegram. This keeps the core thin while delivering the same user value. If the plugin SDK lacks something needed (e.g., access to config env vars), that gap is worth fixing in the SDK rather than bypassing it. See plugins/NODE.md for the event bus architecture and plugins/examples/NODE.md for reference implementations.

---

PR #3776 fix: auto-recover agents from error status and harden opencode adapter
CONFLICT high open PENDING by @tommepham · 2026-04-16T02:07:10Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ⚠️ CONFLICT · severity: high · commit: 3954424

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Agent error recovery | Auto-resets agents from error to idle after 5 min, from running to idle after 10 min with no active runs | "When an agent crashes mid-task, Paperclip does not auto-reassign or auto-release the task. Stale tasks are surfaced through dashboards. Recovery is manual/explicit." (task-system/NODE.md) | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Problem surfacing | Silent auto-recovery on a 2-min sweep interval; errors cleared before operator may see them | "Surface problems, don't hide them. No silent auto-recovery, no automatic reassignment." (product/NODE.md decision #6) | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Adapter hardening | opencode-local: default timeout 900s, deny doom_loop and question permissions | Adapter config is adapter-owned (adapters/NODE.md); sensible defaults are within adapter scope | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — "No Automatic Recovery" decision: stale/error state is surfaced, not auto-fixed
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Decision #6: "Surface problems, don't hide them"
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Agent statuses: active, paused, idle, running, error, terminated
- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Adapter-defined config and runtime behavior

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree explicitly documents "No Automatic Recovery" as a load-bearing design decision. The rationale states: "Automatic recovery hides failures. Good visibility lets the right entity decide what to do." Auto-resetting agents from error to idle on a silent 2-minute sweep contradicts this directly — an operator may never see that an agent errored, because by the time they check the dashboard it has been quietly reset. The opencode adapter hardening portion is fine (adapter-owned config).

Suggested path forward: Split this PR: land the opencode adapter hardening (timeout + permission defaults) separately since it aligns with adapter-owned config. For the auto-recovery service, consider making it opt-in via an instance setting (e.g. autoRecoverStalledAgents: true) that defaults to off, so the default behavior remains "surface problems, don't hide them." Alternatively, surface a high-priority activity event or board notification before resetting — that way the operator sees the error even if it auto-recovers. If the team decides auto-recovery should be default-on, update product/task-system/NODE.md to reflect that decision.

---

Issue #3757 UI Overhaul: Elevate Paperclip UI to production-grade design system
NEW_TERRITORY medium closed by @sundaraselvaraj · 2026-04-15T21:04:44Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: n/a

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Motion and animation system | Proposes comprehensive motion tokens, entrance animations, page transitions, spring-based animations, breathing glow effects | Tree documents Tailwind v4 and shadcn/ui as the styling foundation but has no guidance on motion design, animation tokens, or transition patterns | 🆕 New |
| Typography system | Proposes custom font (Inter Variable with OpenType features), specific type scale (13px workhorse), weight/tracking rules | Tree says "Tailwind v4" for styling but is silent on typography decisions, font choices, or type scale | 🆕 New |
| UI component polish scope | Proposes extensive component-level redesigns across MetricCard, StatusBadge, Sidebar, FilterBar, and more | Tree documents page structure and architecture patterns but not component-level visual specifications | 🆕 New |

- [engineering/frontend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — Checked stack decisions (Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, React 19), page structure, key decisions (no global state library)

### Recommendation

Why: The context tree has no documented design system decisions — no motion language, typography standards, or component-level visual specs. This proposal introduces a comprehensive design system that would become foundational for all future UI work, but the tree currently has no node to evaluate it against or guide its direction.

Suggested path forward: Before implementing, consider adding a tree node under engineering/frontend/ (e.g., design-system.md) that documents the chosen motion tokens, typography scale, and component patterns. This ensures future UI PRs can be evaluated for consistency against a documented standard rather than each contributor making ad-hoc decisions. Note: this issue overlaps significantly with #3756 — consolidating scope between the two would prevent duplicate/conflicting work.

---

v-karbovnichy · 2026-04-15T20:32:11Z

(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · low

Summary: Fixes the opencode-local adapter to resolve config paths using APPDATA on Windows instead of assuming Unix-style paths. Also cleans up config path resolution logic.

Tree context:
- adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md — OpenCode CLI adapter. Platform-specific path handling is an adapter responsibility.

Observations:
1. Changes from 46 deleted lines to 61 added lines — simplifies path resolution while adding Windows support.
2. Uses process.env.APPDATA on Windows, XDG_CONFIG_HOME on Linux, and ~/Library/Application Support on macOS.
3. Scoped to the opencode-local adapter only — no cross-adapter impact.

Verdict: Platform compatibility fix for the adapter layer. No tree impact.

PR #3718 fix(db): use calendar month retention for backups
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-15T20:02:18Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · low

Summary: Fixes backup monthly retention to use actual calendar months instead of a fixed 30-day approximation. Prevents premature deletion of backups in months with 31 days. Uses UTC-based date functions for consistency. Includes a test.

Tree context:
- infrastructure/NODE.md — Operational reliability. Backup retention is an infrastructure concern.
- engineering/database/NODE.md — Database backup library in packages/db/src/backup-lib.ts.

Observations:
1. Clean fix: replaces now - 30 24 60 60 1000 * months with proper calendar month subtraction using Date.setUTCMonth().
2. Also switches monthKey() from local to UTC methods for consistency.
3. Test coverage uses time mocking to verify calendar-month boundary behavior.

Verdict: Correct bug fix with test coverage. Addresses issue #3713. No tree impact.

> tree: 88dabbce · [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree)

ALIGNED low open PENDING by @stubbi · 2026-04-15T20:02:00Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · low

Summary: Fixes the issue identifier regex to accept prefixes with digits (e.g., P2P-42, V2-100). Changes ^[A-Z]+-\d+$ to ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]*-\d+$ across 4 files.

Tree context:
- product/task-system/NODE.md — Issues use human-readable identifiers like PREFIX-NUMBER.

Observations:
1. Consistent fix across all 4 locations where the regex appears (activity, agents, issues routes, issues service).
2. Minimal change (5 additions, 5 deletions). Each regex updated identically.
3. First character must still be a letter ([A-Z]), so pure-numeric prefixes are still rejected, which is correct.

Verdict: Straightforward bug fix. No tree impact.

PR #3729 feat: support TRUST_PROXY env var for correct client IP behind proxies
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @stubbi · 2026-04-15T20:01:41Z → gardener comment
(3 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · low

Summary: Adds support for TRUST_PROXY env var so Express correctly resolves client IPs from X-Forwarded-For behind reverse proxies. Without this, rate limiting and audit logs group all users behind a proxy into a single IP.

Tree context:
- infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md — Most deployments sit behind a reverse proxy. This is a standard operational requirement.
- engineering/backend/NODE.md — Express app configuration in server/src/app.ts.

Observations:
1. Single-file change (31 lines) in server/src/app.ts.
2. Accepts standard Express trust proxy values: boolean, number (hop count), comma-separated CIDRs, or "loopback"/"linklocal"/"uniquelocal".
3. Well-commented with security implications of each value type.

Verdict: Standard infrastructure fix for proxy-aware deployments. No tree impact.

> tree: 88dabbce · [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree)

PR #3735 feat(server): opt-in OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @stubbi · 2026-04-15T20:01:25Z → gardener comment
(4 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · low

Summary: Adds opt-in OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation for the server. Activated only when OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set; otherwise no OTel packages are loaded. Imports are dynamic and packages are optional runtime dependencies.

Tree context:
- infrastructure/NODE.md — Operational concerns. OTel is a standard observability approach.
- engineering/backend/NODE.md — The instrumentation module is imported at the top of server/src/index.ts before any other modules, which is required for auto-instrumentation to patch HTTP/Express/PG.

Observations:
1. Zero-cost when disabled — packages are loaded dynamically only when the env var is set.
2. Correctly placed as the first import in server/src/index.ts to ensure patching happens before module initialization.
3. Minimal footprint (2 files, 99 lines). Self-hosters opt in by installing OTel packages and setting one env var.

Verdict: Clean opt-in instrumentation that follows best practices for OTel integration. No tree impact for default deployments.

> tree: 88dabbce · [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree)

PR #3736 feat: optional Redis pub/sub for cross-replica live events
NEW_TERRITORY medium open PENDING by @stubbi · 2026-04-15T20:01:16Z → gardener comment
(4 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🟡 NEW_TERRITORY · medium

Summary: Adds optional Redis pub/sub for cross-replica live event distribution. When PAPERCLIP_LIVE_EVENTS_REDIS_URL is set, events are published to Redis so WebSocket clients on other replicas see them. Falls back to in-memory EventEmitter (unchanged behavior) when unset.

Tree context:
- engineering/backend/NODE.md — WebSocket-based live events in server/src/realtime/live-events-ws.ts. This PR modifies the underlying event distribution layer.
- infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md — Docker deployment. Multi-replica support is an infrastructure concern.

Observations:
1. New infrastructure dependency (Redis). The tree doesn't mention Redis anywhere. This introduces an optional but significant new dependency for multi-replica deployments.
2. Single-file change (128 lines) — contained entirely in server/src/services/live-events.ts. Clean implementation with origin-tagging to prevent echo loops.
3. Graceful degradation — no behavioral change when Redis is not configured. Good for backward compatibility.
4. No documentation or deployment guide updates. Self-hosters running multi-replica k8s deployments would need to know about this env var.

Verdict: NEW_TERRITORY — Redis as an optional infrastructure dependency is a new architectural decision that should be captured on the tree. The implementation is clean and backward-compatible.

> tree: 88dabbce · [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree)

PR #3739 fix(issues): block agent comments on completed issues from unrelated runs (OCT-552)
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @octasoft-ltd · 2026-04-15T20:01:05Z → gardener comment
(2 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · low

Summary: Blocks agents from commenting on completed (done/cancelled) issues when their active run targets a different issue. Prevents cross-contamination where an agent working on issue X accidentally posts to completed issue Y. Includes comprehensive test coverage.

Tree context:
- product/governance/NODE.md — Governance-first: audit trails and guardrails are core. Preventing stray agent comments on wrong issues strengthens the governance model.
- engineering/backend/NODE.md — Route/service split. The guard is implemented in the route layer where request context (actor, run) is available.

Observations:
1. Well-scoped fix: 258-line test file covering the exact scenario described in the issue.
2. Guard checks run ownership against the target issue, returning 403 when the agent's active run doesn't match.
3. Only applies to closed issues — agents can still comment on open issues they're not assigned to, which is the correct behavior for collaborative workflows.

Verdict: Clean governance improvement that aligns with the governance-first principle. Good test coverage.

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: c4d7046c

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Adapter architecture | Adds openrouter_local adapter following the standard three-layer package structure (server/cli/ui), using @paperclipai/adapter-utils | Adapters NODE.md: one package per adapter, compose from adapter-utils, declarative config schema | ✅ Aligned |
| Adapter type enum | Introduces openrouter_local as a new adapter type — a cloud API adapter, not a local CLI wrapper | Tree lists 7 adapters (claude-local, codex-local, cursor-local, gemini-local, openclaw-gateway, opencode-local, pi-local); openrouter is not documented | 🆕 New |
| Governance revert | Latest commit reverts CEO-only canCreateAgents change, keeping existing governance rules | Governance NODE.md: board-level controls are core; changes to who can create agents need board sign-off | ✅ Aligned |
| Dockerfile changes | Removes stale GPG checksum check, adds openrouter-local package.json to deps stage, reorders plugin-sdk before UI build | Infrastructure NODE.md: Dockerfile deps stage must list every workspace package.json | ✅ Aligned |
| Gemini model list | Updates gemini-local with newer model IDs (3.1 series) | Gemini adapter exists in tree but model list is an execution detail | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Adapter architecture, shared contract, one-package-per-adapter decision, local vs gateway pattern
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Key decision #5 (unopinionated about agent implementation), key decision #1 (control plane, not execution plane)
- [infrastructure/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/NODE.md) — Dockerfile deps stage validation requirement

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The OpenRouter adapter introduces a fundamentally new adapter pattern — a cloud API adapter that calls a model aggregation service directly via HTTP, rather than wrapping a local CLI or connecting to a gateway. This is architecturally sound (it follows the adapter contract) but represents a new category not documented in the context tree. The tree's adapters domain lists local CLI and gateway patterns only.

Suggested path forward: Add a tree node at adapters/openrouter-local/NODE.md documenting this adapter and the "cloud API" pattern it introduces. This helps future contributors understand that adapters can be direct API callers, not just CLI wrappers or gateway connectors.

---

PR #3751 add Ollama agent adapter for tool-calling models
NEW_TERRITORY medium open PENDING by @henrikrexed · 2026-04-15T19:59:59Z → gardener comment 1 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: 3178aee

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Adapter packaging | Adds server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts (647 lines) + registry entry; no dedicated package | adapters/NODE.md: "One package per adapter rather than a monolithic adapter module" — each adapter lives under packages/adapters/<name>/ with server/cli/ui layers | ⚠️ Conflict |
| ServerAdapterModule contract | Implements execute loop; unclear whether testEnvironment, sessionCodec, getConfigSchema, listSkills/syncSkills, getQuotaWindows are wired | Shared contract across all adapters; UI renders settings from declarative getConfigSchema so no frontend change is needed per adapter | ❓ Partial |
| Parallel adapter work | Overlaps with #3748 (OpenRouter) adding a new local adapter via the correct package layout | Tree expects consistency across adapters so CLI + UI layers pick up new runtimes automatically | ❓ Partial |
| Local runtime fit | Ollama as a locally-hosted model runtime fits the existing *-local pattern conceptually | Local adapters spawn the agent CLI / client via adapter-utils helpers | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — One package per adapter; three-layer (server/cli/ui) structure; declarative config schema.
- [adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/shared-server-adapter-contract.md) — All runtimes plug into one server adapter contract.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: Ollama is a reasonable new runtime to support, but the adapter currently lives as a single file under server/src/adapters/ rather than a packages/adapters/ollama-local/ package with server/cli/ui layers. Recent commits (fix(security): replace hardcoded LAN IP with localhost, fix(ollama-adapter): address Greptile review — sandbox filesystem too…) address code-review concerns but don't change the structural fit. The one-package-per-adapter decision is explicit on the tree and every existing adapter follows it; diverging here creates drift (billing inference, CLI formatting, UI transcript rendering, quota windows) and makes future cross-adapter refactors harder.

Suggested path forward:
1. Restructure as packages/adapters/ollama-local/ with server/cli/ui subpackages following claude-local, codex-local, gemini-local as references.
2. Compose via @paperclipai/adapter-utils (runChildProcess/HTTP client, buildPaperclipEnv, session codec normalization) rather than bespoke plumbing.
3. Wire testEnvironment, getConfigSchema, and detectModel so the frontend renders Ollama settings and health status without custom UI code.
4. Coordinate with #3748 (OpenRouter) so both new adapters land with the same package layout.

---

PR #3752 Pr/observability plugin
NEW_TERRITORY medium open PENDING by @henrikrexed · 2026-04-15T19:59:44Z → gardener comment 1 engaging 1 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: 3406382

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Plugin extensibility | Full OpenTelemetry plugin: traces, metrics, logs, health scoring, governance via the plugin event bus | Plugin SDK + runtime is the declared extension surface for cross-cutting concerns like observability | 🆕 New |
| Plugin placement | Adds both packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability/ and a top-level plugins/paperclip-observability/ dir | plugins/examples/NODE.md: "Examples live in-tree, not in a separate repo." Canonical location is packages/plugins/examples/ via pnpm workspace | ⚠️ Conflict (dual location) |
| Capability manifest / JSON-RPC | Plugin worker + manifest declare events/jobs; host-worker split | plugins/runtime/NODE.md: process isolation via JSON-RPC; capability-gated RPC; manifest-driven contract | ✅ Aligned |
| Cross-PR coupling | Adds server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts (581 lines) that belongs to #3751, and depends on #3750's new plugin event types | Tree expects per-package isolation; mixing unrelated adapter code into a plugin PR muddies review & bisect | ⚠️ Conflict |
| New plugin event types | Relies on agent.run.started/finished/failed/cancelled, agent.delegation.created, cost_event.created | Not yet documented on the tree as a stable event contract | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [plugins/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — SDK / runtime / examples split; JSON-RPC host-worker protocol.
- [plugins/runtime/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/runtime/NODE.md) — PluginEventBus routes domain events to subscribed plugins; capability enforcement at host boundary.
- [plugins/examples/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/examples/NODE.md) — Examples live under packages/plugins/examples/ via pnpm workspace.
- [plugins/examples/examples-live-in-tree-as-sdk-contract-tests.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/examples/examples-live-in-tree-as-sdk-contract-tests.md) — Example plugins double as SDK contract tests.
- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — One package per adapter; ollama-adapter.ts belongs in its own package, not bundled with a plugin PR.

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The observability plugin itself is a strong fit for Paperclip's plugin extensibility principle — exactly the kind of cross-cutting concern plugins are meant to absorb. Two process concerns hold up a clean ALIGNED: (1) the plugin is mirrored under two directories (packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability and top-level plugins/paperclip-observability), which contradicts the "examples live under packages/plugins/examples/" decision and risks divergence; (2) the PR also contains the Ollama adapter file from #3751, coupling two unrelated changes. Latest commit (fix(observability): address Greptile review — drop hardcoded companyI…) addresses code-review feedback but not the structural concerns.

Suggested path forward:
1. Pick one canonical location — keep the full implementation under packages/plugins/paperclip-observability/ (not an "example") since it's a first-party production plugin, with a minimal packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability-example/ if you also want a tutorial version. Delete the top-level plugins/paperclip-observability/ tree.
2. Split out server/src/adapters/ollama-adapter.ts into its own PR (or rebase onto #3751 once that lands).
3. Consider splitting the 9k-line plugin into a core tracing plugin plus opt-in metric/health-score/governance extensions so the review and failure surface is smaller.
4. Once the plugin event types from #3750 stabilize, propose a tree node under plugins/runtime/ documenting the agent. and cost_event. event contract.

---

<sub>🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.</sub>

Engaging with gardener (1)

henrikrexed · 2026-04-16T12:08:55Z references-verdict

The verify and e2e jobs are failing at pnpm install --frozen-lockfile because pnpm-lock.yaml is out of sync with the new dependencies introduced in packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability/package.json.
This is a structural CI tension, not a defect in this PR. The repo has two conflicting constraints:

The policy job blocks any PR that modifies pnpm-lock.yaml (unless it targets chore/refresh-lockfile)
verify and e2e run pnpm install --frozen-lockfile, which fails when a package.json declares deps not yet in the lockfile

Any PR that introduces new workspace dependencies cannot satisfy both rules on a single feature branch.
Upstream precedent (e.g. [#3109](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/pull/3109)) resolves this in two steps:
* Merge the feature PR with the lockfile step red
* The scheduled [refresh-lockfile.yml](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/master/.github/workflows/refresh-lockfile.yml) workflow opens a follow-up PR on chore/refresh-lockfile that regenerates pnpm-lock.yaml and merges clean under the policy exemption

If you'd prefer to front-run this, i can open a companion chore/refresh-lockfile PR today containing only the regenerated lockfile entries for the two new packages (plugins/paperclip-observability, packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability) and their @opentelemetry/* deps . happy to go whichever route works for you.

1 other reply (not engaging with gardener)
henrikrexed · 2026-04-16T07:37:00Z

On the dual directories: packages/plugins/examples/plugin-observability/ is the minimal reference example kept in the monorepo as the documented "hello world" shape; plugins/paperclip-observability/ is the full-featured plugin we actually run. We can call this out explicitly in plugins/NODE.md as part of this PR so future contributors know which one is canonical. On scope: agreed it is large , splitting now would create a hard dependency graph (metrics and health scoring share the same event router and span map), so the plan is to land the core router + traces + logs here and peel the health-score module into its own package in a follow-up once the plugin APIs settle.

PR #3712 fix: capture rawBody for form-encoded requests and deduplicate plugin event delivery
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @riptex · 2026-04-15T03:04:19Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · rocket ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: 85d8a3a

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit
<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Plugin Event Bus | Deduplicates event delivery by eventId within a 10-second window | PluginEventBus routes domain events to subscribed plugins; tree doesn't prescribe dedup strategy | ✅ Implementation detail; fixes real duplicate-notification bug |
| Plugin Isolation | In-memory cache for dedup; doesn't affect process isolation | "One process per plugin"; capability enforcement; data-backed state | ✅ Cache is transient; acceptable for collision prevention |
| Webhook Body Handling | Adds express.urlencoded() with verify callback to capture rawBody | Backend middleware contract; no explicit tree guidance on form-encoded payloads | ✅ Enables Slack plugin HMAC verification; isolated to body parsing |
| eventId-less events | Events without eventId skip dedup and can still duplicate | Tree doesn't mandate eventId presence on all events | ⚠️ Documented gap; not a blocker but worth acknowledging |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [plugins/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — plugin SDK and runtime architecture
- [plugins/runtime/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/runtime/NODE.md) — event bus, lifecycle, worker isolation
- [engineering/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/NODE.md) — backend architecture and service patterns

</details>

### Summary

This PR fixes two real bugs that prevent the Slack plugin from working correctly:

1. Slack HMAC verification — express.json() was the only body parser, so form-encoded Slack slash commands never had rawBody populated. HMAC verification always failed. The fix adds express.urlencoded() with a verify callback alongside the JSON parser.

2. Plugin event duplication — When a plugin registers multiple ctx.events.on() subscriptions for the same event pattern (e.g., two issue.created handlers), the event bus delivered N copies of the same event to the worker. The fix deduplicates via an eventId-based Map with a 10-second sliding window, collapsing N deliveries into 1.

Both changes are pragmatic and localized:
- They don't affect plugin process isolation, capability enforcement, or data persistence (which the tree marks as core).
- The in-memory dedup cache is acceptable for transient collision prevention.
- The documented gap (eventId-less events can still duplicate) is acknowledged and not a blocker.

✅ Fit: This PR aligns with the plugin architecture as described in the tree. It fixes bugs at the boundary between the backend webhook handler and the plugin event bus without violating the architectural constraints (process isolation, capability gating, plugin-worker contract).

---
<sub>Reviewed commit: <code>85d8a3a</code> · Tree snapshot: <code>6e68dbe</code> · Commands: <code>@gardener re-review</code> · <code>@gardener pause</code> · <code>@gardener ignore</code></sub>

PR #3711 fix(openclaw-gateway): stop sending top-level paperclip payload
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-15T02:23:19Z → gardener comment 3 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · low · (re-review: new commits)

Summary: Fixes the OpenClaw gateway adapter to stop sending a top-level paperclip property in the gateway request payload, which was causing schema rejection. Restructures the wake payload to fold supplemental context into existing fields instead. Includes new integration tests with a mock WebSocket gateway.

Tree context:
- adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md — OpenClaw WebSocket gateway adapter. Request shape must match gateway schema.
- adapters/NODE.md — Gateway adapters implement a request/response protocol with challenge-based auth.

Observations (re-review):
1. Renames buildStandardPaperclipPayload to buildSupplementalPaperclipContext — clearer naming that reflects the reduced scope.
2. Adds test helpers (extractStructuredWakePayload) to validate wake payload structure in integration tests.
3. 5 files changed (240 additions, 76 deletions) with good test coverage across both adapter and heartbeat test suites.
4. Previous review was ALIGNED; new commits add tests and refine the payload restructuring. Verdict remains ALIGNED.

Verdict: Well-tested fix for a gateway schema compatibility issue. Aligns with the adapter contract.

> tree: 88dabbce · [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree)

3 other replies (not engaging with gardener)
LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-15T04:39:56Z

Follow-up pushed in 8f836e82.

This keeps the unsupported top-level paperclip field removed, but restores the missing Paperclip context through the existing message path instead of reviving the rejected request property. The updated test now verifies three things together: paperclip and text are absent at the top level, legal payload-template keys still pass through, and workspace/runtime/template hints still reach the agent via structured JSON in the wake message.

I also moved the integration case into its own describe("execute") block, hardened the WebSocket teardown, and updated the adapter doc so the stated contract matches the implementation.

LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-15T06:05:24Z

Follow-up pushed in ba50901f.

This keeps the runtime behavior unchanged and updates the remaining gateway/server regressions to assert the structured wake JSON carried in message, which is the supported contract after removing the rejected top-level paperclip field.

Local checks:
- pnpm --filter @paperclipai/server exec vitest run src/__tests__/openclaw-gateway-adapter.test.ts -t "runs connect -> agent -> agent.wait and forwards wake payload" --testTimeout=10000
- pnpm --filter @paperclipai/server exec tsc --noEmit

I could not get heartbeat-comment-wake-batching.test.ts to run in isolation locally because of a pre-existing import-time TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating 'z.string') from packages/shared/src/adapter-type.ts, so I am relying on the PR verify job for that suite.

INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT low open by @spritehc · 2026-04-15T00:56:12Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · ❔ INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT · severity: low · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-----------|---------------|-----|
| External data dependency | SocialData API credits exhausted, blocking KOL tweet ingestion | No tree guidance on external data sources, SocialData API, or KOL monitoring | ❔ Insufficient |
| Agent self-reporting | Agent (x-data-loader) reporting its own blocked state via automated issue | Agents surface problems as tasks/comments — aligns with "surface problems, don't hide them" principle | ✅ Aligned |
| Scope | Requests CTO action to top up third-party API credits | Paperclip is a control plane, not a delivery infrastructure manager — operational billing for external APIs is outside tree scope | ❔ Insufficient |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Anti-requirements: Paperclip is not a delivery infrastructure manager
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Agent lifecycle, minimum contract
- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Tasks as communication channel

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree has no context about x-data-loader, SocialData API, or KOL monitoring as part of Paperclip's core product. This appears to be an operational issue for a specific company running on Paperclip (external API billing), not a platform-level concern. Similar to #3701.

Suggested path forward: If this is a company-specific operational issue, it belongs in that company's task system rather than the platform repo. If x-data-loader is a core Paperclip component, the tree needs updating to document it and its external dependencies.

PR #3700 feat: UX improvements — auto in_progress, Approve button, kanban auto-assign
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open PENDING by @faizer1989 · 2026-04-15T00:51:12Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: 228f72a

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Issue status workflow | Auto-transitions issues to in_progress when heartbeat run starts executing | Fixed status set with automatic side effects on transitions; in_progress sets started_at | ✅ Aligned |
| Approve button | Adds "Approve" button in in_review state that posts "Approved" comment | Board has unrestricted access; tasks are the communication channel | ✅ Aligned |
| Kanban auto-assign | Auto-assigns first agent when dragging unassigned issue to todo | Single-assignee model; each task has at most one assignee | ❓ Partial |
| Status transitions from in_review | Auto-transitions in_review → in_progress when a new run starts | in_review is typically a human-facing state awaiting board input | ❓ Partial |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Issue status workflow, single-assignee model, automatic side effects
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board powers, approval gates
- [engineering/frontend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — React SPA patterns

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: Two areas need maintainer input: (1) Auto-transitioning from in_review back to in_progress on heartbeat run could bypass the human review gate — if an agent dispatches a new run while the board is reviewing, the issue silently leaves review state. The tree says "surface problems, don't hide them." (2) Kanban auto-assign picks agents[0] which is arbitrary rather than intentional — the tree's single-assignee model assumes deliberate ownership assignment.

Suggested path forward: Consider excluding in_review from the auto-transition allowlist if maintainers want review state to be sticky until human action. For kanban auto-assign, consider prompting the user to pick an agent or using a smarter default (e.g., the agent most recently active in that project).

---

<sub>Reviewed commit: <code>228f72a</code> · Tree snapshot: <code>6e68dbe</code> · Commands: <code>@gardener re-review</code> · <code>@gardener pause</code> · <code>@gardener ignore</code></sub>

| Company lifecycle | Adds permanent delete UI to Danger Zone alongside existing archive | Company lifecycle: active, paused, archived. Delete is a natural extension of lifecycle management | ✅ Aligned |
| Governance | Delete gated behind feature flag (companyDeletionEnabled) and board auth on backend | Board has unrestricted access; governance-first — destructive actions require explicit gates | ✅ Aligned |
| Company-scoped isolation | Deletes company and all its data; backend endpoint already exists | Company is the top-level boundary; cascading delete is consistent with company-scoping | ✅ Aligned |
| Frontend patterns | Confirmation dialog, mutation hook, feature-flag query, tests | React 19 SPA patterns with react-query | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/company-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/company-model/NODE.md) — Company lifecycle (active/paused/archived) and company-scoped isolation
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board powers, governance-first design
- [engineering/frontend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — React SPA architecture

</details>

No concerns. Feature-flagged company deletion UI with confirmation dialog fits the governance-first, company-as-boundary model — the backend already supported it.

---

| Adapter contract | Issue targets execute.ts in openclaw-gateway adapter | Each adapter package owns its own execution logic; gateway protocol uses typed frames | ✅ Aligned |

ALIGNED low open PENDING by @cpfarhood · 2026-04-15T00:06:15Z → gardener comment 1 engaging 1 other
(3 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: 2637b78

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Plugin event system | Adds agent.tool.pre_execute and agent.tool.post_execute events | Plugin runtime uses event-driven coordination; PluginEventBus routes domain events to subscribed plugins | ✅ Aligned |
| Plugin tool dispatch | Wires event bus into tool registry and dispatcher | PluginToolRegistry/Dispatcher are documented host services that dispatch executeTool calls | ✅ Aligned |
| Fire-and-forget pattern | Events emitted with .catch(), never block tool execution | Plugin runtime isolates plugin failures from host; events are non-blocking by design | ✅ Aligned |
| Shared constants | Adds new event types to PLUGIN_EVENT_TYPES array | Shared package is the canonical location for cross-package constants | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [plugins/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Plugin architecture: event-driven coordination, capability-gated access
- [plugins/runtime/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/runtime/NODE.md) — PluginEventBus, PluginToolRegistry, PluginToolDispatcher service descriptions
- [engineering/shared/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/shared/NODE.md) — Shared constants and types

</details>

No concerns. New tool execution events follow the established plugin event bus pattern and enable plugins to observe agent tool usage without blocking execution.

<sub>Reviewed commit: <code>2637b78</code> · Tree snapshot: <code>6e68dbe</code> · Commands: <code>@gardener re-review</code> · <code>@gardener pause</code> · <code>@gardener ignore</code></sub>

<sub>🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.</sub>

Engaging with gardener (1)

cpfarhood · 2026-04-15T22:31:07Z addresses-review

Thanks for the thorough review @xkonjin. Addressing each point:

1. actorId undefined guard — Fixed. actorId and actorType are now derived dynamically from runContext: falls back to runId or "unknown" when agentId is undefined, and actorType is "system" instead of hardcoded "agent" in that case. agentId in the payload is also null-safe.

2. withAdapterMutex comment — That's in PR #3490, not this PR. Will note for that branch.

3. --no-save removal — Also in PR #3490 scope. The change was intentional: --no-save was causing sibling plugin pruning on reinstall.

4. deleteBySource TOCTOU — That's in PRs #3237/#3351 scope. Acknowledged — the window is narrow and the consequence is a failed delete (not data loss), but a code comment would be honest.

5. Missing test coverage for pre/post_execute events — Fair point. Will add a unit test for the event emissions in a follow-up.

6. Unrelated Dockerfile changes — Agreed, those are fork-local and have been removed from this PR branch after rebase.

Branch has been rebased on current master with the actorId fix applied.

1 other reply (not engaging with gardener)
cpfarhood · 2026-04-16T19:42:32Z

@greptile give this another look

Issue #3626 How can a company work autonomously when being task driven only?
ALIGNED medium open by @ttomiczek · 2026-04-14T23:58:38Z → gardener comment 1 other

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · severity: medium · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

Asks about autonomous vs task-driven company operation. Touches the core heartbeat protocol design: Paperclip controls when/how to fire, not what the agent does. The OpenClaw adapter's goal-based heartbeat model is a valid pattern within the adapter abstraction.

---

| Area | This issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Checkout error semantics | Reports the issues_open_routine_execution_uq constraint violation surfacing as 500 instead of 409, which crashes agents | Task System declares atomic checkout must return a 409 conflict on contention, not a generic 500 | ✅ Aligned |
| Routine concurrency | Multiple routine runs racing each other is the expected contention path that the DB unique constraint enforces | Routines domain requires enforceable concurrency policies (skip/queue/cancel-previous); agents need a 409 to act on them | ✅ Aligned |

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — "Atomic Task Checkout": failed checkout returns 409
- [product/routines/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/routines/NODE.md) — Concurrency policies require a recoverable conflict signal

</details>

Linked fix: #3790 (also ALIGNED) narrows the checkout route to translate this specific constraint into a 409.

---

PR #3681 fix: preserve managed Codex auth and repo-root env loading
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @neerazz · 2026-04-14T23:56:09Z → gardener comment 1 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · low · (re-review: new commits)

Summary: Fixes Codex adapter to preserve managed auth (API key vs ChatGPT session) and repo-root .env loading. When OPENAI_API_KEY is present, the managed CODEX_HOME prefers API-key auth instead of inheriting a shared login session. Also fixes Gemini adapter stdout line parsing.

Tree context:
- adapters/codex-local/NODE.md — Codex CLI adapter. Managed CODEX_HOME and environment probes are core adapter responsibilities.
- adapters/NODE.md — Adapters handle lifecycle, workspace management, and billing inference. Environment configuration is adapter-side.

Observations (re-review):
1. 11 files changed (448 additions, 19 deletions) — mostly new tests covering auth mode selection and env loading.
2. Includes Gemini adapter fix for parsing stdout lines — minor cross-adapter improvement.
3. Updates server/src/config.ts for repo-root .env loading order.
4. Previous review was ALIGNED; new commits expand test coverage. Verdict remains ALIGNED.

Verdict: Adapter environment configuration fix with comprehensive test coverage. Follows established adapter patterns.

> tree: 88dabbce · [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree)

1 other reply (not engaging with gardener)
neerazz-parafin · 2026-04-15T17:03:51Z

Implemented the two Greptile follow-ups in 00e77c91.

- server/src/config.ts: findProjectRootEnvPath() now starts from process.cwd() instead of its parent, so a repo-root launch does not pick up an ancestor .env.
- server/src/__tests__/config-env-loading.test.ts: added a regression test that proves an ancestor-only env var stays unloaded when the current repo root already has its own .env.
- docs/adapters/codex-local.md: moved the OPENAI_API_KEY auth note into ## Managed \CODEX_HOME\` and restored the paragraph break before ## Manual Local CLI`.

Verified with:
- pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/config-env-loading.test.ts

ALIGNED low open PENDING by @Daradaal · 2026-04-14T23:56:07Z → gardener comment

### Context fit
<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Backend architecture | Adds server-side pagination (limit/offset) to heartbeat-runs list, new /stats and /latest-failed endpoints | Tree says routes are thin HTTP, services hold logic — new endpoints follow this pattern | ✅ |
| Database layer | New raw SQL DISTINCT ON query with LIMIT 500 for latestFailed, Drizzle query builder for paginated list and stats | Tree says Drizzle queries live in services; raw SQL is acceptable for Postgres-specific features | ✅ |
| Adapter abstraction | Converts parseClaudeStreamJson and parseOpenCodeJsonl from sync string-based to async stream-based parsing | Tree says adapters parse agent output into unified format — streaming is an implementation improvement, contract unchanged | ✅ |
| Company-scoped isolation | All new queries filter by companyId; assertCompanyAccess called in routes | Multi-company isolation preserved | ✅ |
| Agent execution lifecycle | CLI heartbeat-run command switches from unbounded list fetch to single-run GET by ID | Aligns with heartbeat execution lifecycle; reduces unnecessary data transfer | ✅ |
| Access control | Fixes accessService to skip membership gate for agent principals (agents use their own table with company FK) | Agents are not tracked in company_memberships — this is a correct access-model fix | ✅ |
| Feedback/telemetry | Converts feedback trace building from buffered string to streaming, adds RunLogStore.stream() | Implementation improvement to reduce memory; feedback architecture unchanged | ✅ |
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [engineering/database/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/database/NODE.md) — Drizzle ORM, heartbeat_runs schema, query-level isolation
- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Adapter parse contract, three-layer package structure
- [engineering/cli/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/cli/NODE.md) — CLI heartbeat-run command
</details>

No concerns. Comprehensive performance fix addressing unbounded heartbeat queries (referenced issues #958, #1846) with proper server-side pagination, streaming parsers, and memory-bounded trace building — all following existing architectural patterns.

PR #3694 Fix stale execution lock cleanup
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @TheConnMan · 2026-04-14T23:55:20Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · ✅ ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: c75b4555

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Task checkout consistency | Fixes stale execution locks so heartbeat finalizer reconciles all issues touched by a run, not just one | Atomic Task Checkout: claiming a task is an atomic DB operation; checkout integrity is a core invariant (product/task-system) | ✅ Aligned |
| Ownership recovery | Adds adoption path for terminal/missing execution locks while rejecting live queued/running locks | No Automatic Recovery: stale tasks are surfaced, recovery is explicit (product/task-system) | ✅ Aligned |
| Audit trail | Adds telemetry distinguishing stale checkout-run vs stale execution-run adoption | Surface problems, don't hide them: good auditing lets the right entity decide (product key decision #6) | ✅ Aligned |
| Deferred wake promotion | Broadens finalizer to promote deferred wakes per-issue after multi-issue runs | Heartbeat run lifecycle and control plane orchestration (product/agent-model) | ✅ Aligned |

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Atomic Task Checkout, Single-Assignee Model, No Automatic Recovery decisions
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Key Product Decisions #1 (control plane), #6 (surface problems)
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Agent lifecycle, heartbeat protocol

</details>

No concerns. Bug fix that strengthens execution lock consistency across multi-issue heartbeat runs, fully aligned with the atomic checkout and explicit recovery design decisions.

---

Issue #3630 creating a portable zip so we can run the paperclip from pendrive
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open by @37OMKAR · 2026-04-14T23:54:26Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: issue

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This issue | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|------------|---------------|-----|
| Distribution model | Requests portable ZIP distribution to run from USB drive | Infrastructure: Two deployment modes (local_trusted + authenticated); Docker images are the primary distribution | ❓ Partial |
| Local-first | Aligns with the "local-first" principle | Product: "Local-first, cloud-ready. The mental model should not change between local solo use and shared/cloud deployment" | ❓ Partial |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Local-first, cloud-ready principle
- [infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md) — Docker deployment, embedded Postgres for local dev

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: A portable ZIP distribution is not part of the current deployment model. Paperclip uses Docker images for production and pnpm/Node.js for development. A portable ZIP would need to bundle Node.js, embedded Postgres, and all agent CLIs — significant packaging complexity outside current scope. However, the local-first principle does suggest accessibility for solo operators.

Suggested path forward: This is likely out of scope for V1 priorities. The closest existing path for portable use is the Docker image with embedded Postgres in local_trusted mode. If there is demand, a self-contained binary (e.g., via pkg or sea) could be a future consideration.

---

<sub>Reviewed commit: <code>issue</code> · Tree snapshot: <code>6e68dbe</code> · Commands: <code>@gardener re-review</code> · <code>@gardener pause</code> · <code>@gardener ignore</code></sub>

PR #3648 fix: remove stale pinned sha256 for GitHub CLI keyring in Dockerfile
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open PENDING by @bobbyhyam · 2026-04-14T23:54:06Z → gardener comment
(2 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: 89295fe

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Docker security | Removes SHA256 checksum verification for GitHub CLI GPG keyring entirely | Infrastructure: Docker images pin dependencies; governance: auditable everything | ❓ Partial |
| Build fix | Unblocks Docker builds broken by stale checksum | Infrastructure: Docker build must work for deployment | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md) — Docker image base and deployment
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Security and audit trail

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This PR removes the SHA256 checksum verification line entirely rather than updating it to the correct hash. While this fixes the immediate build breakage, it removes a supply-chain security check. Compare with PR #3649 which updates the checksum to the correct value, maintaining the security verification.

Suggested path forward: Prefer #3649 (update checksum) over this approach (remove checksum). If the concern is future keyring rotations, consider fetching the checksum dynamically or documenting a maintenance process, rather than removing the verification.

---

PR #3655 fix: upgrade drizzle-orm to 0.45.2, 1.0.0-beta.20 (CVE-2026-39356)
NEEDS_REVIEW high closed CORRECT by @orbisai0security · 2026-04-14T23:53:42Z → gardener comment 1 other
(2 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: high · commit: 0f63014

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Security fix | Upgrades drizzle-orm to fix CVE-2026-39356 (SQL injection) | Governance: auditable everything; security is foundational | ✅ Aligned |
| Lockfile changes | Directly modifies pnpm-lock.yaml | Infrastructure: "The lockfile is CI-owned — PRs that manually edit pnpm-lock.yaml are rejected by policy" | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Drizzle version | Jumps from 0.38.4 to 0.45.2 (major version bump) | Engineering: Drizzle ORM is the DB layer across the entire backend | ❓ Partial |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [infrastructure/ci-cd/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/ci-cd/NODE.md) — CI/CD lockfile policy
- [engineering/database/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/database/NODE.md) — Drizzle ORM schema and migrations
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Security and audit requirements

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The security fix (CVE-2026-39356, SQL injection in drizzle-orm) is critical and should be prioritized. However, this PR directly edits pnpm-lock.yaml, which violates the CI lockfile policy. The version jump from 0.38.4 to 0.45.2 is also significant and could introduce breaking changes in the Drizzle API surface used throughout the codebase.

Suggested path forward: (1) Remove the pnpm-lock.yaml changes and let CI regenerate it per policy. (2) Verify the drizzle-orm 0.45.2 API is compatible with the existing schema and query patterns — a major version bump needs validation. (3) If API-compatible, this should be fast-tracked given the SQL injection severity.

---

<sub>Reviewed commit: <code>0f63014</code> · Tree snapshot: <code>6e68dbe</code> · Commands: <code>@gardener re-review</code> · <code>@gardener pause</code> · <code>@gardener ignore</code></sub>

(2 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: 041c053

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| New UI surface | Adds a Visibility tab showing real-time file edit grid per agent | Product: progressive disclosure (summary > steps > raw logs); no "Visibility tab" in scope | 🆕 New |
| Event pipeline | Adapters POST file.edit events through existing heartbeat.run.event pipeline | Engineering: event bus, heartbeat run events are established patterns | ✅ Aligned |
| Agent observability | Real-time view of what agents are editing as they work | Product: "Surface problems, don't hide them"; board-level visibility | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Progressive disclosure, board-level abstraction, surface problems

- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — Server event pipeline

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This adds a new top-level UI surface (Visibility tab) that provides real-time file-level observability into agent work. While not currently scoped in the tree, it aligns well with the "surface problems, don't hide them" and "board-level abstraction" principles. The implementation leverages existing event infrastructure rather than creating new plumbing.

Suggested path forward: This is a natural extension of the observability model. The main question is whether it belongs as a core UI tab or as a plugin-provided surface (the tree lists "knowledge bases, rich chat, and special surfaces" as plugin territory). If adopted as core, the product tree should document this as a new visibility surface.

---

NEW_TERRITORY medium open PENDING by @stakeswky · 2026-04-14T23:52:46Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: 67e40ec

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Internationalization | Complete i18n framework with I18nContext, useTranslation hook, 1,700+ zh-CN keys | No i18n decisions documented in tree | 🆕 New |
| Server-side locale | Adds locale setting to server API and persists per-user | Product: local-first, cloud-ready; no locale model documented | 🆕 New |
| UI framework | Custom lightweight i18n engine with interpolation and pluralization | Engineering: React 19 SPA | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Product strategy (no i18n mention)
- [engineering/frontend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — React 19 SPA architecture

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This is a large i18n PR (7.8K+ additions) with a full zh-CN locale and custom i18n engine. Like #3672, internationalization is not in the current product scope. This PR takes a different approach (custom lightweight engine vs #3672's i18next-based approach), creating a potential conflict if both are considered.

Suggested path forward: Same as #3672 — maintainers should make a product decision on i18n scope and approach before merging either PR. If i18n is adopted, choosing one consistent framework (custom vs i18next) across the codebase is important.
