🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: 26d9362

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Internationalization | Full i18n framework: locale infrastructure, createTranslator/resolveLocale in shared, DB-backed user preferences | No i18n decisions documented in tree; product is English-only currently | 🆕 New |
| Database schema | New user_preferences table with locale enum (en, zh-CN, ja-JP, es-ES, fr-FR, de-DE) | DB migrations via Drizzle ORM | 🆕 New |
| Shared package | Adds i18n.ts with translator factory and locale resolution | Shared package: cross-package types, constants, validators | ✅ Aligned |
| CLI changes | Replaces hardcoded strings with t() calls in board-auth flow | CLI: setup, diagnostics, worktree management | 🆕 New |
| Server middleware | Adds locale-aware request context | Express middleware patterns | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Product strategy (no i18n mention)
- [engineering/shared/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/shared/NODE.md) — Cross-package types and constants
- [engineering/cli/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/cli/NODE.md) — CLI architecture
- [engineering/database/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/database/NODE.md) — Drizzle ORM schema and migrations

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: i18n is not documented in the product tree as a V1 priority or a planned capability. This PR introduces a scalable locale infrastructure across 99 files spanning CLI, server, shared, and database layers. While the implementation pattern is clean (shared translator, DB preferences, middleware), the product decision to support multiple languages has not been captured as a strategic choice.

Suggested path forward: Maintainers should decide whether i18n is in scope for V1. If yes, a tree node documenting the locale strategy (supported languages, fallback behavior, where translations live) would help future contributors. The PR author rebuilt this as a reviewable first slice — the approach of starting with issue/activity flows and CLI is reasonable if i18n is accepted.

LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-15T14:53:04Z references-verdict

I refreshed this PR onto the current master again, and GitHub now shows it as clean and mergeable while still staying within the 99-file review limit.\n\nThe latest upstream changes are included, and the recent dev/watch updates did not introduce additional end-user UI copy that needed a wider i18n expansion in this slice.\n\nIf internationalization is a direction the team would consider, I am happy to keep maintaining and updating this PR line. If not, that is completely fine too. In that case, I would appreciate a quick signal and I will stop spending time on further rebases and conflict maintenance for this branch.

LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-16T20:04:43Z references-verdict

Re-checked this branch against the current latest master: it is up to date, conflict-free, and the latest PR checks are passing.\n\nThis PR is still scoped to the internationalization work only: locale infrastructure, translation catalogs, and UI text wiring. 2 other replies (not engaging with gardener)
LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-15T00:56:35Z

This PR is intended to present a merge-ready multilingual internationalization framework, not just a small two-locale copy change.

The architecture is already in place and follows a systematic best-practice approach across shared, server, UI, and CLI layers, so the localized surfaces included in this PR are immediately usable after merge.

Because the repository review tooling has a 100 changed file limit, I intentionally submitted this as a reviewable nano slice first so the team can evaluate and approve the framework direction without having to review the entire remaining localization rollout in one pass.

I plan to continue owning the follow-up work for the remaining untranslated surfaces after this slice is reviewed. I care about this area because many users around the world are interested in products like this, but a pure English-only experience creates avoidable usability barriers for people operating in non-native English environments.

My goal here is to help reduce that friction with a solid multilingual foundation first, then complete the rest of the rollout in follow-up PRs built on the same contract.

LeonSGP43 · 2026-04-15T14:59:26Z

Because of the 99-file review limit, I have only submitted the core internationalization framework in this PR for now. I still have follow-up commits prepared that are needed to bring the rollout to the full framework level across the remaining surfaces.\n\nI’m happy to continue owning and maintaining this part of the work in follow-up updates if the team would like to move forward with i18n.

(2 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: 44938f4

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Issue status enum | Adds parked to the fixed status set | Task system: "Fixed status set for V1: backlog, todo, in_progress, in_review, done, blocked, cancelled" | ❓ Partial |
| Status deduplication | Centralizes status arrays to ISSUE_STATUSES from @paperclipai/shared | Shared package: cross-package types and constants | ✅ Aligned |
| Execution constraint | Adds parked to the routine execution uniqueness check | Task system: atomic task checkout, single-assignee model | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Fixed V1 status set, open question about team-specific workflow states
- [engineering/shared/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/shared/NODE.md) — Cross-package constants

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: The tree explicitly documents a fixed V1 status set: backlog, todo, in_progress, in_review, done, blocked, cancelled. Adding parked extends this set, which is a product decision. The distinction from blocked (intentionally set aside vs. external dependency) is reasonable, but this touches a documented V1 boundary. The deduplication part (centralizing to shared constants) is clearly good.

Suggested path forward: Confirm with maintainers whether parked is an accepted V1 addition or should wait for the aspirational team-specific workflow states model. If accepted, the tree's task-system node should be updated to include parked in the status set.

PR #3693 feat: add NanoClaw Gateway adapter for WhatsApp agent orchestration
NEW_TERRITORY medium open PENDING by @Franky4Fing3rs · 2026-04-14T23:51:07Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🆕 NEW_TERRITORY · severity: medium · commit: e6c316a

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Adapter pattern | Follows ServerAdapterModule interface, three-layer package structure, registered in CLI/server/UI | Adapters: one package per adapter, shared interface, declarative config schema | ✅ Aligned |
| Gateway pattern | HTTP-based gateway (fire-and-forget POST to NanoClaw MCP server) | Gateway adapters connect via WebSocket with challenge auth (openclaw pattern) | 🆕 New |
| Response delivery | Responses go to WhatsApp, not back to Paperclip UI transcript | Adapters parse output into shared TranscriptEntry types; agents report status back | 🆕 New |
| Cost tracking | NanoClaw reports cost back to Paperclip via reportCostToPaperclip() | Billing inference is adapter-side; each adapter reports cost/billing info | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Adapter architecture and shared interface contract
- [product/agent-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Minimum agent contract: be callable

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: NanoClaw introduces a new adapter communication pattern: fire-and-forget HTTP (not WebSocket like openclaw-gateway) where agent responses are delivered via WhatsApp instead of back to Paperclip. This means the Paperclip UI will have no transcript visibility for these runs, which diverges from the progressive disclosure principle (top layer: summary, middle: steps, bottom: raw logs). The adapter otherwise follows the established pattern well.

Suggested path forward: This is valid new territory that extends Paperclip's adapter ecosystem. Consider documenting the "fire-and-forget" adapter category as a recognized pattern in the adapter architecture (alongside local and gateway), and clarify what visibility the operator gets when responses bypass the Paperclip transcript.

---

<sub>Reviewed commit: <code>e6c316a</code> · Tree snapshot: <code>6e68dbe</code> · Commands: <code>@gardener re-review</code> · <code>@gardener pause</code> · <code>@gardener ignore</code></sub>

NEEDS_REVIEW high closed AUTHOR WITHDRAWN by @cpfarhood · 2026-04-14T23:50:36Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · 🔍 NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: high · commit: 7b6af39

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Delete Company | Adds Delete Company button with confirmation dialog | Company lifecycle is a core product domain; deletion is a high-impact governance action | ❓ Partial |
| CI/CD pipeline | Renames docker.yml to build.yml, deletes release.yml, changes GHCR target org to farhoodliquor, uses personal runner | CI/CD is CI-owned; release pipeline is the shipping mechanism for all users | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Docker build | Removes multi-platform support (linux/amd64,linux/arm64), removes build caching | Infrastructure: Docker images target node:lts-trixie-slim with broad platform support | ⚠️ Conflict |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/company-model/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/company-model/NODE.md) — Company lifecycle decisions
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board powers include full company management
- [infrastructure/ci-cd/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/ci-cd/NODE.md) — CI/CD pipeline and release workflow
- [infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md) — Docker deployment topology

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: This PR bundles two unrelated changes. The Delete Company button is a reasonable feature (board has unrestricted access per governance tree), but the CI/CD changes are concerning: they rename the Docker workflow, delete the release workflow entirely, point GHCR images at farhoodliquor (not paperclipai), use a personal self-hosted runner (runners-farhoodliquor), and remove multi-platform and build cache support. These CI changes would break the project's release pipeline for all other contributors.

Suggested path forward: Split into two PRs: (1) the Delete Company UI feature, and (2) any CI/CD changes that should be discussed separately with maintainers. The CI/CD changes as-is appear fork-specific and should not merge to upstream.

---

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|

</details>

No concerns. The opt-in private IP allowance for self-hosted deployments maintains critical SSRF protections (loopback and cloud IMDS always blocked) while enabling homelab setups where plugin endpoints resolve to RFC-1918 addresses.

<sub>🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.</sub>

1 other reply (not engaging with gardener)
redhelix · 2026-04-14T15:32:46Z

Rebased onto current master and addressed the Greptile P1 security finding.

What changed in this force-push:

- Branch is now based on current upstream master (fixes the policy CI failure caused by branch divergence / pnpm-lock.yaml appearing in the two-dot diff)
- Added isAlwaysBlockedIP(): blocks loopback (127.x/::1) and link-local (169.254.x/fe80::) unconditionally — these include cloud IMDS endpoints (169.254.169.254 on AWS/GCP/Azure)
- When PLUGIN_ALLOW_PRIVATE_IPS=true, safeResults now filters through isAlwaysBlockedIP() instead of bypassing all guards — so RFC-1918 and IPv6 ULA are reachable but IMDS and localhost remain blocked
- Updated JSDoc on PLUGIN_ALLOW_PRIVATE_IPS to document the always-blocked caveat

This directly addresses the Greptile P1 comment: _"Consider keeping loopback and link-local always blocked and only relaxing RFC-1918 + IPv6 ULA."_

PR #3601 Fix mobile onboarding permissions and signup handoff
NEW_TERRITORY low open PENDING by @jonslimak · 2026-04-13T22:25:16Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener · NEW_TERRITORY · severity: low · commit: aa967986

> What is this? repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's product decisions, architecture, and roadmap — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit.

### Context fit

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|---------|---------------|-----|
| Mobile onboarding | Fixes permissions and signup handoff for mobile web handoff routes | No tree guidance on mobile client support or onboarding handoff flows | 🆕 New |
| Auth routes | Adjusts auth page behavior for mobile signup flow | Auth modes documented (local_trusted vs authenticated) but mobile-specific auth not covered | 🆕 New |
| Agent/company routes | Adds permission adjustments for mobile context | Route/service split pattern followed (engineering/backend/NODE.md) | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — Route/service split architecture
- [infrastructure/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/NODE.md) — Two deployment modes (local_trusted vs authenticated)
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Local-first, cloud-ready (decision #8)

</details>

### Recommendation

Why: Mobile client support is a new surface area not yet documented in the context tree. This is the second PR in this series (follows #3594) establishing mobile onboarding as an ongoing effort.

Suggested path forward: If mobile becomes a sustained effort, consider adding a tree node under product/ or engineering/frontend/ documenting mobile support scope and constraints. Low risk as-is — the code changes follow existing patterns.

---

Issue #3587 Use Linear.app as the Task Board
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open by @anaclumos · 2026-04-13T21:44:07Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium

> [!CAUTION]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

Proposes replacing Paperclip's built-in task system with Linear. The tree explicitly states tasks are the communication channel (product decision #3) and all work traces to the goal (task-system/NODE.md). Using an external tool as the primary board would change a core architectural assumption.

NEEDS_REVIEW medium open by @joshdutcher · 2026-04-13T21:44:05Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium

> [!CAUTION]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

Session rotation on config change touches the adapter session management model (adapters/NODE.md: session management). The tree says adapters with nativeContextManagement=confirmed handle their own context — forced rotation from the control plane may conflict with that.

cpfarhood · 2026-04-14T01:23:48Z

@greptile This is already fixed in the third commit in this PR (\8f02e66e\ — "Fix instructionsPathKey default and async capability cold-load regression"). The fix adds the \supportsInstructionsBundle\ check to \resolveInstructionsPathKey\ so external adapters with that flag default to \"instructionsFilePath"\ rather than \null\.

cpfarhood · 2026-04-14T10:36:58Z

@greptile This PR has been rebased on current master and squashed to a single clean commit. Please re-evaluate.

cpfarhood · 2026-04-14T10:49:34Z

@greptile Please re-evaluate. Fixes applied: added missing capabilities prop to AdapterManager.tsx (CI fix) and guarded resolveInstructionsPathKey against explicit supportsInstructionsBundle: false falling through to legacy map.

kbecking · 2026-04-15T20:51:52Z

## Test fixes + rebase

Pushed 1d1c60e6 fixing the remaining CI failures and rebased onto current master:

Test fixes:
- svc.create() called with wrong signature (single-object instead of (companyId, data) two-param) — fixed all 3 create calls
- tickScheduledIssues used raw sql template literal with Date object, causing postgres driver TypeError — replaced with Drizzle's lte() and isNotNull() helpers
- Added isNotNull(issues.scheduledFor) guard to prevent matching rows with NULL scheduledFor

Rebase:
- Rebased onto latest upstream master (7463479f)
- Renumbered migration from 0056 to 0057 (upstream added 0056_spooky_ultragirl since the PR was opened)

All tests pass locally (23/23 in issues-service.test.ts), typecheck clean.

Engaging with gardener (1)

qazsero · 2026-04-15T08:14:49Z references-verdict

## Staff review: changes requested

This is close, but I do not think it is safe for QA yet.

Blocking issues:

- server/src/services/routines.ts:925 queues the heartbeat wakeup before the run is finalized at server/src/services/routines.ts:934. If finalizeRun() or updateRoutineTouchedState() fails after the wakeup succeeds, the catch path deletes the execution issue at server/src/services/routines.ts:949, and the outer cleanup only deletes issue rows at server/src/services/routines.ts:995. The queued heartbeat_runs / agent_wakeup_requests rows can remain pointed at an issue that was deleted and no longer has a linked routine run. The rollback test at server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts:773 only covers the easier case where wakeup throws before wakeup artifacts commit, so this guardrail is still unproven.

- tickScheduledTriggers() advances routineTriggers.nextRunAt at server/src/services/routines.ts:1626 before dispatching at server/src/services/routines.ts:1644. If dispatch aborts after that claim, the scheduled occurrence is consumed with no run and no visible blocker. The existing rollback test asserts no run/issue remains, but does not assert trigger state. That still conflicts with the goal that automated visual routines cannot disappear silently.

Please either make those side effects atomic, or add compensating cleanup/retry behavior for both the wakeup artifacts and schedule claim, with regression coverage that forces the post-wakeup finalization failure path.

Verification I ran on a merge-result worktree against current origin/master:

- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile completed, with existing plugin bin warnings for unbuilt plugin SDK artifacts.
- pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts passed: 22 tests.
- git diff --check origin/master passed.

I did not run broader gates after finding the blocking review issue.

3 other replies (not engaging with gardener)
qazsero · 2026-04-15T08:41:15Z

## Staff Review

Not ready for QA yet. The latest patch still does not cover the production wakeup lifecycle for the post-wakeup finalization failure case.

Blocker:
- server/src/services/routines.ts cleanup only selects heartbeat_runs with status = 'queued' and only deletes wakeup requests with status in ('queued', 'coalesced').
- The real heartbeat.wakeup() path inserts the queued run, then immediately calls startNextQueuedRunForAgent() before returning. That can claim the run as running and mark the wakeup request claimed before dispatchRoutineRun() reaches finalizeRun().
- If finalizeRun() or touched-state update fails after wakeup returns, cleanupIssueWakeupArtifacts() can delete the routine execution issue while leaving a running heartbeat and claimed wakeup request pointed at the deleted issue. That is the same class of orphaned wakeup artifact the fix is meant to close.
- The new regression test uses a fake wakeup that leaves the run/request queued, so it does not exercise the production path where wakeup() starts the run before returning.

What I would expect before QA:
- Make the cleanup/failure path safe when the wakeup has already been claimed/running, or change dispatch ordering so the run cannot be claimed until routine run finalization is durable.
- Add regression coverage that uses the real heartbeat wakeup behavior or faithfully simulates queued -> running and queued -> claimed before post-wakeup finalization fails.

Verification I ran:
- pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts passed: 24 tests.
- pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project server/tsconfig.json passed.
- git diff --check origin/master...HEAD passed.

qazsero · 2026-04-15T08:58:16Z

## Staff Review

Not ready for QA yet. The latest patch improves the steady queued and steady running cases, but there is still a race in the cleanup path.

Blocking issue:

- cleanupIssueWakeupArtifacts() snapshots live runs, classifies queued runs, then later deletes the queued run ids without re-checking status. If a queued run is claimed between the select and the delete, the cleanup can delete a now-running heartbeat_runs row while leaving the wakeup request in claimed status. The follow-up wakeup delete only covers queued/coalesced, so the claimed agent_wakeup_requests row can survive pointing at an issue that the caller then deletes.
- Relevant flow: server/src/services/routines.ts:724 selects live runs, server/src/services/routines.ts:739 classifies queued/running, server/src/services/routines.ts:804 deletes queued run ids without a status predicate, and server/src/services/routines.ts:816 only deletes queued/coalesced wakeup requests. The issue row can then be deleted at server/src/services/routines.ts:1080 or server/src/services/routines.ts:844.
- The new tests cover stable queued and stable running artifacts, but not the queued-to-running race between cleanup snapshot and cleanup delete.

Expected before QA:

- Revalidate run status before deleting queued run ids, or handle all selected live runs through a cancellation path that remains safe if they are claimed mid-cleanup.
- Ensure claimed wakeup requests cannot survive for a transient routine issue that is deleted.
- Add regression coverage that forces a queued artifact to become running/claimed during cleanup before the issue deletion proceeds.

Verification I ran:

- pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts passed: 25 tests.
- pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project server/tsconfig.json passed.
- git diff --check origin/master...HEAD passed.

qazsero · 2026-04-15T09:18:57Z

## Staff Review

Approved for QA.

- The previous queued-to-running cleanup race is addressed: routine cleanup now reconciles live heartbeat runs over bounded passes, deletes only still-queued runs, cancels running/claimed runs through heartbeat.cancelRun(), and leaves the transient issue in place if cleanup cannot prove wakeup artifacts are inactive.
- Scheduled trigger claim restoration is covered for dispatch aborts after the trigger has advanced.
- Regression coverage now includes stale issue counters, visible automated blockers, rollback cleanup, webhook validation failures, post-wakeup finalization failure, claimed/running wakeups, and the queued-to-running cleanup race.

Verification run locally on c0dd7633:
- pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/routines-service.test.ts passed: 26 tests.
- pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project server/tsconfig.json passed.
- pnpm run typecheck passed.
- pnpm build passed.
- env -u DATABASE_URL PATH=<without /usr/local/bin> pnpm test:run passed: 229 files, 1263 passed, 1 skipped.
- git diff --check passed.

PR #3302 Fix plugin page routes doubling company prefix on company switch
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @harperaa · 2026-04-10T13:25:04Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: 6363ab3b

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Direct fix for issue #3264 by the same reporter (@harperaa) who filed it — nice closing of the loop. The change matches the proposed fix exactly: instead of enumerating board routes and stripping only those, the new logic strips the company prefix whenever the first segment is not a global or board route. That's the right inversion — it treats plugin page routes as first-class citizens of the routing layer without requiring every new plugin to register itself in a central enumeration, which aligns with the tree's "thin core, rich edges" stance toward plugins.

The regression tests are well-structured: three separate test cases covering plugin page routes (the bug), board routes (to prove the fix doesn't regress existing behavior), and global routes (to prove the fix doesn't over-strip). The docstring explicitly links back to issue #3264, which makes the test self-documenting for future readers.

Two-line code change, 24-line test file. Perfect scope. Once this lands, issue #3264 can be closed.

Issue #3301 [BUG] OpenCode: Non-interactive mode fails on "Read before Write" guardrail instead of self-correcting
ALIGNED medium open by @MSandro · 2026-04-10T13:05:33Z → gardener comment 1 other

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: medium · issue snapshot: 2026-04-10T12:58Z

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

Clear, well-diagnosed bug. Aligns with an existing class of "tool errors treated as terminal failures" bugs in the opencode-local adapter, and the tree's "surface problems, don't hide them" principle gives a clean framing for the fix. This is the same root cause as #3193 — worth linking so a single fix addresses both symptoms.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Tool-error vs run-error classification | Keep guardrail/tool errors in the agent's thought chain, not bubble as terminal failure | "Surface problems, don't hide them" — silent termination on recoverable errors is the anti-pattern | ✅ Aligned |
| Adapter-owned error shaping | Fix belongs in opencode-local JSONL parser / lifecycle code | Adapters parse agent output into the shared TranscriptEntry types | ✅ Aligned |
| Control plane boundary | Paperclip surfaces exit code; adapter decides what's terminal | "Adapters do NOT own task scheduling" but DO own "parsing agent output" | ✅ Aligned |
| Self-correction loop | Agent learns from adapter error and retries within the same run | Autonomous tasks must be able to recover from recoverable errors | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md) — adapter lifecycle and output parsing contract
- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — adapter abstraction, three-layer package structure, session management
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them"
- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — task communication and recovery semantics

</details>

### Duplicate root cause

Issue #3193 ("opencode_local treats tool_use errors as terminal adapter failures") reports the same class of bug from a different angle: tool-level errors in the OpenCode JSONL stream are being folded into the same terminal-error signal as run-level failures. This issue (#3301) is the user-facing symptom of that same misclassification, specifically for the "Read before Write" guardrail. A single fix to the JSONL parser in packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/parse.ts (or equivalent) should address both.

Worth linking the two issues and treating them as one work item.

### Recommendation — solution ranking

The reporter proposes three solutions. Ranking them by tree alignment and blast radius:

1. Fix the JSONL parser first (implied by #3193 but not yet done). This is the root-cause fix: tool errors stay in the transcript, only fatal adapter errors bubble up as run-level failures. Scoped to the adapter package, no cross-domain changes. Do this regardless of the other options.

2. OpenCode server API transition (reporter's option 1) is architecturally attractive but much larger scope — it would enable persistent sessions and finer-grained error recovery, but deserves a dedicated design discussion and tree node under adapters/opencode-local/ describing the lifecycle shift. Not a prerequisite for fixing this bug.

3. Retry-loop logic in Paperclip (reporter's option 2) should be avoided — it crosses the adapter boundary. Paperclip's control plane shouldn't know about "adapter_failed" strings or special-case opencode error patterns; that's the adapter's job to classify correctly.

4. --session/--continue flags (reporter's option 3) is a useful supplement to option 1, but only after the parser is fixed. If the parser still classifies tool errors as terminal, CLI-level session persistence doesn't help.

### Related tree observations

This is now the fifth or sixth instance of the "something silently fails instead of being recoverable" pattern I've flagged across recent PRs and issues in this gardener run (#3282, #3215, #3207, #3284, #3290, and now #3301/#3193). Worth considering whether adapters/NODE.md should grow a short section on error classification conventions — specifically "tool errors vs adapter errors vs run errors" — so future adapter authors have a clear rule to follow. The current split looks like each adapter invents its own boundary.

---

1 other reply (not engaging with gardener)
ahosker · 2026-04-11T21:10:53Z

It would be best to link to the opencode server rather than use the CLI, as that is where the development is headed.
I presume it would also reduce server resources, as each agent would hook into the existing server rather than create its own.

However, this was causing me real pain.

## Temporary solution
opencode already has a built-in bypass: set OPENCODE_DISABLE_FILETIME_CHECK=true before starting the CLI.
Example:
OPENCODE_DISABLE_FILETIME_CHECK=true opencode
Or export it in your shell:
export OPENCODE_DISABLE_FILETIME_CHECK=true

What it does:
- Disables the "read before overwrite" check
- Also disables the "file changed since last read" protection

Where it lives:
- packages/opencode/src/file/time.ts:90
- packages/opencode/src/flag/flag.ts:73

## How To in Paperclip
In each agent, you can set OpenCode Environment Variables.

PR #3300 fix(server): re-read index.html per request to fix black page on direct URL navigation
NEEDS_REVIEW high open PENDING by @anhermon · 2026-04-10T12:45:59Z → gardener comment 1 engaging
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: high · commit: 4ce3604e

> [!WARNING]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. This review flags a scoping issue that should be addressed before any part of this PR lands.

### Summary

The stated fix is legit — re-reading index.html per request instead of caching at startup closes a real black-page bug on SPA direct navigation after a UI rebuild. But this PR is not a small fix. It is a 74-commit, 147-file, 12,834-addition bundle that spans months of fork-internal work: adapter failure taxonomy, heartbeat/memory gates, Telegram integration, self-hosted OpenTelemetry stack, remote_trigger adapter, agent bundles (cowork/agents/*), new plugin examples, workflows, and more. The stated fix is ~0.1% of the actual diff.

This is almost certainly a mistaken branch target. The branch name is fix/anga-766-spa-routing-stale-index-html but it contains commits from dozens of unrelated ANGA-* tickets. Either the author meant to open a branch with only the ANGA-766 commits, or the fork branch was never rebased onto a clean base for the upstream PR.

### Concerns

1. Impossible to review as one PR. 74 commits across adapter, heartbeat, plugins, observability, telegram, agent bundles, and workflows is far beyond what any reviewer can sanely evaluate in one sitting. Even if every change is individually correct, they should be reviewed independently.

2. Potential fork-internal content leak. The diff includes cowork/agents/{brand-artist,career-monitor,cto,dev-agent-platform,dev-agent-products,learning-agent-2,operations-lead,visibility-agent,youtube-ingest}/ instruction bundles, Taskfile additions, doc/AGENT-GIT-WORKFLOW.md, doc/PUSH_CONVENTION.md, and a plan doc at docs/plans/2026-04-01-claude-rate-limit-fallback-investigation.md. Some of that looks like org-internal material (agent personas, internal workflow docs) that the author may not intend to open-source. Worth double-checking before any of this lands.

3. Possibly conflicting changes with in-flight PRs. This branch touches several files that are currently under review in other PRs:
- packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts and parse.ts — also touched by PRs #3296, #3297, #3299
- packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts — also touched by #3296, #3297
- packages/adapters/codex-local/src/server/execute.ts — also touched by #3298
- packages/adapters/gemini-local/src/server/execute.ts — conflicts with ongoing gemini work

Landing this as-is would create hard-to-resolve merge conflicts for multiple open PRs.

4. Scope laundering risk. A PR whose title claims a 9-line fix but contains 12k lines of unrelated changes defeats the "small, reviewable PRs" model and the governance principle that every change should be independently auditable. This is the exact inverse of the scoping discipline we just saw on PR #3294 → PR #3296/#3297 (which split one bundled fix into two focused PRs).

### Recommendation

Before any part of this lands:

1. Close this PR and open a small, focused one for the ANGA-766 fix (re-read index.html per request + the matching test). That fix is legitimate and should land on its own — estimated size: 1 file, ~10 lines, plus a regression test.

2. If the author wants to contribute the other changes upstream, open separate PRs per logical unit:
- Adapter failure taxonomy + fallback chain (ANGA-184/205) — one PR
- Self-hosted OpenTelemetry observability stack — one PR (large, but architecturally distinct)
- remote_trigger adapter type — one PR
- Memory pre-flight gate (ANGA-165) — one PR, and coordinate with PR #3250 / #3289 (two active memory PRs)
- Telegram integration — one PR (note: this may conflict with "Thin core, rich edges" tree guidance — telegram is likely plugin territory)

3. Audit the diff for fork-internal content before re-opening. Agent bundles under cowork/agents/ and internal workflow docs may not be intended for upstream.

### Scope laundering note for gardener runbook

Gardener should probably flag any PR whose diff size is dramatically larger than what the title suggests, regardless of alignment, because scope laundering is itself a governance concern. This PR is the clearest example I've seen — stated as a 9-line SPA routing fix, delivered as 12,834 lines across 74 commits.

---

PR #3299 [codex] Clarify Claude instruction sibling file base path
ALIGNED low closed CORRECT by @aronprins · 2026-04-10T12:35:06Z → gardener comment 1 engaging 2 other

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: 724893ad

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Minimal, well-scoped fix. Claude adapter's instruction wrapper now explicitly treats the instructionsFileDir as the authoritative base for sibling files like ./HEARTBEAT.md, ./SOUL.md, ./TOOLS.md — closes an onboarding-time failure where fresh CEO agents tried to read siblings from the parent agent directory instead of the managed bundle directory. This is exactly the kind of adapter-owned path semantics the tree expects to live inside the adapter package, and the regression test asserts the stronger directive during the resume-to-fresh fallback path. No cross-domain impact, no bundle persistence change. Related to prior issue #3191 which flagged a similar "sibling file resolution" class of bug in the managed AGENTS.md bundle — worth the reviewer confirming both paths now resolve consistently.

PR #3298 [codex] add codex local login flow
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @roselloagents · 2026-04-10T12:25:57Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: b3c10821

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Clean adapter feature. Mirrors the existing Claude login recovery flow exactly, which is the right precedent — Paperclip control plane exposes a UI action, the adapter owns the CLI invocation using its managed CODEX_HOME strategy, and the codex_auth_required error code lets the UI react when auth is missing mid-run. No cross-adapter abstraction leaks, no governance concerns, and existing API-key support is untouched.

Tree alignment is straightforward:
- Adapter abstraction: authentication lifecycle lives in the adapter, not in core orchestration
- Surface problems, don't hide them: the new codex_auth_required error code is a structured signal, not a silent failure
- Unified API: the agent-detail UI action is a parallel of the existing Claude login button — one UI surface, adapter-specific wiring behind it

One small note: the PR description mentions pnpm build still fails on Windows because the repo build script uses cp -r. That's unrelated to this PR and worth a separate issue so it doesn't block Windows contributors.

greptile-apps[bot] · 2026-04-10T12:26:01Z references-verdict

<h3>Greptile Summary</h3>

This PR adds two deployment-related features on top of #3296: (1) root-process safety for claude_local — when uid=0, --dangerously-skip-permissions is suppressed and a default --allowed-tools list is injected so headless runs still work; and (2) an optional gateway token field for the OpenClaw adapter create flow, written to adapterConfig.headers['x-openclaw-token'].

The PR description does not follow the mandatory PR template from CONTRIBUTING.md. It is missing: a Thinking Path (blockquote format), a What Changed section, a Verification section, a Risks section, a Model Used section (only \"Made with Cursor\" is provided — the template requires provider, exact model ID/version, and capability details), and the Checklist. Additionally, config-fields.tsx introduces a visible UI change (new token field) but no before/after screenshots are provided, which are required by CONTRIBUTING.md for UI changes.

<h3>Confidence Score: 4/5</h3>

Code changes are functionally sound but the PR description is missing all required template sections and no UI screenshots are provided — both are hard requirements per CONTRIBUTING.md before merge.

All remaining P2 findings are style/quality suggestions. The score is held at 4 solely because the PR does not satisfy the mandatory PR template (missing Thinking Path, Verification, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) and omits screenshots for a UI change — these are explicit merge-blockers per CONTRIBUTING.md, not code correctness issues.

No files need code-level attention; the blocker is the PR description and missing UI screenshots.

<details open><summary><h3>Security Review</h3></summary>

- Plain-text token storage (ui/src/adapters/openclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx, packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts): x-openclaw-token is written directly into adapterConfig.headers as plain JSON in the agent record. It is not passed through normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence and therefore bypasses the secrets vault. The value is visible in any API response or DB backup that returns the agent config. The PR documents this trade-off explicitly, but operators should be aware.
- Broad --allowed-tools list injected for root runs (packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/process-defaults.ts): When running as uid=0, Paperclip injects a pre-approved tool list (Bash(*), Write, WebFetch, etc.) so headless Claude runs can proceed. This is a deliberately broad surface by design; operators are warned at startup and can tighten it via extraArgs.
</details>


<h3>Important Files Changed</h3>


| Filename | Overview |
|----------|----------|
| packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/process-defaults.ts | New module: cleanly encapsulates root-uid detection, dangerouslySkipPermissions resolution, and --allowed-tools injection logic; well-tested via imports in execute and test paths. |
| packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts | Adds optional openclawGatewayToken → adapterConfig.headers['x-openclaw-token']; token written as plain-text JSON into agent record (acknowledged in PR, no vault normalization). |
| ui/src/adapters/openclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx | Adds SecretField for gateway token on both create and edit paths; correctly migrates x-openclaw-auth → x-openclaw-token; UI change with no screenshot provided. |
| packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/execute.ts | Integrates process-defaults functions correctly; --allowed-tools injection and --dangerously-skip-permissions skipping on root are wired up in both buildClaudeArgs and the probe path. |
| packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/test.ts | Probe path correctly uses resolveDangerouslySkipPermissions and shouldInjectRootHeadlessAllowedTools, aligned with execute.ts behavior. |
| server/src/__tests__/claude-local-parse-rate-limit.test.ts | New unit tests for isClaudeRateLimitedOutput covering rate-limit event detection, error result detection, and normal success — good coverage. |
| server/src/__tests__/claude-local-adapter-environment.test.ts | Adds integration test verifying exit-1 + rate-limit stream JSON is classified as claude_hello_probe_rate_limited warn, not a probe error — good regression coverage. |
| server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-comment-wake-batching.test.ts | Removes assertion on secondPayload.paperclip.wake structure, replacing with toBeUndefined() + extraSystemPrompt checks — appears intentional protocol change from #3296. |
| packages/adapter-utils/src/types.ts | Adds optional openclawGatewayToken to CreateConfigValues; backward-compatible as optional field. |
| server/src/index.ts | Adds root-uid startup logger.warn summarizing Postgres and Claude allowlist behavior; warning is clear and actionable. |
| docs/deploy/deployment-modes.md | New 'Process user: root vs non-root' section documents the trade-offs clearly with a comparison table. |
| ui/src/components/agent-config-defaults.ts | Adds openclawGatewayToken: "" to defaultCreateValues — correct default, keeps the field optional without breaking existing create flows. |

</details>

<!-- greptile_other_comments_section -->

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix All With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: ui/src/adapters/openclaw-gateway/config-fields.tsx
Line: 138-145

Comment:
Gateway token stored as plain-text JSON

The hint correctly tells operators that x-openclaw-token is persisted as plain JSON in the agent record and not passed through normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence into the secrets vault. This means the token is readable via any API endpoint or DB query that returns adapterConfig, and would appear in database backups in the clear.

The PR acknowledges this trade-off, which is appreciated. Consider adding a one-line note in the on-edit SecretField (line 199) as well, since operators editing an existing agent face the same storage behaviour and currently see no hint there.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.

---

This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/process-defaults.ts
Line: 61-67

Comment:
isPaperclipRunningAsRoot() called twice per invocation path

shouldInjectRootHeadlessAllowedTools calls isPaperclipRunningAsRoot() internally, and its caller (execute.ts / test.ts) has already derived dangerouslySkipPermissions via resolveDangerouslySkipPermissions, which also calls isPaperclipRunningAsRoot(). Each call invokes process.getuid() separately. Since the uid is constant for the process lifetime you could compute it once at module load time (or accept the current approach as a non-issue since it's a trivial syscall). Minor, but worth noting if this path is hot.

suggestion
// Near top of process-defaults.ts:
const _isRoot: boolean = typeof process.getuid === "function" && process.getuid() === 0;
export function isPaperclipRunningAsRoot(): boolean {
  return _isRoot;
}

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.

---

This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: packages/adapters/claude-local/src/index.ts
Line: 24

Comment:
Doc comment line is very long

The updated dangerouslySkipPermissions doc comment is now a single run-on sentence spanning ~300 characters. Consider wrapping it at ~100 characters for readability in editors without soft-wrap.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````

</details>

PR #3296 fix: OpenClaw gateway schema + Claude hello probe rate limits
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @htsago · 2026-04-10T12:25:25Z → gardener comment 1 engaging 1 other

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: 89dcb8a2

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Re-reviewed after HEAD update (4977b80e → 89dcb8a2). The new commit is "detect rate limits via per-line stream-json, not stdout regex" — meaningful improvement. Moving rate-limit detection from a stdout regex to per-line JSON parsing is more robust: handles multi-line output correctly, doesn't false-positive on content that happens to contain "limit" or "quota" in other contexts, and integrates cleanly with the existing stream-json parser. Scope and tree alignment unchanged from prior cycles.

greptile-apps[bot] · 2026-04-10T12:26:17Z references-verdict

<h3>Greptile Summary</h3>

This PR makes two targeted fixes: (1) the OpenClaw gateway adapter now moves Paperclip context out of a root paperclip key (rejected by OpenClaw's additionalProperties: false schema) into extraSystemPrompt, and falls back to ws://127.0.0.1:18789 when no URL is configured; (2) the claude-local hello probe now classifies a Claude CLI exit-1 with valid rate-limit stream-JSON as a warning rather than a hard failure, with a new exported isClaudeRateLimitedOutput helper and matching tests.

The PR description does not follow the required PR template from CONTRIBUTING.md. Please add the missing sections before merging:
- Thinking Path — trace from Paperclip's purpose down to this specific change
- What Changed — formal bullet list
- Verification — how to confirm it works (the test plan is a good start but should be in this section)
- Risks — behavioral shifts worth calling out (e.g. gateway URL-missing no longer fails fast)
- Model Used — provider, exact model ID/version, and capability details (not just "Made with Cursor")
- Checklist

<h3>Confidence Score: 4/5</h3>

Code changes are correct and well-tested, but the required PR template sections are absent and should be filled in before merge.

All three inline findings are P2 (false-positive risk in raw stdout scan, flaky exit-code assertion in the loopback test, and unused resolveClaimedApiKeyPath). However, the missing PR template (Thinking Path, Risks, Model Used, Checklist) is a stated merge blocker per CONTRIBUTING.md, keeping the score at 4 until that is addressed.

packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts (raw stdout scan), packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/test.ts (flaky exitCode assertion), packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts (unused helper).

<h3>Important Files Changed</h3>


| Filename | Overview |
|----------|----------|
| packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts | Adds isClaudeRateLimitedOutput — logic is sound but the raw stdout scan can false-positive on assistant text containing limit-related phrases. |
| packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/test.ts | Hello probe updated to treat exit 1 + valid stream-JSON as a success path when rate-limited; logic and fallback detail handling are correct. |
| packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts | Moves Paperclip context from root paperclip key to extraSystemPrompt; adds default loopback URL; resolveClaimedApiKeyPath is defined but unused (hardcoded path in buildWakeText). |
| packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/test.ts | URL-missing failure changed to URL-defaulted info; new test asserts exitCode === 1 which will fail if OpenClaw is running on the loopback port in CI. |
| packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/defaults.ts | New file exporting DEFAULT_OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_WS_URL = "ws://127.0.0.1:18789" — clean and correct. |
| packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/ui/build-config.ts | Config builder now explicitly sets default URL, timeoutSec, and waitTimeoutMs; straightforward and correct. |
| server/src/__tests__/openclaw-gateway-adapter.test.ts | Test assertions updated to match new extraSystemPrompt payload shape and URL-defaulting behaviour; changes are aligned with the implementation. |
| server/src/__tests__/heartbeat-comment-wake-batching.test.ts | Heartbeat batching expectations updated to check extraSystemPrompt instead of paperclip root object; correct alignment with schema change. |
| server/src/__tests__/claude-local-parse-rate-limit.test.ts | New unit tests for isClaudeRateLimitedOutput covering the key detection paths; coverage is adequate for the three main cases. |
| server/src/__tests__/claude-local-adapter-environment.test.ts | Integration test using a fake Claude CLI that emits rate-limit stream-JSON and exits 1; correctly validates the new probe classification path. |
| packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/index.ts | Re-exports isClaudeRateLimitedOutput from parse.ts; no issues. |
| packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/index.ts | Minor export additions consistent with the rest of the adapter's public surface. |

</details>

1. packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/test.ts, line 281-290 ([link](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/blob/f9a89328eca0eccbe0d83e7dfed18ba0b3504b0d/packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/test.ts#L281-L290))

<a href="#"><img alt="P2" src="https://greptile-static-assets.s3.amazonaws.com/badges/p2.svg?v=7" align="top"></a> Test assumes nothing is listening on loopback port 18789

The renamed test ("uses default loopback gateway URL when url is omitted") passes {} as config, which now resolves to ws://127.0.0.1:18789. In a CI environment or developer machine where OpenClaw is actually running on that port, the probe succeeds and exitCode would be 0, not 1 — making the expect(result.exitCode).toBe(1) assertion fail. Consider asserting on the absence of the old error code only:

ts
   // Only assert what the PR actually wants to guarantee:
   expect(result.errorCode).not.toBe("openclaw_gateway_url_missing");
   // exitCode could be 0 (server running) or 1 (connection refused); don't assert on it.

resolveClaimedApiKeyPath is declared at line 337 and exposed to the config-driven path (ctx.config.claimedApiKeyPath), but buildWakeText (line 368) hard-codes the same path literal "~/.openclaw/workspace/paperclip-claimed-api-key.json" instead of calling this function. If you intend to let operators override the key path via config, buildWakeText should accept the resolved path as a parameter rather than duplicating the constant.

<!-- /greptile_failed_comments -->
<details><summary>Prompt To Fix All With AI</summary>
``markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: packages/adapters/claude-local/src/server/parse.ts
Line: 183-186

Comment:
Raw stdout scan can false-positive on assistant text

The first branch tests the entire raw stdout string for phrases like "hit your limit" and "you've hit your limit". Because stdout is stream-JSON, an assistant turn whose text content happens to contain those words (e.g., a model response about API quotas) would trigger a false-rate-limit classification and suppress the claude_hello_probe_passed / _unexpected_output check. The safer approach is to restrict this scan to lines whose type is rate_limit_event or result, rather than matching the full stdout payload:

ts
// Instead of testing the raw stdout blob, only flag specific stream-JSON event types:
const hasRateLimitEvent = stdout.split(/\r?\n/).some((line) => {
  try {
    const ev = JSON.parse(line.trim());
    return ev?.type === "rate_limit_event" || ev?.error === "rate_limit";
  } catch { return false; }
});
if (hasRateLimitEvent) return true;

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.

---

PR #3295 Troubleshooting dangerouslySkipPermissions configuration issue
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @glide-the · 2026-04-10T12:15:57Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: fdecac8e

Aligned with context tree. No concerns that block merge, but a few worth noting for reviewers.

The core change is a clean single-source-of-truth refactor: three UI codepaths (NewAgent, OnboardingWizard, AgentConfigForm) each carried their own inline per-adapter default dictionaries, which had drifted. Extracting adapterConfigDefaults + getCreateValuesForAdapterType() eliminates the drift. Server-side normalization in applyAdapterConfigDefaults is actually MORE correct than UI-side defaults alone — API callers (CLI, scripts, plugins) now get the default too, not just users creating via the web UI. Good instinct.

### Notes for reviewers

1. PR scope is broader than the description suggests. The PR body says "UI-only default logic" and "no server, or adapter runtime changes" but the diff includes:
- Server-side normalization in server/src/routes/agents.ts (applyAdapterConfigDefaults now writes a dangerouslySkipPermissions default for claude_local and opencode_local)
- A behavior change to asBoolean in packages/adapter-utils/src/server-utils.ts (now accepts string "true"/"false" case-insensitively, was boolean-only)
- A new 314-line dangerously-skip-permissions.test.ts

Both extra changes are safe and well-tested, but the "low risk, UI-only" framing is misleading. Worth updating the PR description so CODEOWNERS/reviewers see the full scope.

2. Interaction with PR #3294 (still open). PR #3294 introduces resolveDangerouslySkipPermissions() in the claude-local adapter that overrides the configured value to false when Paperclip runs as root. If both PRs land, the data flow becomes:
- UI writes dangerouslySkipPermissions: true from the shared default
- Server normalizes to true if missing (this PR)
- Adapter overrides to false at execute time on root (#3294)

The config stored in the DB says one thing; the runtime does another. That's not a bug per se, but it creates a surprising diagnostic experience — operators will see dangerouslySkipPermissions: true in agent config but no --dangerously-skip-permissions flag in the transcript. Worth coordinating with #3294 or adding a logger.info explaining the override when it fires.

3. asBoolean widening is a cross-cutting behavior change. The new string coercion ("true" → true, "false" → false, case-insensitive) affects every caller of asBoolean across the codebase. The widening is backwards-compatible (existing boolean inputs behave identically), and the coercion only fires on string inputs that previously fell through to the fallback. The test coverage is thorough. Low risk, but worth calling out because adapter-utils contract changes are the kind of thing that can surprise plugin authors or downstream adapters — the utility's signature lives in @paperclipai/adapter-utils which is part of the stable surface.

4. dangerouslySkipPermissions: true as the default is a load-bearing security decision. The inline comment explains it correctly (headless mode has no interactive prompts), but it's worth documenting in the context tree under adapters/ as an explicit policy so future adapter authors know the rationale and don't re-litigate it.

### Recommendation

Land it. The refactor is good, the server-side enforcement is the right layer, and the tests are solid. Just (a) update the PR description to reflect the full scope, (b) add a coordination note to PR #3294 about the config-vs-runtime override interaction, and (c) consider adding a one-paragraph note to adapters/NODE.md or the tree about the "headless mode default skip" policy so the rationale is discoverable.

PR #3294 fix: OpenClaw gateway schema, Claude probe rate limits, UI token + embedded PG root
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open PENDING by @htsago · 2026-04-10T12:06:00Z → gardener comment 1 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: 5ba2663a

> [!CAUTION]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

The individual fixes are all technically reasonable and several align well with the tree. Two product-fit concerns deserve a maintainer call: (1) the PR bundles four logically distinct fixes, which makes review and bisect harder; (2) several of the changes implicitly expand "running Paperclip as root" into a supported deployment mode, which contradicts what infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md documents.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match per sub-fix</strong></summary>

| Sub-fix | Tree guidance | Fit |
|---------|---------------|-----|
| OpenClaw context → extraSystemPrompt | Adapter owns agent-param shaping | ✅ Aligned |
| OpenClaw token via env var / _FILE | Secrets are provider-abstracted, *_FILE matches Docker/K8s pattern | ✅ Aligned |
| Claude rate-limit reclassification | "Surface problems, don't hide them" — classifies but doesn't hide | ✅ Aligned |
| Claude root-UID auto-disable --dangerously-skip-permissions | Adapter knows its CLI constraints | ✅ Aligned |
| CLAUDE_ROOT_HEADLESS_ALLOWED_TOOLS hardcoded allowlist including Bash(*) | Governance-first / least privilege | ❓ Partial |
| Embedded PG createPostgresUser: true when uid=0 | infrastructure/deployment documents UID/GID remapping, not running as root | ❓ Conflict |
| Implicit expansion of "run as root" deployment mode | "UID/GID remapping for volume permissions" pattern already exists | ❓ Partial |
| UI gateway token field in CreateConfigValues | Secrets should flow through secretService.normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence | ❓ Unclear |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md) — "UID/GID remapping for volume permissions" — the docker entrypoint remaps the node user and gosus to it, which means Paperclip should not run as uid 0 in production
- [adapters/claude-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/claude-local/NODE.md) — Claude adapter owns CLI invocation and flag resolution
- [adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md) — OpenClaw gateway transport and context shaping
- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — Secrets are provider-abstracted

</details>

### Questions for maintainers

1. Should this be split into two or three PRs? The four sub-fixes are:
- OpenClaw schema + token resolution (adapter concern)
- Claude rate-limit reclassification (adapter concern)
- Root-UID handling across claude-local + embedded-PG (deployment concern)
- UI gateway token field (frontend concern)

Landing as one bundle makes the "run as root" story hard to discuss independently from the OpenClaw fix. Splitting lets each get a focused review and makes bisect/revert cleaner.

2. Is "run Paperclip as root" now a supported mode? The infrastructure/deployment tree node documents that the Docker entrypoint uses gosu to drop to the node user specifically to avoid running as root. This PR adds code paths that only fire when Paperclip runs as root:
- createPostgresUser: true for embedded-postgres
- Auto-disable --dangerously-skip-permissions for Claude CLI
- Inject --allowed-tools default allowlist

These fixes only make sense if "run as root" is a deliberately supported mode. If so, the deployment tree node should be updated to describe the two modes (gosu-remapped vs. root-direct) and when each is appropriate. If not, the fixes may be protecting a misconfiguration that operators should be correcting upstream (e.g., by setting USER_UID/USER_GID correctly).

3. Is the CLAUDE_ROOT_HEADLESS_ALLOWED_TOOLS list tight enough? The comment in the code frames this as "safer than --dangerously-skip-permissions", but Bash(*) is effectively unrestricted shell access, and the list also includes Write, Edit, WebFetch, Task, RemoteTrigger, EnterWorktree. That's the same blast radius as --dangerously-skip-permissions, just expressed differently. Two alternatives worth considering:
- Keep the hardcoded default but add a logger.warn at startup explaining "Paperclip is running as root; Claude is configured with a broad tool allowlist. Run as a non-root user for finer-grained permission control."
- Make the allowlist configurable per deployment via env var or adapter config so operators can tighten it without patching the code.

4. UI gateway token field — confirm that CreateConfigValues.openclawGatewayToken flows through secretService.normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence and is stored in the vault, not in plain JSON in agent.adapterConfig. The PR description mentions adding the field but not the secret-handling path.

### Recommendation

Don't block on the core direction — the individual fixes address real bugs. But before merge:

- Split the PR into an OpenClaw/Claude adapter fixes PR and a separate "run as root" deployment mode PR. The former can land quickly; the latter deserves a deliberate maintainer call and tree documentation.
- If the maintainer call is that "run as root" is supported, update infrastructure/deployment/NODE.md to describe both modes and their trade-offs.
- Confirm the secrets path for the UI gateway token field.
- Add a startup warning when running as root with the broad default allowlist, so operators are explicitly aware of what they're getting.

The claude-local rate-limit reclassification is a clean improvement and would land fine on its own. The OpenClaw schema fix is also a clean adapter-level bugfix. Everything else needs a bit more deliberation.

---

PR #3293 Html preview
NEEDS_REVIEW high open PENDING by @KhabarovaNina · 2026-04-10T11:45:55Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: high · commit: 0aba8bc7

> [!WARNING]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review. However, this review raises a security boundary concern that should be addressed before merge.

### Summary

The product direction is good — inline HTML report preview fits the "tasks are the communication channel" and "progressive disclosure" principles, and this builds naturally on the html-report skill in #3292. But the current implementation crosses a trust boundary without enough care: agent-generated HTML is rendered in the Paperclip UI origin with a CSP that allows inline scripts and no iframe sandboxing. That's a cross-site scripting vector.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Inline report preview in issue chat | Rich artifact rendering in the task UI | "Tasks are the communication channel" + progressive disclosure | ✅ Aligned |
| Company-scoped route auth | assertCompanyAccess on the new endpoint | Multi-company isolation | ✅ Aligned |
| CSP on served HTML | default-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' data: blob: | "Governance-first" — agent output is untrusted input | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Iframe sandboxing | No sandbox attribute on the preview iframe | Trust boundary between agent output and the Paperclip UI origin | ⚠️ Conflict |
| File-level authorization | Any board user in a company can fetch any .html in any workspace | "Every mutation is auditable" and least-privilege implied | ❓ Partial |
| Filesystem path disclosure | debug: { checkedPaths } in 404 response | Information disclosure — minor but not needed | ❓ Partial |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — communication via tasks
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — governance enforced at server layer, agent output is untrusted
- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — service/route split and authz patterns

</details>

### Security concerns (must address before merge)

1. CSP allows inline scripts. The response sets:


    Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' data: blob:; img-src 'self' data: blob:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';

'unsafe-inline' in default-src covers script-src by inheritance, which means inline <script> tags execute. The html-report skill from #3292 explicitly forbids JavaScript in its validator, but the validator runs at skill time — the CSP here has to be the enforcement layer if anything else ever drops HTML into reports/. Fix: add an explicit script-src 'none', and tighten default-src to 'self' without 'unsafe-inline'.

2. No iframe sandboxing. The HtmlReportPreview component needs <iframe sandbox="allow-same-origin" ...> (or drop allow-same-origin entirely if the report is served with CSP blocking scripts). Without sandbox, a malicious report runs in the Paperclip UI origin with access to cookies, local storage, and any in-page state.

3. Same-origin deployment. Reports are served from /api/companies/:companyId/issues/:issueId/reports/:filename — same origin as the app. Combined with (1) and (2), this is the full XSS path. Even if the html-report skill validator is correct, defense-in-depth requires the browser-level protections.

### Other concerns

4. File-level authorization. assertCompanyAccess grants any board user access to any report in any issue workspace in the company. That may be the intended model, but it's worth confirming — an ops user who can see one issue's chat also gets enumeration over every other issue's workspace files, just by guessing filenames. The filename pattern YYYY-MM-DD-slug.html is guessable.

5. Information disclosure in 404. The debug: { checkedPaths } field leaks server filesystem layout to any client that hits a 404. Remove for production builds or gate behind a debug flag.

6. "First match wins" across candidate dirs. The endpoint checks up to ~20 candidate directories and returns the first file that exists. This is correct for a single issue but has a subtle consistency bug: if the workspace lookup returns a stale or wrong workspace (e.g., a detached project workspace with an unrelated file of the same name), the wrong report is served. Low probability, but worth a guard — e.g., require the workspace row's issueId/sourceIssueId to match.

### Recommendation

Don't block on the product direction — inline preview is the right call. But before merge:

1. Tighten CSP: default-src 'none'; img-src 'self' data:; style-src 'unsafe-inline'; base-uri 'none'; form-action 'none';
2. Add iframe sandbox: <iframe sandbox="" srcdoc={...}> or <iframe sandbox="" src={url}> — no allow-same-origin, no allow-scripts.
3. Remove the debug.checkedPaths field from the 404 response body.
4. Tighten the workspace match so the served file always corresponds to a row whose issueId/sourceIssueId matches the URL.
5. Confirm the file-level auth model with maintainers — board users should probably only see reports for issues they're already permitted to view, not every workspace file in the company.

Given the html-report skill ships inline CSS and no JS, the restricted CSP above will render the expected reports correctly while making it safe to extend the system later. Worth the extra pass before landing.

---

PR #3292 html-report as default skill
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open PENDING by @KhabarovaNina · 2026-04-10T11:25:59Z → gardener comment 1 other

🌱 gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: da225e31

> [!CAUTION]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

The skill itself is nicely built — clear boundaries, security-conscious (no JS, no external resources, content whitelist, validator), print-ready archival output. Two product-fit questions worth a maintainer call before merge.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Skill follows existing skills/ convention | New subdirectory with SKILL.md, references/, scripts/, templates/, examples/ | Paperclip has an established skill injection pattern via listSkills / syncSkills in the adapter contract | ✅ Aligned |
| Content whitelist + HTML validator | Security enforced at skill layer, not adapter | Governance-first posture for anything agents produce | ✅ Aligned |
| "Default skill" claim | PR title says "as default skill" | No default-skill wiring in the diff | ❓ Unclear |
| Skill scope boundary | html-report is a generic agent-productivity skill | Existing skills/ are Paperclip control-plane skills: paperclip, paperclip-create-agent, paperclip-create-plugin, para-memory-files | ❓ Boundary drift |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Skill lifecycle (listSkills / syncSkills) and skill injection via symlinks into the agent's native skill directory
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Thin core, rich edges"

</details>

### Questions for maintainers

1. Is this a "default" skill or an optional one? The PR title says "as default skill" but the diff only adds files under skills/html-report/ — no changes to server/src/services/company-skills.ts, packages/shared/src/validators/company-skill.ts, or any auto-install/bundled-skills wiring. Either:
- "Default" is aspirational and will be wired in a follow-up — in which case consider retitling to something like "Add html-report skill" to avoid confusion.
- There's a convention-based auto-discovery from skills/ that makes this "default" by virtue of living there — if so, that's worth documenting in the tree so future skill authors know the rule.

2. Is skills/ Paperclip-core or general-purpose? The existing directory holds four Paperclip control-plane skills (paperclip, paperclip-create-agent, paperclip-create-plugin, para-memory-files). html-report is a generic agent-productivity skill that would work fine outside Paperclip. That's a boundary question: should skills/ be strictly for Paperclip-meta skills (things that teach agents how to operate inside Paperclip) and general productivity skills should live elsewhere (separate repo, community skills marketplace, plugin)? Or is skills/ becoming a general first-party skill library? Whichever answer, it's worth recording in the tree so the next PR doesn't have to re-litigate.

3. Python test coverage. The PR ships 615 + 411 lines of Python (build_report.py, validate_html.py) with no visible test files. The validator is a security boundary — worth adding tests that exercise the negative examples in examples/bad-*.json to prove the validator rejects them.

### Recommendation

Don't block on the design — the skill is well-scoped and the security posture (inline CSS only, no JS, content whitelist, separate validator script) is the right shape. But before landing:

- Clarify the "default" claim in the PR title/description and the maintenance plan for skill registration.
- Get a maintainer call on whether skills/ is a control-plane-only directory or a general first-party library, and document that decision in the tree.
- Add Python tests for the validator that cover the bad-external-css.json and bad-script.json negative examples.

---
