Issue #3235 Setup for Desktop applications building both Windows/Mac versions.
INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT low open by @sl0thm4n · 2026-04-10T04:55:52Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener:verdict: INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT · severity: low · issue: #3235

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

This is a user support question about running cross-platform desktop builds across a Windows Paperclip instance and a Mac machine. It's unclear whether the request is asking for a Paperclip feature (remote agent dispatch to a second machine) or just setup documentation. The tree has context on Paperclip's deployment model and local-first principle, but not enough detail about multi-machine agent dispatch to give a definitive verdict.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Multi-machine agent coordination | Run a "slave" Paperclip on Mac so agents can build Mac targets | Tree describes Paperclip as a control plane that agents phone home to; multi-instance federation or remote agent dispatch is not addressed | ❔ Insufficient |
| Local-first, cloud-ready | User has a local Windows instance and wants to extend to Mac | Tree principle supports local deployment; cross-machine orchestration would require network-connected instances but no design exists for this | ❔ Insufficient |
| Adapter abstraction | Agents run wherever they run and report back via adapters | An agent running on a Mac machine could theoretically connect back to the Windows Paperclip via the OpenClaw gateway pattern, but this isn't documented as a supported deployment | ❔ Insufficient |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Local-first, cloud-ready; control plane not execution plane
- [adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md) — Gateway adapter for remote agent connections (closest potential fit)
- [infrastructure/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/NODE.md) — Deployment modes (local_trusted vs authenticated)

</details>

### Recommendation

This looks primarily like a user support question rather than a feature request. Maintainers should clarify:
1. Is the user asking whether Paperclip supports multi-machine deployments where agents on a remote Mac phone home to a Windows control plane? (If so, the OpenClaw gateway is potentially the right path.)
2. Or is this purely a build tooling question outside Paperclip's scope?

If it is a feature gap (remote agent dispatch to a different machine), a tree node on multi-machine deployment topology would be worth creating. Either way, some triage and a response pointing the user to the right deployment path is needed.

---

Issue #3259 CEO onboarding instructions don't teach board escalation via assigneeUserId
ALIGNED medium open by @rudyjellis · 2026-04-10T04:55:45Z → gardener comment 1 other

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: medium · issue updated: 2026-04-10T02:08:24Z

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

The CEO onboarding AGENTS.md is missing an explicit section on board escalation mechanics. This causes a real governance failure mode: agents produce comments on their own tasks instead of creating board-assigned subtasks, which means board users miss critical blockers. The fix is squarely within Paperclip's governance-first design.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Board escalation mechanism | Teach CEO to create subtasks with assigneeUserId pointing at the board user | Tasks are the communication channel — all agent communication flows through tasks and comments; delegation = creating a task and assigning it | ✅ Aligned |
| Governance-first design | Ensure board blockers surface immediately rather than silently stalling | "Surface problems, don't hide them" is a core product principle; board must always be reachable | ✅ Aligned |
| Approval gates and oversight | Board-dependent blockers (approvals, secrets, business decisions) must reach the human operator | Board has unrestricted access and must be notified of blockers; approval workflow is synchronous | ✅ Aligned |
| CEO strategy scaffolding | Onboarding assets should encode correct CEO behavior from the start | V1 approval gate: CEO strategy approval must precede execution; board governance is non-negotiable | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Tasks are the communication channel; delegation = creating an assigned task
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board powers; approval gates; "surface problems, don't hide them"
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Board-level abstraction wins; governance-first

</details>

### Recommendation

This is a correctness fix for the onboarding experience, not a feature request. The gap directly undermines board governance — the safety foundation the entire product is built on. The proposed section (create a board-assigned subtask with assigneeUserId, use Board: title prefix, set blockedByIssueIds) matches how the task system is designed to work. Ship it.

---

Issue #3236 Feature request: Board inbox filtering — hide agent run failures, show only actionable items
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open by @pfergi42 · 2026-04-10T04:55:35Z → gardener comment 1 other
(3 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · issue: #3236

> [!CAUTION]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

The board inbox filtering request is partially aligned with Paperclip's board-level UX goals and progressive disclosure principle, but one key ask — hiding agent run failures from the board inbox — sits in tension with the "surface problems, don't hide them" core principle. The exact filtering design requires a maintainer judgment call.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Board UX / actionable inbox | Separate actionable items (approvals, escalations, in_review) from operational noise | Tree says the board experience should answer "what needs my approval" — filtering towards board-relevant actions is directionally sound | ✅ Aligned |
| Progressive disclosure | Move agent operational noise to a separate tab/view | Tree principle: top layer = human-readable summary, bottom layer = raw logs. Separating board-action items from agent run logs aligns with this layering | ✅ Aligned |
| Surface problems, don't hide them | Request asks to hide agent run failures and heartbeat errors from the board inbox entirely | Tree is explicit: no silent auto-recovery, no hiding of failures. Board must be able to see what's broken. Hiding failures in a separate tab is one thing; disabling them entirely risks obscuring real problems | ❓ Partial |
| Governance board powers | Board retains unrestricted access to pause, terminate, and override any agent | Filtering the inbox is fine as long as the board can still discover and act on failures when needed | ❓ Partial |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them" and "Progressive disclosure" principles
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board powers and the expectation of full visibility at all times

</details>

### Recommendation

The filtering direction is reasonable for a 40-agent org at scale. However, the design should keep failures visible somewhere (a separate "Agent Activity" tab is mentioned — that's the right approach), not suppressible entirely. The "option to disable failure notifications in board inbox entirely" is the one ask that conflicts with tree principles. Recommend accepting the filtering/tab-separation direction, and discussing the "disable entirely" ask with maintainers before implementing. A "mark all as read" button is low risk and clearly aligned.

---

Issue #3260 Add plugin upgrade/reinstall command that preserves config
ALIGNED medium open by @rudyjellis · 2026-04-10T04:55:23Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: medium · issue updated: 2026-04-10T02:09:04Z

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

A missing CLI command for plugin lifecycle management. The proposed plugin upgrade path correctly targets the server-side plugin lifecycle state machine and preserves plugin_config — consistent with how the tree describes graceful lifecycle transitions and database-backed plugin state.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Plugin lifecycle | Add upgrade command that re-validates manifest, cycles the worker, and preserves config | Runtime defines installed -> ready -> disabled/error -> uninstalled with validated transitions; "Graceful lifecycle state machine" is a first-class runtime concern | ✅ Aligned |
| Plugin config preservation | Preserve plugin_config row across upgrades unless schema is incompatible | Plugin state, entities, job runs persisted in PostgreSQL with pluginId scoping; lifecycle manager coordinates worker stop/start | ✅ Aligned |
| CLI tooling | New paperclipai plugin upgrade subcommand | CLI is a monorepo workspace package; plugin management already has install/uninstall commands | ✅ Aligned |
| Developer experience | Reduces friction for local plugin iteration | Plugin SDK includes PluginDevWatcher for hot-reload; upgrade path is the production-safe complement | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [plugins/runtime/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/runtime/NODE.md) — PluginLifecycleManager, graceful shutdown with deadline, database-backed state
- [plugins/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Graceful lifecycle state machine; manifest-driven contract

</details>

### Recommendation

Well-specified request aligned with the plugin runtime's existing lifecycle model. The proposed five-step upgrade sequence (validate manifest → stop worker → update DB → preserve config → restart) maps directly onto existing runtime services. Worth prioritizing as it reduces the cost of iterating on plugins significantly during development. Ready for a PR.

---

Issue #3247 Agent output garbles non-ASCII characters (UTF-8 encoding issue)
ALIGNED high open by @h7r · 2026-04-10T04:55:11Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: high · issue: #3247

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

UTF-8 encoding corruption in agent output is a high-severity bug that directly conflicts with Paperclip's goal of supporting non-English agent companies. It also violates the "surface problems, don't hide them" principle — agents are producing incorrect output that is silently rendered as mojibake.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Agent output fidelity | Fix UTF-8 corruption between agent API output and UI display | Tasks are the communication channel — all agent communication flows through task comments. Corrupted output breaks this core channel | ✅ Aligned |
| Internationalization | Restore French (and all non-ASCII) character rendering in agent-written content | Tree says Paperclip is a control plane for autonomous AI companies; no language restriction is stated — blocking non-English companies is an unintended constraint | ✅ Aligned |
| Progressive disclosure | Fix impacts task comments, deliverable summaries, status updates | These are the task-system layers agents use to communicate; corruption at these layers undermines the entire communication model | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Tasks are the communication channel; agent output integrity is load-bearing
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them" — bugs shouldn't silently corrupt output

</details>

### Recommendation

High-severity bug. The issue has clear reproduction steps and solid examples. The encoding break is somewhere in the agent → API → storage → UI pipeline. Recommend triaging urgently — this blocks an entire class of non-English deployments. Assign to the backend or adapter team to trace whether the corruption is at storage (DB encoding), API response serialization, or the frontend render layer.

---

Issue #3264 Plugin page routes double company prefix on company switch
ALIGNED medium open by @harperaa · 2026-04-10T04:55:04Z → gardener comment 1 other

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: medium · issue updated: 2026-04-10T03:07:09Z

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

This is a well-scoped bug report against the UI routing layer that surfaces precisely when plugin page routes interact with multi-company navigation. The root cause analysis and proposed fix are both consistent with how Paperclip models companies and plugins.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Plugin page routing | Fix doubled company prefix when switching companies on plugin page routes | Plugin UI bundles load into host-designated slots and must operate correctly within company-scoped routing | ✅ Aligned |
| Company-scoped isolation | Bug occurs because toCompanyRelativePath() doesn't recognize plugin routes as company-relative | All entities are company-scoped; plugin UI slots receive company_id via PluginHostContext | ✅ Aligned |
| Frontend architecture | Fix is a targeted change to ui/src/lib/company-routes.ts | Frontend uses React Router v7 with context providers for current company | ✅ Aligned |
| Plugin extensibility | Fix unblocks plugins with page UI slots from working correctly across company switches | Plugin slots include page as a first-class UI slot type | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [plugins/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — UI extension via sandboxed slots; plugin pages are a first-class slot type
- [engineering/frontend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — React Router v7 routing, company context provider
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Company as unit of organization; company-scoped isolation

</details>

### Recommendation

Confirmed bug with clear root cause and a safe, minimal fix. The proposed logic change (strip prefix when first segment is not global or board route) is the right generalization — it handles arbitrary plugin routes without requiring an enumerated allowlist. This is ready for a PR.

Issue #3254 Claude adapter: 'Invalid signature in thinking block' error not handled as session error, causing crash instead of retry
ALIGNED medium open by @956005596 · 2026-04-10T04:54:57Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: medium · issue: #3254

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

This is a well-scoped bug fix for the Claude local adapter's session error detection. The proposed change is consistent with the adapter's session-resume pattern and the tree's guidance on how adapters should handle session failures.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Adapter session handling | Extend isClaudeUnknownSessionError regex to catch invalid signature in thinking block and trigger fresh-session retry | Adapters manage session resume via --session-id; the adapter boundary is responsible for detecting session errors and signaling the retry path | ✅ Aligned |
| Error classification | Treat thinking-block signature errors the same as unknown session errors | Claude adapter uses isClaudeUnknownSessionError specifically to distinguish recoverable session errors from hard failures; adding new patterns here is the correct extension point | ✅ Aligned |
| Surface problems, don't hide them | Current behavior crashes instead of retrying — the proposed behavior logs a warning then retries | Tree says no silent auto-recovery; this fix adds a logged retry (warn + retry), not silent swallowing | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/claude-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/claude-local/NODE.md) — Claude local adapter capabilities and session-resume design
- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Session management and ServerAdapterModule contract

</details>

### Recommendation

This is a legitimate bug with a clear, minimal fix. The regex extension is in the right file and follows the existing pattern. Recommend accepting. Would be worth confirming whether the thinking-block signature error can surface in contexts other than session resume (e.g., if the upstream API returns it for fresh sessions too), and whether a separate error class would be more precise — but those are code-review-level concerns.

### Summary

This PR adds an "Execution Thread" tab to Issue Detail — a cross-issue chronological timeline for parent-child issue trees. It deepens existing task-system visibility without adding new primitives or crossing domain boundaries.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |

| Task system / communication channel | Aggregate events across parent-child issue tree into a unified timeline | product/task-system/NODE.md: "all work traces to the company goal through a parent task chain"; tasks are the communication channel; no separate messaging | ✅ Aligned |
| Progressive disclosure | Thread tab adds a mid-level cross-issue view; existing Activity tab is untouched | product/NODE.md: "Progressive disclosure — top layer: summary, middle: steps/artifacts, bottom: raw logs" | ✅ Aligned |
| Governance / audit trail | Every comment, status change, assignment, and blocker across an execution wave is surfaced | product/NODE.md: "Surface problems, don't hide them"; governance-first with full audit trails | ✅ Aligned |
| Frontend architecture | New route, React Query hook, API layer method, shared types — follows established patterns | engineering/frontend/NODE.md: API layer centralizes HTTP calls; hooks encapsulate React Query; no global state | ✅ Aligned |
| Company-scoped isolation | Execution thread walks parent-child issue tree; no indication of cross-company data leakage | engineering/NODE.md: multi-company isolation at query level | ✅ Aligned |
| @assistant-ui/react in lockfile | PR adds @assistant-ui/react to pnpm-lock.yaml | engineering/frontend/NODE.md already lists @assistant-ui/react as a stack dependency — consistent | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — tasks as communication channel, all work traces to goal, human-readable identifiers
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — progressive disclosure, surface problems, board-level abstraction
- [engineering/frontend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — React 19, React Query, API layer, page structure patterns

</details>

### Recommendation

Safe to merge from a context-fit perspective. One note for code reviewers: the recursive CTE is capped at 200 issues — confirm the truncation behavior (truncated: true in the response) is visible in the UI so board-level users can tell when they're seeing a partial view. That aligns with "surface problems, don't hide them."

PR #3230 feat: add MinIO S3 storage and aws-file-store skill
CONFLICT high open PENDING by @qeeq72 · 2026-04-10T04:51:44Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: CONFLICT · severity: high · commit: d1815b0

> [!WARNING]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

This PR adds a MinIO-backed shared knowledge base as infrastructure (docker-compose service + injected skill) and wires AWS S3 env vars directly into the server. The context tree explicitly classifies knowledge bases as out-of-scope for the core control plane and deferred from V1. Adding it as a core docker-compose service and an alwaysApply: true agent skill bypasses the plugin system that the tree designates as the correct extension boundary.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Knowledge base | Add shared S3-backed file store accessible to all agents | product/NODE.md Anti-Requirements: "Paperclip is explicitly not: … a knowledge base." Knowledge bases belong in plugins, not the core. | ⚠️ Conflict |
| V1 scope | Ship knowledge base capability now | product/NODE.md V1 Scope: "Explicitly deferred from V1: plugin framework/SDK, knowledge base" | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Core vs plugin boundary | New MinIO service wired directly into docker-compose server block | product/NODE.md "Thin core, rich edges — knowledge bases… go into plugins, not the core control plane." plugins/NODE.md defines the correct extension point. | ⚠️ Conflict |
| alwaysApply: true skill | Skill injected into every agent session regardless of company config | Tree governance principle: company-scoped isolation; capabilities should be opt-in per company | ❓ Partial |
| Dockerfile SHA update | Update GitHub CLI keyring hash | Routine maintenance, no tree impact | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Anti-requirements ("not a knowledge base"), thin-core principle, V1 scope defers knowledge base, "knowledge bases go into plugins"
- [plugins/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Plugin SDK + runtime as the correct extension boundary for capabilities like a knowledge base
- [infrastructure/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/NODE.md) — docker-compose is core deployment config; adding MinIO here embeds the knowledge base into every deployment

</details>

### Recommendation

Do not merge as-is. Two paths forward:

1. Defer until after V1 ships and the plugin SDK is stable, then build this as a first-party plugin (e.g. @paperclipai/plugin-file-store) using the plugin SDK's process isolation and capability gates — exactly what the tree says knowledge-base features should use.
2. Discuss with the team whether this decision should be revisited in the tree. If the project's stance on knowledge-base-in-core has changed, update product/NODE.md first, then re-open this PR with the tree as backing.

The Dockerfile SHA bump in this PR is safe; it can be cherry-picked to a separate PR if the team wants that fix now without the knowledge-base feature.

---

PR #3250 feat(memory): add memory adapter framework and UI
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open PENDING by @davison · 2026-04-10T04:51:33Z → gardener comment 2 engaging 5 other
(3 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: 6896dfb6

> [!CAUTION]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

Re-reviewed after HEAD update (7f5f43a2 → 6896dfb6). The architectural concern from the prior review is unchanged: the memory subsystem is still wired directly into server/src/services/memory-, server/src/routes/memory-, and heartbeat.ts — it has NOT been moved to the plugin system. The PR has grown and the implementation quality remains high (company-scoped, audited via memory_operations table, isolated sidecar failure modes, PARA fallback), but the core-vs-plugin question is still the blocker.

### What's new and what's unchanged

Unchanged between the two HEADs: architectural placement. server/src/services/memory-adapters/ (mempalace, PARA, sidecar), memory-hooks.ts, memory-operations.ts, memory-bindings.ts, routes/memory-bindings.ts, routes/memory-operations.ts, and heartbeat integration all still live in core.

Helpful new concrete comparison point: [PR #3285 (feat(plugins): add aws-file-store plugin with MinIO compose)](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/pull/3285) just landed and demonstrates exactly the architectural pattern the tree calls for — it re-scoped a similar "storage-backed agent capability" feature from the CONFLICT version in #3230 into a proper first-party plugin at packages/plugins/examples/aws-file-store, using the standard plugin SDK (9 tools, lifecycle hooks, capability manifest, host-owned audit logging). That's the reference implementation for how this kind of subsystem is supposed to integrate.

### Recommendation

The two recommendations from the prior review still apply:

1. Keep in core with explicit rationale — document a new product/memory/ or updated product/agent-model/ tree node that says pre-run hydration and post-run capture are fundamental to the heartbeat protocol, analogous to cost tracking. This is a deliberate scope expansion and needs tree PR first.

2. Move to a first-party plugin — use PR #3285 as the template. The MemoryAdapter interface in packages/plugins/sdk/src/types.ts is already the right abstraction. The gap is that core currently calls adapters directly; instead, a @paperclipai/plugin-mempalace (or plugin-memory-core) would register the adapter and hook into heartbeat via the plugin event/tool system. The UI pages could live in the plugin's UI slots.

The re-review is unchanged: this is a substantial feature that is technically well-built but makes an undocumented architectural decision. Either path is defensible — the decision needs to be made deliberately, not by default.

---

<sub>🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Engaging with gardener (2)

davison · 2026-04-11T05:11:07Z mentions-gardener addresses-review

## CTO Response — Memory Subsystem Architecture

Thanks for the thorough context review. I've considered both paths and am making a deliberate architectural decision:

Memory stays in core. Here's the rationale:

### Core vs. Plugin distinction

The key architectural question is whether memory lifecycle hooks (pre-run hydration, post-run capture) are orchestration concerns or agent capabilities.

They are orchestration. Specifically:

- Pre-run hydration populates agent context before the heartbeat begins — this is system-initiated, automatic, and unconditional for agents with memory bindings. The agent never "calls" it.
- Post-run capture extracts learnings after execution completes — again system-initiated, part of the run lifecycle.
- This is analogous to cost tracking and budget enforcement — also in core, also system-initiated lifecycle hooks that agents don't opt into.

### Why this differs from aws-file-store (PR #3285)

PR #3285 is the right pattern for agent-initiated tools — the agent decides when to read/write files, and the plugin registers tools the agent can call. File storage is an optional capability.

Memory hooks are the inverse: the system decides when to hydrate and capture, not the agent. Moving this to a plugin would mean the heartbeat depends on an optional plugin for a non-optional behavior — that's architecturally worse, not better.

### Where the adapter boundary lives

The MemoryAdapter interface (write, query, get, forget) IS the plugin boundary. Mempalace (ChromaDB-backed MCP) and PARA (file-based) are swappable backends registered through adapter configuration. The core owns the lifecycle; adapters own the storage.

### Documentation path

I'll document this in a docs/architecture/memory.md that captures:
- Memory as a core orchestration concern (not an agent capability)
- The adapter interface as the extensibility boundary
- Why the heartbeat lifecycle hooks are non-optional

This is a deliberate scope expansion of core, not an accidental one.

PR #3237 feat(skills): GitHub PAT support for private skill repos + delete by source
ALIGNED medium open PENDING by @cpfarhood · 2026-04-10T04:51:22Z → gardener comment 1 engaging 6 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: medium · commit: 1956ccd

> [!WARNING]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

This PR adds GitHub PAT support for private skill repos and a bulk delete-by-source endpoint. Both features are well-aligned with the adapter abstraction, governance-first, and company-scoped isolation principles. Severity is medium because PAT handling at the boundary between client and secrets vault is a sensitive pattern — the implementation looks correct but warrants a human eye on the credential lifecycle.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Secrets / PAT storage | PAT accepted at import time, stored as encrypted company secret via secretService, referenced by ID in skill metadata | Governance-first: audit trails on all mutations — PATCH /:skillId/auth logs skill_auth_updated/skill_auth_removed, import logs via existing activity system. Secrets vault already established. | ✅ Aligned |
| Company-scoped isolation | deleteBySource and updateSkillAuth both scope to companyId; route-level assertCanMutateCompanySkills guard present | All entities must be scoped to company_id — enforced at both route and DB query layers | ✅ Aligned |
| Adapter abstraction | Auth token threaded through ghFetch → all GitHub resolution helpers; retrieved from vault on subsequent calls | Agent runtimes connect through common adapter interface; auth wiring is internal to the adapter layer | ✅ Aligned |
| PAT not logged in audit details | logActivity for skill_auth_updated records only slug, not the token value | Governance: full audit trails required, but logging secrets would be a security anti-pattern — the current approach (log action + skill id, not credential) is correct | ✅ Aligned |
| Secret not deleted on PAT clear | updateSkillAuth(null) clears sourceAuthSecretId from metadata but leaves the underlying secret row | Minor lifecycle gap: orphaned secret rows accumulate. No tree decision violated, but worth a follow-up cleanup task | ⚠️ Minor gap |
| Bulk delete by source (UI) | Trash icon on source label triggers DELETE /by-source — removes all skills from a source without per-skill confirmation of agent usage | Pre-check atomicity is correct (service checks all skills before deleting any). Dialog warns user. No tree conflict. | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — governance-first, audit trails on every mutation, hard-stops as core (not optional)
- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — route/service split, secrets vault, authentication patterns

</details>

### Recommendation

Approve with one follow-up: the orphaned secret on PAT clear is a minor hygiene issue (not a blocker). Consider opening a follow-up issue to delete the underlying secret when updateSkillAuth(null) is called and no other skill references it.

---

greptile-apps[bot] · 2026-04-12T23:26:19Z

All 4 of my review threads are marked resolved, and CI is green. Here's a quick assessment:

Ready indicators:
- ✅ P1 (no confirmation dialog for delete-by-source) — resolved
- ✅ P2 (companyId filter in metadata update) — resolved
- ✅ P2 (deleteBySource atomicity + orphaned secrets) — resolved and reflected in the PR description ("pre-checks all skills for agent usage before deleting any")
- ✅ P2 (CompanySkillUpdateAuth type export) — resolved
- ✅ Snyk security check passing

Remaining checklist gaps (non-blocking, but worth noting):
- No before/after UI screenshots — the PAT field, SkillAuthSection, and delete-by-source trash icon are new UI. Screenshots would help reviewers verify the UX matches intent.
- Documentation not updated — if there are any docs covering the skills import API or secret management, they'd need updating.

If your team doesn't require screenshots and docs for this type of change, the PR looks good to request merge. The core security concern (PAT stored as encrypted secret, no plaintext exposure) and the data-safety concern (atomic delete with pre-flight agent check) are both addressed.

PR #3231 fix(opencode-local): model discovery caching, retry backoff, and heartbeat jitter
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @richmondteo-code · 2026-04-10T04:50:52Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: 7c941fe

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

This PR fixes a production thundering-herd / timeout issue in the opencode-local adapter's model discovery and in the heartbeat scheduler. Both changes are squarely within the adapter abstraction and server service layers, consistent with all tree decisions.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Adapter isolation | Model discovery caching + retry lives inside opencode-local package only | One package per adapter; adapters compose adapter-utils, don't modify core | ✅ Aligned |
| Heartbeat jitter + shuffle | Deterministic per-agent jitter and eligible-agent shuffle in heartbeat.ts | Server services own orchestration scheduling; adapters own execution | ✅ Aligned |
| Governance / audit | Retry backoff and jitter are timing concerns, not state mutations; no audit trail impact | Governance-first means mutations are logged — timing helpers are not mutations | ✅ Aligned |
| Company-scoped isolation | Changes are adapter-level and scheduler-level; no cross-company data flow introduced | All entities scoped to company_id; no scope changes here | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Adapter architecture: one package per adapter, adapter-utils as shared library, adapters do not own scheduling
- [engineering/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/NODE.md) — Service-oriented backend; services accept db and config as function arguments
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Surface problems, don't hide them; control plane, not execution plane

</details>

### Recommendation

Safe to merge. The jitter formula adds up to 10% delay on top of the configured interval (not before it), so heartbeats will never fire earlier than scheduled — reviewers should confirm that is the intended semantics, but it has no context-tree-level conflict.

PR #3252 feat: board escalation - boardAttentionAt field, inbox filters, and noise reduction
ALIGNED medium closed AUTHOR WITHDRAWN by @yunwei-zh · 2026-04-10T04:49:15Z → gardener comment
(2 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: medium · commit: b3f660b

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

This PR adds a boardAttentionAt escalation primitive that lets agents signal "I need a human" without side-effecting issue status or assignee, and cleans up Board inbox noise by adding focused filter categories (Escalated, In Review, Assigned to me), hiding terminal-status issues from the Board default view, and moving the "Hide agent failures" toggle into an Options popover. The changes are well-aligned with Paperclip's governance-first and surface-problems-don't-hide-them principles, and the progressive-disclosure approach (amber banner in issue detail + filter categories in inbox) fits the thin-core/rich-edges pattern.

Note on Greptile's P1/P2 findings: After reading the diff directly, the Greptile P1 ("dismiss does not clear escalation globally") and P2 ("missing filter categories / localStorage persistence") are inaccurate for this version of the PR. The archiveIssueMutation.mutationFn correctly calls issuesApi.update(id, { boardAttentionAt: null }) before archiving when the issue is escalated. The escalations, in_review_issues, and assigned_to_me filter categories exist in InboxCategoryFilter (moved to ui/src/lib/inbox.ts), and saveInboxCategoryFilter is called on every filter change. The escalation badge test is also present in inbox.test.ts. Greptile appears to have evaluated an earlier version or missed the type definition move.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Governance / Board control surface | Add a channel for agents to signal "I need a human" without side-effecting task status | Governance node: "The Board is not just an approval gate — it is a live control surface. The human can intervene at any level at any time." Board powers always available. | ✅ Aligned |
| Auditable everything | boardAttentionAt is a field on the issue, mutated via the same PATCH endpoint; clears globally when board user dismisses | Governance node: "Every mutation writes to activity_log. The system is designed so that nothing happens silently." | ✅ Aligned |
| Board-only clear guard | Non-board actors return 403 when attempting to set boardAttentionAt: null | Governance node: "Governance rules are control-plane invariants enforced at the server, not at the adapter or agent level." | ✅ Aligned |
| Tasks as communication channel | Agents escalate via boardAttentionAt on an existing issue rather than through a side-channel | Task system node: "All agent communication flows through the task system. There is no separate messaging or chat system." | ✅ Aligned |
| Surface problems, don't hide them | Amber escalation banner in issue detail, red dot badge for escalations, terminal-status Board cleanup | Tree principle: "Surface problems, don't hide them" | ✅ Aligned |
| Progressive disclosure | Escalation summary in inbox badge → filter categories → amber banner in issue detail with Clear action | Tree principle: "Progressive disclosure (summary → checklist → raw logs)" | ✅ Aligned |
| Company-scoped isolation | No cross-company data; boardQueueUserId filter is scoped via companyId from the existing route guard | Tree decision: "All entities scoped to company_id. Never leak data across companies." | ✅ Aligned |
| Dismiss side-effect risk | If issuesApi.update succeeds but archiveFromInbox fails, escalation is already cleared — intentional per PR | PR documents this as "Acceptable — the issue stays visible and the user can re-dismiss." This is a reasonable UX tradeoff; not a governance concern. | ✅ Aligned |
| Clear button visible to all actors on IssueDetail | Board-only guard is server-side; agents viewing their own issue detail could see a "Clear" button that returns 403 on click | Governance: "Unified API, Different Authorization." The server guard is correct, but the UI shows a control that silently fails for non-board users — a minor UX inconsistency worth noting. | ❓ Partial |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board powers, approval gates, budget controls, audit trail requirements
- [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Tasks as the communication channel; no side-channel messaging

</details>

### Recommendation

Ship with one minor follow-up to consider: the "Clear" button in IssueDetail.tsx is rendered for all viewers of an escalated issue, but it silently fails (403) for non-board actors. Since agents can view their own issues, they'll see a button they can't use. A lightweight client-side guard (hide the button unless the current session is a board user) would be consistent with Paperclip's "surface problems, don't hide them" principle — surface the escalation state, but don't show a control that isn't available. This is low-severity and can be done as a follow-up.

PR #3262 feat: Company Files — scoped knowledge base with UI, API, and agent integration
CONFLICT high open PENDING by @vinccirom · 2026-04-10T04:47:44Z → gardener comment 1 engaging
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: \CONFLICT\ · severity: \high\ · commit: \a4690b3\

> [!WARNING]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

This PR adds a knowledge base as a first-class core feature (DB table, 10 REST endpoints, heartbeat injection across all 6 adapters, UI page), which directly contradicts three explicit tree decisions: knowledge bases belong in plugins not the core control plane, Paperclip is explicitly "not a knowledge base", and knowledge base was deferred from V1 scope.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Knowledge base in core | Adds knowledge_entries table, service, and 10 REST routes directly into server core | "Thin core, rich edges. Knowledge bases… go into plugins — not the core control plane." (product/NODE.md) | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Paperclip identity | Positions knowledge base as a first-class Paperclip feature | "Paperclip is explicitly not: …a knowledge base…" (product/NODE.md) | ⚠️ Conflict |
| V1 scope | Ships as a new core feature | "Explicitly deferred from V1: …knowledge base…" (product/NODE.md) | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Adapter integration | Injects knowledge manifest into all 6 adapter heartbeat prompts | Adapter abstraction is aligned; the question is whether the data source should be core vs plugin | ❓ Partial |
| Company-scoped isolation | All queries are scoped to company_id; auth follows existing assertCompanyAccess pattern | Company-scoped isolation (engineering/NODE.md) | ✅ Aligned |
| Audit trail | All mutations call logActivity with before/after details | Governance-first; every mutation logged (product/NODE.md) | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Product strategy, anti-requirements, V1 scope; explicitly lists knowledge base as out-of-scope for core and for V1
- [engineering/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/NODE.md) — Backend architecture; service-oriented pattern; company-scoped isolation

</details>

### Recommendation

This work is solid in implementation quality — the scoping, audit trail, and adapter integration are all well-executed. The conflict is with where it lives, not whether it should exist.

Two paths forward:

1. Move to a plugin (tree-aligned): Extract the knowledge base into a plugin using the Plugin SDK. The heartbeat injection via renderPaperclipKnowledgePrompt and the knowledge service logic can live in a plugin that registers routes, injects into heartbeat context, and owns the DB migration. The tree's "thin core, rich edges" decision exists precisely to protect the control plane from scope creep.

2. Update the tree first (if the team has decided knowledge base is now core): If product has deliberately changed direction on this, update product/NODE.md to remove the anti-requirement and un-defer knowledge base from V1 scope — then land this PR. That PR should be discussed and merged before this code lands.

Either way, this PR should not merge until the conflict is resolved. The tree is the decision record; the code should follow from it.

---

<sub>Reviewed commit: <code>a4690b3</code> · Tree snapshot: <code>33dc6fd</code> · Commands: <code>@gardener re-review</code> · <code>@gardener pause</code> · <code>@gardener ignore</code></sub>

<sub>🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.</sub>

Engaging with gardener (1)

vinccirom · 2026-04-10T06:11:18Z mentions-gardener addresses-review

## Response to gardener context review

We investigated the plugin path thoroughly. After an exhaustive audit of the Plugin SDK (41 capabilities, 13 UI slot types, all data persistence APIs), the plugin architecture cannot support a company file system without significant degradation:

| Requirement | Core | Plugin SDK | Impact |
|-------------|------|-----------|--------|
| Folder hierarchy with referential integrity | ✅ Self-referencing parentId FK | ❌ Flat JSONB with parentId field | No cascade delete, no tree integrity |
| Relational data model (FKs to documents, assets, agents) | ✅ knowledge_entries table | ❌ plugin_entities JSONB blobs only | No referential integrity, no indexes |
| Document revision tracking | ✅ Reuses documents + document_revisions | ❌ Manual versioning in JSONB | No audit trail on document changes |
| File upload + storage | ✅ multer + StorageService | ❌ "Future scope" per plugin spec | No binary files at all |
| Agent context awareness | ✅ Lightweight manifest in heartbeat | ❌ Agent must explicitly call tool | Agents don't know files exist unless they search |
| Department scoping via org tree | ✅ Recursive CTE on agents.reportsTo | ❌ No SQL access | Cannot efficiently resolve subtrees |
| Custom REST API (10 endpoints) | ✅ Express routes | ❌ Only webhook + bridge proxy | No direct API |

### The case for core

The gardener classifies this as "a knowledge base." It isn't. It's a company file system — structured folders and documents scoped to company, department, and agent.

Every real organization has files. Not a knowledge base product, not a wiki, not Notion — just a structured place where persistent company files live. This is as fundamental as having an org chart or a budget. A company without a file system is incomplete infrastructure.

- Org charts are core → they define who reports to whom
- Budgets are core → they define spending limits
- Approval policies are core → they define governance gates
- Company files should be core → they define everything else the company needs to persist

The plugin system can't provide this because it was designed for external integrations (Linear sync, GitHub webhooks) — not organizational primitives. The gaps (no schema, no hierarchy, no uploads, no revision tracking) prove the point.

### Tree PR

Per the gardener's guidance, we've opened a PR to update the context tree:

[serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree#1](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/pull/1) — proposes distinguishing "general-purpose knowledge base / wiki" (still deferred) from "company file system" (foundational infrastructure).

Happy to hear the team's perspective.

PR #3265 Fix plugin page routes doubling company prefix on company switch
NEEDS_REVIEW medium closed AUTHOR WITHDRAWN by @harperaa · 2026-04-10T04:46:00Z → gardener comment 1 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

> [!WARNING]
> gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: ccd7776

---

## Summary

This PR is titled as a bug fix for plugin page route prefix-doubling, and that fix is correct and aligned. However, the PR bundles four substantial and largely unrelated changes under a misleading title: a new BastionClaw adapter (filesystem IPC + SQLite polling), a company-level kill switch (pause/resume), a heartbeat model override feature, and the actual bug fix. The BastionClaw adapter introduces a new architectural pattern not recorded in the context tree, and there is a SQL injection risk in the adapter's polling loop.

---

## Context Match

| Area | Tree | PR | Match |
|---|---|---|---|
| Plugin page route fix (company-routes.ts) | Plugin extensibility is core; routing correctness required | Correctly strips company prefix for any non-global segment | ✅ Aligned |
| Company pause/resume (kill switch) | Governance: "Board can pause/resume any Agent immediately"; company lifecycle has paused status | Board-only POST /:companyId/pause + POST /:companyId/resume, cancels active runs | ✅ Aligned |
| Heartbeat skips paused companies | Company lifecycle: "Paused companies stop all heartbeats" | tickTimers now queries paused companies and skips their agents | ✅ Aligned |
| BastionClaw adapter pattern | Tree lists gateway adapters as WebSocket-based (openclaw-gateway); "gateway adapters connect to a remote agent over WebSocket" | BastionClaw uses filesystem IPC + SQLite polling — not WebSocket | ⚠️ New Territory |
| Heartbeat model override | No tree node records adapter-level model override at heartbeat invocation | heartbeat.model added to heartbeat policy, injected into runtimeConfig on timer runs only | ⚠️ New Territory |

---

## Issues

### 1. SQL injection risk in BastionClaw adapter (medium severity)

In packages/adapters/bastionclaw/src/server/execute.ts, the task poll query interpolates taskId directly into an SQL string with only single-quote escaping:

```ts
SELECT status, result, error, duration_ms FROM task_run_logs WHERE task_id = '${taskId.replace(/'/g, "''")}' ORDER BY run_at DESC LIMIT 1
```

taskId is constructed as paperclip-${ctx.runId} where ctx.runId comes from the server. While the server controls runId today, this pattern bypasses parameterized queries and creates a brittle trust assumption. The sqlite3 CLI used here supports parameterized queries via -cmd. This should use a parameterized form or at minimum assert that taskId contains only safe characters before interpolation.

### 2. PR bundles four unrelated changes under a misleading title (low-medium)

The title "Fix plugin page routes doubling company prefix on company switch" covers only ~10 lines of the 3,800-line diff. The rest introduces: a new adapter, a company kill switch feature, a heartbeat model override, GitHub issue templates, and a SECURITY.md. Bundling makes review harder and commit history less useful. These should be separate PRs.

### 3. BastionClaw adapter pattern is not in the tree (new territory)

The adapters tree node defines two patterns: local (spawns a child process) and gateway (WebSocket). BastionClaw uses a third pattern: filesystem IPC + SQLite polling. The adapter writes a JSON task file to a shared directory and polls a SQLite DB for completion. This is a novel integration pattern with meaningful tradeoffs (local-only, polling latency, SQLite lock sensitivity) that should be captured in the tree before the adapter ships, so future adapters don't re-litigate this design.

### 4. models: [] in BastionClaw index.ts

packages/adapters/bastionclaw/src/index.ts exports models: []. The tree notes that adapters report model info via detectModel. If BastionClaw can detect the model from the Claude instance it dispatches to, this should be wired up rather than left empty.

---

## Tree Nodes Referenced

- adapters/NODE.md — local vs gateway pattern, ServerAdapterModule interface
- adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md — gateway adapter precedent
- product/governance/NODE.md — board powers, pause/resume, budget hard-stops
- product/company-model/NODE.md — company lifecycle (active/paused/archived)
- product/NODE.md — control plane principles

---

## Recommendation

The routing fix and the governance/heartbeat changes are aligned and look correct. The BastionClaw adapter is new territory worth flagging:

1. Fix the SQL injection before merge — use parameterized queries or character-validation on taskId.
2. Confirm the IPC+SQLite pattern is intentional and draft a adapters/bastionclaw/NODE.md tree node capturing the design rationale and constraints (local-only, SQLite polling, requires BastionClaw to be co-located).
3. Consider splitting the PR — the bug fix and the BastionClaw adapter are independent and the bundling makes each harder to review.

<sub>Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore</sub>

Reviews against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree).</sub>

<sub>Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore</sub>

<sub>🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree).</sub>

Engaging with gardener (1)

ndholakia · 2026-04-10T15:10:51Z addresses-review

Thanks for the thorough review. Addressed all three points in the latest commit (df09acb):

1. heartbeat.enabled default — This was NOT changed by this PR. Confirmed by checking git show master:server/src/services/heartbeat.ts — the default was already true in upstream master before our changes.

2. Cooldown blocks on-demand wakeups — Fixed. The cooldown now peeks at the next queued run's invocationSource. If it's on_demand (UI button), the cooldown is bypassed entirely. Only automated/timer-sourced runs are throttled.

3. Hardcoded 60s ignores intervalSec — Fixed. MIN_GAP_MS now derives from the agent's policy.intervalSec, capped at 60s: Math.min(60_000, policy.intervalSec > 0 ? policy.intervalSec * 1000 : 60_000). Agents with 30s intervals won't be throttled below their configured rate.

Issue #3168 Orphaned runs block agent queue: queued runs not cancelled when issue closes + reaper skips zombie processes
ALIGNED low open by @vzert · 2026-04-10T00:29:01Z → gardener comment 1 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · type: issue

Aligned with context tree. This issue describes both halves of the orphaned-run problem: queued runs not cancelled when issues close, AND the orphan reaper skipping zombie processes. Three complementary fixes are in flight, and together they address the full failure mode you describe:

- PR #3200 — cancels orphaned queued/running runs when issue is reassigned or closed (event-triggered cleanup)
- PR #3190 — clears stale executionRunId lock metadata when issue leaves in_progress (event-triggered cleanup)
- PR #3218 — SIGTERM→SIGKILL orphaned PIDs in the stale-run reaper, marks them failed (background-triggered cleanup, addresses the "process alive but hung" half)

Gardener flagged all three as NEEDS_REVIEW because they sit close to the [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) "no silent auto-recovery" principle and the boundary between event-triggered cleanup (likely allowed) and background-driven recovery (likely not) is not yet codified in the tree.

### Recommendation

- The three PRs should be coordinated and ideally land together or in a known order to avoid leaving the system in a half-cleaned state.
- Once they land, this issue can be closed.
- Strongest tree-update suggestion in this iteration: product/task-system/NODE.md should add an explicit decision on auto-cleanup vs auto-recovery boundaries. Three orphan-cleanup PRs in one batch is a clear signal the tree is missing a load-bearing decision.

<sub>Reviewed: <code>2026-04-10T00:29:01Z</code> · Tree snapshot: <code>ffda000b</code> · Commands: <code>@gardener re-review</code> · <code>@gardener pause</code> · <code>@gardener ignore</code></sub>

1 other reply (not engaging with gardener)
JaimeBadiola · 2026-04-14T05:04:04Z

Adding a fresh reproduction from production use — this issue just bit us hard and cost several hours of debugging + a blocked pilot launch.

## Repro / timeline

- Agent: CTO (claude_local, sonnet, heartbeat enabled)
- Task: debug a React hooks violation causing production white-page
- Heartbeat run dbf916f3 started 2026-04-14T04:45:15 via manual invoke
- Agent loaded cache, cloned repo, ran eslint, got tool result back at 04:57:44
- Then complete silence — no more stdout, no assistant response, no crash
- Paperclip DB still shows status: running, exitCode: null, finishedAt: null
- Child claude --print process (PID 83234) stayed alive at 0.3% CPU, 190MB RSS, for 70+ minutes before I killed it manually
- Meanwhile the next manually-invoked heartbeat 005657c5 sat in queued — never started — because HEARTBEAT_MAX_CONCURRENT_RUNS_DEFAULT = 1 and the stuck run held the slot.

The reaper ran every 5 min per index.ts:615 with staleThresholdMs: 5 60 1000, but reapOrphanedRuns at heartbeat.ts:2395 skips when processPidAlive === true. There is no max-run-duration cap anywhere in the reaper, so a silent-but-alive child blocks the queue forever.


heartbeat.ts:2421
  if (processPidAlive) {
    // mark as DETACHED_PROCESS_ERROR, continue — never reaps a live PID
    continue;
  }

## Impact

- Agent queue frozen for the duration (here: 70+ min, until human intervention)
- No clear signal to the operator — UI shows "running" indefinitely
- With maxConcurrentRuns: 1, one hung Claude CLI = one agent effectively dead
- Downstream issues blocked (in our case: a critical white-page fix that was already committed locally but the push never ran because the agent session hung)

## What claude --print was stuck on

Last observed tool use: Bash → npx eslint .... Result returned cleanly (is_error: false). After that, silence. Could be:

- A long-poll to the Anthropic API that never returned
- Backpressured stdout if the parent consumer stopped reading
- An un-broken MCP connection

The relevant observation is that Paperclip can't tell the difference and has no watchdog.

## Why PR #3218 mattered

#3218 would have ended this quickly — the stale-run reaper SIGTERM→SIGKILL would have marked dbf916f3 as failed at the 5-min mark and let the queue advance. It was closed unmerged pending the "scheduled auto-recovery vs no-silent-recovery" tree decision. From an operator's perspective:

- This is not silent auto-recovery if the reaper writes a failed status + log entry + error code. That IS surfacing the problem — loudly, at DB level, visible in the UI, with the PID and duration recorded.
- The silent failure is the status quo — a run that stays running forever with no log events, no error, no clock, nothing. That is the actual "hidden auto-recovery" (hiding the fact that the agent is dead).

Suggest the tree node revisit that framing: reaping a 15-min-idle process and marking it failed is closer to "surface the problem" than leaving it as a zombie.

## What we want (prioritised)

1. Max-run-duration cap in reapOrphanedRuns with a sensible default (15 min?) and an env var override. This is the smallest possible fix and would unblock essentially all queue-deadlock reports in this thread.
2. Bonus: expose lastStdoutAt on the heartbeat run so operators can see "running but idle for N min" in the UI, even without changing reaper behavior.
3. Longer term: the lastStdoutAt + a watchdog would let us reap based on inactivity rather than absolute duration, which is probably the better semantic.

Happy to open a fresh PR along the lines of #3218 if the maintainers signal they'd accept it with the failed status + log emission framing. Or to try option 2 first if that's less contentious.

Paperclip commit we hit this on: b649bd4 (tip of main as of ~2 days ago). Workspace: claude_local adapter, standard heartbeat settings.

Issue #3219 Docker build fails: hardcoded GitHub CLI GPG key checksum is outdated (sha256sum mismatch)
ALIGNED low open by @pascalsz · 2026-04-10T00:28:45Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · type: issue

Aligned with context tree. This issue documents the well-known Docker build failure caused by GitHub's GPG signing key rotation. Two candidate fixes are in flight, both updating the same hardcoded SHA256 in the Dockerfile:

- PR #3234 ("fix: update GitHub CLI GPG keyring checksum") — preferred: cleaner body and verification steps
- PR #3212 ("Github signing key rotation") — duplicate fix from a different contributor

The fix matches the [infrastructure/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/NODE.md) Dockerfile + supply-chain-verification concerns.

### Recommendation

- Merge #3234 (or #3212), close the other.
- Once landed, this issue can be closed.
- Tree-update suggestion: infrastructure/NODE.md already mentions hardcoded checksums as a gotcha; consider documenting "rotate the checksum when GitHub rotates their signing key" as a recurring maintenance task, or switching to an unpinned-but-verified install pattern long-term so this doesn't recur quarterly.

PR #3214 Tighten social-agent cost discipline
CONFLICT high open PENDING by @natelion · 2026-04-10T00:28:29Z → gardener comment 1 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: CONFLICT · severity: high · commit: b8b4b5fe

> [!WARNING]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

The PR title is "Tighten social-agent cost discipline." The actual diff is 666 additions across 7 files, breaking down as:

1. 488 LOC of company-specific seed data and agent prompts (agents/instructions/brand-lead.md, chief-of-staff.md, lead-gen-agent.md, ops-agent.md, social-agent.md, and scripts/seed-truwitz.ts) — a complete agent roster + seed script for a specific company called "truwitz".
2. Two major undocumented core changes in server/src/services/heartbeat.ts:
- Global 15-minute heartbeat execution timeout (HEARTBEAT_EXECUTION_TIMEOUT_MS)
- Per-agent circuit breaker with exponential backoff (CB_FAILURE_THRESHOLD, CB_MAX_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER, CircuitBreakerState stored in agentRuntimeState.stateJson)

Neither core change is mentioned in the PR title or summary, and both are policy decisions that affect every Paperclip deployment, not just the social agent.

Two distinct tree-conflict concerns below.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Company-specific seed data in core | seed-truwitz.ts + 5 truwitz agent instruction files in main repo | [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) Anti-requirements: "Paperclip is explicitly not: an agent runtime, a knowledge base, ... or a complete Jira/GitHub replacement" + "Paperclip is infrastructure, not the company itself" | ⚠️ Conflict |
| Hidden core policy: 15min heartbeat timeout | Adds a global execution timeout cap not mentioned in title/summary | [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) compaction thresholds reference suggests timeout policy is core behavior worth declaring on the tree | ⚠️ Conflict — undocumented policy change |
| Hidden core policy: per-agent circuit breaker | Exponential heartbeat backoff after 3 consecutive failures | [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) "Surface problems, don't hide them. No silent auto-recovery." — automatic backoff IS auto-recovery | ⚠️ Conflict |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — anti-requirements, control plane vs company
- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them" / no silent auto-recovery
- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — adapter timeout pattern

### Recommendation

This PR should be split into three before merging, in this order:

1. A separate "core: per-agent circuit breaker for heartbeat failures" PR that explicitly proposes the auto-backoff policy. This is a governance-relevant decision (auto-recovery), needs a tree update before it lands, and absolutely should not ship hidden inside a "tighten social-agent" PR. The 15-minute global heartbeat timeout decision belongs in the same PR.
2. A separate "scripts: truwitz company seed" PR in a examples/ or seeds/ directory if the maintainer is OK with example seeds in the core repo at all. My read of [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) is that this content does not belong in paperclipai/paperclip at all — Paperclip is the control plane, not the company. Truwitz should be its own repo using Paperclip as a dependency, with seed data living there.
3. The actual social-agent calendar throttle (whatever code change the title describes) — but I don't see a corresponding code change in the diff. The 666 LOC are all seeds + instructions + heartbeat policy. Either the calendar throttle is missing or it's hidden inside one of the markdown instruction files. If it's in a .md instruction file, that's a documentation/prompt update, not a code change, and the PR title overstates what it does.

Pulling this apart will let the governance-affecting parts get the review they deserve and keep the core repo from accreting company-specific seeds.

---

PR #3218 fix(heartbeat): kill orphaned agent processes after server restart
NEEDS_REVIEW medium closed AUTHOR WITHDRAWN by @bartrtl · 2026-04-10T00:27:51Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: e733cee0

> [!CAUTION]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

Closes #3215. Adds killOrphanedPid() (SIGTERM → SIGKILL after 5s grace), wires it as a fallback in cancelRunInternal and cancelActiveForAgentInternal when the in-memory ChildProcess handle is gone, and changes the stale-run reaper to actually kill orphaned processes and mark runs failed.

This is the third PR in the queue addressing the orphaned-execution-cleanup pattern (after #3200 cancels orphaned queued runs on reassign/close, and #3190 clears stale executionRunId lock metadata). The trio is internally consistent and complementary, but they should be coordinated.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Reaper kills, doesn't observe | Stale-run reaper now SIGKILLs orphans and marks runs failed | [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) principle 6: "Surface problems, don't hide them. No silent auto-recovery." — but a documented timeout-based reaper is closer to "surfacing a problem as a failure" than to "hiding it" | ❓ Partial |
| SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL grace | 5s grace period before hard kill | Standard process-management pattern | ✅ Aligned |
| Trigger | Reaper runs on a schedule (background); kill is unattended | Tree hasn't drawn the line on whether scheduled cleanup is "auto-recovery" | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them"
- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — heartbeat path
- (Suggested new) product/task-system/NODE.md — explicit auto-cleanup boundary

### Recommendation

This is the third PR I've flagged on the same auto-cleanup-vs-auto-recovery question (#3200, #3190, #3218). The boundary needs to be drawn explicitly in the tree before more PRs in this category arrive.

1. Coordinate with #3200 and #3190. All three PRs touch the orphaned-run cleanup path:
- #3200 — cancel queued runs when issue is reassigned/closed (event-triggered)
- #3190 — clear stale executionRunId metadata on issue state transitions (event-triggered)
- #3218 — SIGKILL orphaned PIDs in scheduled reaper (background-triggered)
The first two are gated on explicit human/agent state changes. #3218 is the first one that's purely background-driven — that's a meaningful difference for the "no silent auto-recovery" principle and worth a maintainer decision.
2. The stale-run reaper marking runs failed is a behavior change worth flagging in the changelog. Operators monitoring run status will see failed runs that previously appeared as running-with-warning-log.
3. Add a tree node. product/task-system/NODE.md should codify when scheduled cleanup is allowed vs. forbidden. This category of PR will keep arriving until the tree has an opinion.

Otherwise the implementation is well-scoped and tested.

---

Issue #3173 Bug: timeoutSec: 0 causes heartbeat runs to hang forever with no recovery
ALIGNED low open by @rudyjellis · 2026-04-10T00:23:28Z → gardener comment 1 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · type: issue

Aligned with context tree. This issue documents the well-defined timeoutSec=0 infinite-hang bug in codex_local (and other adapters that default timeoutSec to 0). A candidate fix is in flight: PR #3186 ("fix: apply 30-minute default timeout when timeoutSec is 0") closes this issue by treating timeoutSec <= 0 as "use default 30min" across codex-local, cursor-local, and process adapter execute functions.

The fix surfaces the hang as a timeout instead of an indefinite block, aligning with [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) principle 6 ("Surface problems, don't hide them"). Gardener flagged PR #3186 as NEEDS_REVIEW/low because the same guard is duplicated across 3 adapter packages — see the gardener comment on #3186 for the suggestion to lift the default into adapter-utils.

### Recommendation

- Track PR #3186 as the canonical fix.
- Once #3186 lands (ideally with the adapter-utils lift discussed there), this issue can be closed.
- Consider adding the "default adapter timeout" decision to [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) so future adapters inherit the rule automatically.

<sub>Reviewed: <code>2026-04-10T00:25:20Z</code> · Tree snapshot: <code>ffda000b</code> · Commands: <code>@gardener re-review</code> · <code>@gardener pause</code> · <code>@gardener ignore</code></sub>

1 other reply (not engaging with gardener)
Thelastlineofcode · 2026-04-10T15:27:37Z

Implemented the Codex local timeout safety net.

What changed:
- codex_local now treats timeoutSec: 0 as unset and falls back to a 600s default
- the UI config builder now emits 600s instead of 0 for the default timeout
- this prevents the default Codex local config from hanging forever when the child process stops exiting cleanly

Validation:
- pnpm --filter @paperclipai/adapter-codex-local typecheck ✅

Issue #3208 OpenClaw adapter still sends 'paperclip' property despite fix in PR #626
ALIGNED low open by @confersolutions · 2026-04-10T00:23:14Z → gardener comment

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · type: issue

Aligned with context tree. This issue documents a regression of the OpenClaw paperclip root-property fix from PR #626. A candidate fix is already in flight: PR #3245 ("fix: strip paperclip payload from agent params") explicitly addresses this issue along with #3089 and #617. PR #3245 hardens the OpenClaw gateway adapter to always strip agentParams.paperclip before sending the gateway request, removes the dead paperclipPayload path, updates docs to state the actual contract, and adds regression coverage.

The fix matches the [adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md) protocol-boundary contract and the broader [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) "adapters parse agent output INTO shared transcript types" decision.

### Recommendation

- Track PR #3245 as the canonical fix.
- Once #3245 lands, this issue can be closed.
- The reproducer in this issue (with redacted payload) would be valuable as a fixture in the OpenClaw gateway adapter test suite — consider attaching to PR #3245 if not already covered.

PR #3194 fix: keep opencode tool errors from failing runs
ALIGNED medium open PENDING by @rbarinov · 2026-04-10T00:22:58Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: medium · commit: 8934b3d4

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

Separates tool_use errors from terminal adapter errors in the OpenCode JSONL parser. Closes #3193. Tool failures no longer get promoted into fatal errorMessage, so opencode_local run status reflects actual adapter/runtime failure instead of recoverable tool errors. Aligned in spirit with multiple tree decisions.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Per-adapter parsing | parsePiJsonl-style: each adapter parses its own stream into TranscriptEntry types | [adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md) + [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md): "Adapters parse agent output INTO the shared TranscriptEntry types" | ✅ Aligned |
| Surface vs hide | A tool error is a recoverable signal; promoting it to fatal is hiding the real success state behind a false failure | [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) principle 6: "Surface problems, don't hide them" — and the inverse: don't manufacture problems where there aren't any | ✅ Aligned |
| Test coverage | Parser-level + server-level regression tests | [infrastructure/testing/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/infrastructure/testing/NODE.md) | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md) — JSONL parser, output classification
- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — adapter parses INTO shared transcript types
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — surface vs hide

### Recommendation

Looks good to merge after Greptile/CodeRabbit clears code correctness. Closes #3193 (which is also in the open queue). One thought: this same "tool error vs adapter error" distinction probably applies to other adapters (claude-local, gemini-local, codex-local). Worth considering whether the classification should be lifted into a shared adapter-utils helper so each adapter parser doesn't have to re-derive the rule. Otherwise the [adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) "compose utilities" decision risks drift across adapters.

---
