PR #3246 feat: add copilot_local adapter for GitHub Copilot subscribers
ALIGNED medium open PENDING by @oespinozai · 2026-04-10T00:22:06Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: medium · commit: 50aa0fde

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

New copilot_local adapter that routes Paperclip agent runs through GitHub Copilot's API endpoint, reusing the Claude CLI binary for Anthropic models and an OpenAI-compat path for non-Claude models. Aligns with the adapter tree's "one package per adapter" + "control plane, not execution plane" decisions. Two non-blocking flags below.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Duplicate alert: PR #3187 is titled "docs: add gh cli quickstart" but actually adds the same copilot_local adapter package (542+317+407+544 LOC across the same files), plus 367 LOC of test changes. The maintainer needs to pick one and close the other. See gardener comment on #3187.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Adapter package layout | New packages/adapters/copilot-local/ with server/, cli/, ui/, shared/ | [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md): three-layer package structure | ✅ Aligned |
| Control plane boundary | Adapter shells out to claude CLI; Paperclip never runs the LLM directly | [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) decision 1: "Control plane, not execution plane" | ✅ Aligned |
| Local-vs-gateway pattern | Local adapter spawns child process | [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) "Local vs Gateway Pattern" | ✅ Aligned |
| Model list | Hardcoded 7-model list | [adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md) prefers dynamic model discovery; static is acceptable but not ideal | ❓ Partial |
| Auth chain | GITHUB_COPILOT_TOKEN → GITHUB_TOKEN → gh auth token | No tree guidance on auth precedence — worth documenting | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — package structure, local-vs-gateway, model discovery
- [adapters/claude-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/claude-local/NODE.md) — Claude CLI host
- [adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/opencode-local/NODE.md) — dynamic model discovery pattern
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — control plane boundary

### Recommendation

Aligned in spirit. Three things before/after merge:

1. Resolve the dup with #3187 first. That PR adds the same adapter package under a misleading "docs" title. Whichever PR has cleaner code review wins; close the other.
2. Consider dynamic model discovery. The OpenCode and Pi adapters both ship discover*Models() patterns because their model lists are provider-driven. Copilot's available models are also subscription-driven (Pro/Business/Enterprise have different access). Hardcoding 7 models will drift; querying Copilot's /models endpoint at startup is more in line with the rest of the adapter family.
3. Document the auth-resolution chain. Add an adapters/copilot-local/NODE.md tree node once the package lands, including the auth precedence and the rationale (why GITHUB_COPILOT_TOKEN over GITHUB_TOKEN over gh auth token). This is exactly the kind of cross-adapter constraint that belongs on the tree.

---

PR #3192 fix: normalize managed AGENTS.md companion links
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @rbarinov · 2026-04-10T00:18:07Z → gardener comment
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: ad6f2d68

Aligned with context tree. Closes #3191. Normalizes legacy $AGENT_HOME/... companion references to bundle-relative ./... form, but only for managed instruction bundles — external bundles untouched. Heals already-materialized managed bundles during recovery and writes the normalized form on materialization. Matches the [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) "skill injection via symlinks into a temporary .claude/skills/" pattern (managed = paperclip-owned, external = user-owned, so paperclip can rewrite the former but must leave the latter alone). The managed/external split is exactly the right boundary. Adds regression tests for healing, external no-op, idempotence, and route-level materialization. No product/context conflict.

PR #3190 fix(server): clear stale issue execution locks
NEEDS_REVIEW medium open PENDING by @hutchic · 2026-04-10T00:17:53Z → gardener comment
(2 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW · severity: medium · commit: 25ac8ad3

> [!CAUTION]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

Clears executionRunId lock metadata when an issue leaves in_progress or is reassigned. This is the second auto-cleanup PR in this batch (after #3200 which cancels orphaned heartbeat runs). Both sit in the same gray zone relative to the tree's "no silent auto-recovery" principle — same flag, same recommendation: confirm the boundary explicitly.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Lock cleanup | Clear executionRunId when status leaves in_progress or assignee changes | [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) principle 6: "Surface problems, don't hide them. No silent auto-recovery." | ❓ Partial |
| Trigger | Cleanup is gated on explicit human/agent state change (status leaving in_progress or reassignment), not on background reaper | Tree allows "good auditing → right entity decides what to do" | ✅ Aligned |
| Test coverage | Adds issues-service-update-execution-lock.test.ts | Tree values regression tests on state-machine transitions | ✅ Aligned |
| Pair with #3200 | Both fix orphaned-execution lock state with the same gating principle | No tree node yet for the auto-cleanup boundary | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them"
- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — issues-service patterns
- (Suggested new) product/task-system/NODE.md — auto-cleanup vs auto-recovery boundary

### Recommendation

Two suggestions before merging:

1. Coordinate with PR #3200. That PR cancels orphaned queued/running heartbeat runs on the same trigger (issue reassign/close). #3190 clears the lock metadata. The two are complementary halves of the same cleanup story — should they land together as one PR, or in a known order to avoid leaving the system in a half-cleaned state?
2. Add a tree node. I've now flagged this same boundary twice in the queue (#3200, #3190). The implicit rule appears to be: "Cleanup of dead state is allowed when explicit human/agent action triggers it; background auto-recovery is not." That's a real product-level decision worth codifying in product/task-system/NODE.md so future contributors don't have to re-derive it. If a tree owner can confirm the rule, gardener can stop flagging these.
3. PR body uses internal SUPAAAA-475/466 refs. External reviewers won't have context — a one-line summary of the failure mode would help downstream review.

Otherwise aligned. The change is well-scoped, tested, and the trigger gating is the right shape.

---

greptile-apps[bot] · 2026-04-16T07:13:52Z

<h3>Greptile Summary</h3>

This PR widens the GET /api/agents/me/inbox-lite query to include routine execution issues (via includeRoutineExecutions: true) so the compact inbox view no longer diverges from the heartbeat and direct issue routes, and adds a route-level regression test to lock in that behavior. The change is a one-line addition with no impact on auth scoping (results are still filtered to the authenticated agent).

<h3>Confidence Score: 5/5</h3>

Safe to merge — minimal, well-scoped change with solid regression coverage and no auth or data-integrity risk.

The only change to production code is adding includeRoutineExecutions: true to an existing scoped query. Auth is unchanged, results are still filtered to the calling agent, and the new test verifies both the service call arguments and the full response shape. No P0/P1 findings.

No files require special attention.

<h3>Important Files Changed</h3>


| Filename | Overview |
|----------|----------|
| server/src/routes/agents.ts | Adds includeRoutineExecutions: true to the inbox-lite issueService.list call — a single-line, targeted fix with no side-effects on auth or scoping. |
| server/src/__tests__/agent-permissions-routes.test.ts | Adds a new route-level regression test verifying that inbox-lite calls issueService.list with includeRoutineExecutions: true and maps the full compact response shape including activeRun. |

</details>

PR #3240 fix(db): add UNIQUE constraint to agent_api_keys.key_hash
ALIGNED medium open PENDING by @EternalVision-AI · 2026-04-09T23:17:30Z → gardener comment 2 other
(3 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: medium · commit: ce5be85e

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

Adds uniqueIndex on agent_api_keys.key_hash to match the existing board_api_keys pattern. Migration is rollback-safe with a pre-flight duplicate check. Closes #2925. This is a direct alignment win with governance — API key collisions break actor identity, which cascades into mis-routed heartbeats and wrong audit-log attribution.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Agent identity | DB-level guarantee that no two agents share a key hash | [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md): "Agent API keys are hashed (plaintext shown once at creation)" + "Auditable everything" | ✅ Aligned |
| Schema symmetry | Matches board_api_keys which already uses uniqueIndex on key_hash | [engineering/database/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/database/NODE.md) | ✅ Aligned |
| Migration safety | Pre-flight PL/pgSQL guard, transactional, diagnostic-on-failure | No tree guidance on migration patterns — worth adding | 🆕 New |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [product/governance/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — API keys, audit trail
- [engineering/database/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/database/NODE.md) — schema conventions

### Recommendation

Merge after Greptile/CodeRabbit code review. Two tree-update suggestions:

1. Document the "hashed, unique, auditable" API key invariant in product/governance/NODE.md so future schema additions inherit the pattern automatically. The symmetry between agent_api_keys and board_api_keys should be a tree decision, not a convention discoverable only by reading the DB schema.
2. Consider documenting the "pre-flight PL/pgSQL guard + diagnostic query" migration pattern under engineering/database/NODE.md — this is a good defensive pattern that other risky migrations should follow.

> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

Server-side tenant-guards the PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID / wake snapshot on heartbeat runs and process-loss retries. This is load-bearing for the company-scoped isolation invariant — exactly the kind of fix the tree wants prioritized.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Tenant isolation | Sanitize cross-tenant wake context before runs + retries | Root CLAUDE.md: "Company-scoped isolation — All entities... scoped to company_id. Never leak data across companies." | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- Root [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/CLAUDE.md) — "Company-scoped isolation" (decision #1)

- [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) — heartbeat path

### Recommendation

Merge as a high-priority isolation fix after Greptile/CodeRabbit sign off on code correctness. Two non-blocking observations:

1. Consider a targeted test fixture for cross-tenant retry replay — the failure mode is process-loss retry carrying stale PAPERCLIP_TASK_ID, which is hard to reproduce without a fixture that simulates interrupted runs.
2. The PR body references internal PAY-114/PAY-158 and paperclip.internal. Not a product/tree concern — just flagging that external contributors reading the PR won't have context. A one-line summary of the actual failure mode (vs the ticket link) would help downstream reviewers.

---

(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: ea5485fb

Aligned with context tree. Fixes a PATCH hang when {status, comment} are combined by removing a redundant UPDATE issues SET updatedAt inside addComment() via a new skipUpdatedAt option. Matches [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) route/service split and the "Drizzle queries live in services" decision. The sidecar tweak to the openclaw-gateway skill template (two-step close flow) is also consistent with [adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md) — it hardens the adapter against similar contention by splitting the close into POST-then-PATCH. No product/context conflict.

PR #3199 fix: include routine_execution issues in agent inbox-lite
ALIGNED low closed AUTHOR WITHDRAWN by @googlarz · 2026-04-09T22:53:30Z → gardener comment 1 other
(1 comment(s) before gardener on this thread)

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: 4a8c8bae

Aligned with context tree. 2-line fix: adds includeRoutineExecutions: true to the inbox-lite query so agents actually see routine-execution issues they've been assigned. Closes #3090. Routine execution is part of the task system per [product/task-system/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) and the inbox is how agents discover work — this restores the intended visibility. Matches [engineering/backend/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/backend/NODE.md) route/service split. No product/context conflict.

PR #3245 fix: strip paperclip payload from agent params
ALIGNED low open PENDING by @jodok · 2026-04-09T22:42:25Z → gardener comment 3 other

🌱 gardener:verdict: ALIGNED · severity: low · commit: 5395100

> [!NOTE]
> Context Review — this checks product-context fit against the project's
> context tree, not code correctness. Run Greptile/CodeRabbit for code review.

### Summary

Hardens the OpenClaw gateway adapter so that agentParams.paperclip is always stripped before sending the gateway agent request, and moves wake context entirely into the message field. Removes the buildStandardPaperclipPayload function and the dead paperclipPayload root-level path. Updates tests to assert wake data is present in the message rather than in the now-removed paperclip root field. Adds a regression test that exercises the legacy payloadTemplate.paperclip strip path. Aligns with adapter architecture and docs-as-contract principles.

<details open>
<summary><strong>Context match</strong></summary>

| Area | Item intent | Tree guidance | Fit |
|------|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Adapter protocol boundary | Strip the paperclip root field that OpenClaw strictly rejects; wake context moves to message text | [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md): "Adapters do NOT define the transcript schema. They parse agent output INTO the shared TranscriptEntry types." Adapters should conform to what external gateways accept, not push internal structs outbound. | ✅ Aligned |
| Gateway adapter pattern | OpenClaw WS protocol requires strict param validation; removing an unknown root field is the correct fix | [adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md): "Transport: WebSocket only. Follows a structured protocol: challenge, connect, agent request, wait, event streaming." | ✅ Aligned |
| Surface problems, don't hide them | Rather than silently passing the paperclip field and hoping OpenClaw ignores it, the fix deletes the field unconditionally so failures surface at the message-contract level | Tree principle: "Surface problems, don't hide them. No silent auto-recovery." | ✅ Aligned |
| Docs sync | README updated to reflect actual outbound contract — wake context in message, no paperclip root field | Tree principle: docs should reflect actual contract, not intended-but-broken state. | ✅ Aligned |
| Regression test coverage | New test pins the strip behavior against persisted payloadTemplate.paperclip fixtures | The previous gardener recommendation was to add exactly this test. PR delivers it. | ✅ Aligned |
| Test update accuracy | heartbeat-comment-wake-batching.test.ts and openclaw-gateway-adapter.test.ts updated to match message-embedded wake data | Accurate test alignment — old assertions checked a field that now doesn't exist; new assertions check the same data in its correct location. | ✅ Aligned |
| Control plane, not execution plane | Removing the paperclipPayload that forwarded internal Paperclip IDs into the gateway request reinforces that Paperclip orchestrates; it doesn't serialize internal state into adapters | [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md): "Control plane, not execution plane. Paperclip never runs agent code directly." | ✅ Aligned |

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Tree nodes referenced</strong></summary>

- [adapters/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — adapter architecture, local vs gateway pattern, transcript schema ownership
- [adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md) — OpenClaw WS gateway adapter, protocol constraints
- [product/NODE.md](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — control plane identity, not execution plane

</details>

### Recommendation

Looks good to merge once code review clears. The previous recommendation to add a regression test for persisted payloadTemplate.paperclip fixtures has been addressed in this version. No new context concerns.

---
